title: [redesign/phase-3a] Presets + foundation: Modal, useShortcut (hand-rolled), useLocalStorage, UIContext, forwardRef StlViewer, SCAD @preset grammar
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-r1qt
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T23:29:19Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

**Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 3
**Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-23-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-3-plan.md — read this first; it documents the architectural decisions and cut-list from plan_review (DHH, Kieran-TS, Simplicity)
**Blocks**: st-<3b>, st-<3c> (filed concurrently; sling after this merges)

Biggest phase-3 bead. Lands the shared primitives + presets together (per DHH's "infrastructure lands with its first consumer" rule). Every decision below was validated in plan_review — do not re-derive.

## Scope

### A. Shared Modal + portal

- `components/Modal.tsx` — `createPortal` to `#modal-root`, Escape + backdrop close, autofocus first focusable, focus restore on close, Tab/Shift-Tab trap (INLINED in Modal.tsx — no separate `useFocusTrap.ts`), `className` prop for width (not `widthPx`). See plan D1.
- `app/layout.tsx` — add `<div id="modal-root" />` after `<AppShell>` inside `<body>`.
- **Migrate `ErrorLogModal`** (currently inline in `ViewerChrome.tsx:475–527`) to use the shared `<Modal>`. Behaviorally identical modulo gaining the focus trap. Reads `modal.kind === "errorLog"` from UIContext.

### B. Hand-rolled `useShortcut` (NO tinykeys)

- `hooks/useShortcut.ts` — window-level `keydown` listener, `$mod = e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey` resolution, chord parser splits on `+`, input-field guard (skip bare keys in INPUT/SELECT/TEXTAREA, always fire modifier chords).
- **Typed `Binding` union** — const tuple of allowed bindings + `typeof[number]`. See plan D2. Catches typos and lets `ShortcutSheet` (3b) hand-enumerate without drift.
- Signature:
  ```ts
  function useShortcut(
    binding: Binding,
    handler: (e: KeyboardEvent) => void,
    opts?: { enabled?: boolean; preventDefault?: boolean },
  ): void;
  ```

### C. `useLocalStorage` + v1 version prefix

- `hooks/useLocalStorage.ts` — SSR-safe (useState fallback, useEffect reads on client).
- Three keys used in phase 3, each with `stuff.v1.` prefix:
  - `stuff.v1.presets.<slug>` (user presets)
  - `stuff.v1.lastParams.<slug>` (last-edited params)
  - `stuff.v1.recent.models` (3b uses this — set up the hook; 3b consumes)
- No `lib/storage.ts` accessor module — three keys isn't enough to justify indirection. Keys at call sites.

### D. UIContext — reducer-driven single-slot

- `contexts/UIContext.tsx`:
  ```ts
  type Modal =
    | { kind: "none" }
    | { kind: "palette" }
    | { kind: "share" }
    | { kind: "errorLog" }
    | { kind: "shortcutSheet" };
  type UIAction = { type: "open"; modal: Modal } | { type: "close" };
  function useUI(): { modal: Modal; dispatch: (a: UIAction) => void };
  ```
- `<UIProvider>` wraps AppShell children in `app/layout.tsx`.
- Consumers: `modal.kind === "palette"` to read, `dispatch({ type: "open", modal: { kind: "palette" } })` to open.

### E. `StlViewer` forwardRef + dev-only escape hatch shim

- `components/StlViewer.tsx` migrates to:
  ```tsx
  export interface StlViewerHandle {
    camera: PerspectiveCamera;
    controls: OrbitControls;
    setCameraPreset(preset: "top" | "front" | "iso"): void;
    resetCamera(): void;  // NEW in phase 3
  }
  export const StlViewer = forwardRef<StlViewerHandle, Props>(...);
  ```
- Dev-only useEffect keeps the `canvas.__stlViewer = handleRef.current` shim so `preview-controls.spec.ts` still reads it. `process.env.NODE_ENV === "production"` early-returns — shim never ships to prod.
- `ViewerChrome` reads the ref to invoke `setCameraPreset` / `resetCamera`. No more `canvas.__stlViewer` calls in app code.

### F. Viewer keys migrate to `useShortcut`

- Delete `ViewerChrome.handleKeyDown`.
- Replace with `useShortcut("1", ...)`, `("2", ...)`, `("3", ...)`, `("g", toggleGrid)`, `("d", toggleDims)`, `("f", toggleFullscreen)`, `("r", resetCamera)`, `("Enter", onRefresh)` at the top of ViewerChrome.
- Gate each via `{ enabled: modal.kind === "none" && focusInViewer }`. Track `focusInViewer` with `onFocus/onBlur` on the `<section>`.
- `R` reset camera is NEW — wire `resetCamera` on the StlViewer ref.

### G. SCAD `@preset` grammar + seed stock presets

- `lib/scad-params/parse.ts` — extend to parse `@preset` lines:
  ```scad
  // @preset id="42mm-cylinder" label="42mm cylinder" can_diameter=42 clearance=0.25 ...
  ```
  Reuse the existing `key=value` attribute parser. Return `Preset[]` alongside `Param[]` from `parseScad(source)`. Coerce values to the declared param's kind at parse time; fail parse on type mismatch.
- **Non-optional `shortKey` post-parse**: if `@param` lacks `short=`, parser defaults `shortKey = name`. Validate all shortKeys in a file are unique; throw on collision.
- Seed stock presets on all three models:
  - `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` — the four historic presets documented at lines 10–19 (42mm, 46mm, 70mm, 77mm) become real `@preset` lines.
  - `popcorn_kernel.scad` — one "default" preset.
  - `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` — one "stock" preset with defaults.
- Add `short=` to every `@param` in all three models (prefer 1–3 lowercase letters per param; disambiguate when multiple params share an initial letter).

### H. `hooks/useDetailState` — preset binding

- Add fields:
  ```ts
  activePresetId: string | null;
  modified: boolean;
  ```
  (NOT a discriminated union — two states don't warrant one. Documented in plan D6.)
- Add mutators:
  - `loadPreset(id)` — replaces all params in one setState, sets `activePresetId`, `modified = false`.
  - `saveAsPreset(label)` — captures current `params` + pushes to `stuff.v1.presets.<slug>`.
- On every `setParam`: recompute `modified = !paramsEqual(params, presetValues(activePresetId))`.
- Inline `paramsEqual(a, b, paramDefs)` at the bottom of `useDetailState.ts`, ~10 lines. Plain `===` per-param. No float-tolerance — add when a test actually fails.

### I. Preset list UI in `DetailLeftRail`

- Replace phase-1 stub with a real preset list.
- Each row: name + modified-dot (rendered only when `activePresetId === preset.id && modified`) + (for user presets) delete button.
- Click row → `loadPreset(row.id)`.
- **Inline save-row** at top of list — appears when `⌘S` pressed or "+ Save as preset" button clicked. Shows a text input + Save button; Enter saves, Escape cancels. NO SavePresetModal component — inline is simpler and has no modal hop.
- Shortcuts: `⌘1`–`⌘9` load preset by index (stock presets first, then user; truncate to 9). `⌘S` activates the inline save-row.

### J. `⌘E` download STL shortcut

- Wire `⌘E` via `useShortcut` to the existing `DownloadButton`'s `handleDownload`. Works from anywhere on the detail page.

### K. CI invariants: preset validity

- `scripts/check-invariants.py` — new built-in check: every `@preset` key matches a declared `@param`, values are in-kind. Warn-don't-fail on out-of-range values (phase 1 allows typed out-of-range). Fail-loud on unknown param keys.
- Test fixtures for the invariant.

## Acceptance

- All three models show real preset lists in the left rail.
- Click a preset → all params replace; modified-dot appears on first edit.
- `⌘S` activates inline save-row; typing a name + Enter persists to localStorage.
- `⌘1`–`⌘9` load preset by index.
- `⌘E` downloads STL from anywhere on detail page.
- `ErrorLogModal` still works (Escape, backdrop, now also Tab trap).
- Viewer keys (1/2/3/G/D/F/Enter/R) work via `useShortcut`; suppressed when any modal is open.
- `preview-controls.spec.ts` still passes — `canvas.__stlViewer` dev shim keeps the E2E path working.
- New unit tests: `useShortcut` chord detection, `useLocalStorage` SSR fallback, `paramsEqual` edge cases, `@preset` parser.
- `scripts/check-invariants.py` fails on a preset with an unknown param key; passes on seeded presets.
- New E2E `tests/e2e/presets.spec.ts` covers load/save/modified-dot/delete flows.
- All existing E2E + vitest green.
- `npm run build` green; `grep __stlViewer .next/` returns zero hits (prod shim excluded).

## Do not

- Add the `tinykeys` dependency. Hand-roll. Plan_review killed the dep.
- Ship sidecar `<stem>.presets.json` files. Presets are inline in `.scad` via `@preset`.
- Introduce `lib/storage.ts` or `lib/share/diff.ts` as separate files. Keys and `paramsEqual` are inline.
- Build a `SavePresetModal` component. Inline save-row in the preset list.
- Use `widthPx: number` on Modal. Consumer owns width via `className`.
- Keep extending `canvas.__stlViewer` as a production code path. forwardRef now; shim is dev-only.
- Turn `PresetBinding` into a discriminated union. Two states = nullable pair.

## References

- docs/plans/2026-04-23-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-3-plan.md — full architectural context
- docs/design/caliper/README.md § "Presets", "Keyboard Shortcuts"
- components/ViewerChrome.tsx:475–527 (ErrorLogModal to migrate)
- components/StlViewer.tsx (forwardRef migration target)
- lib/scad-params/parse.ts:194 (existing attribute parser — extend for @preset)
- cylindrical_holder_slot.scad:10–19 (historic preset prose to convert to @preset lines)

title: [redesign/phase-2b] Bbox dimensions in RenderResult + stat strip display + loading/empty state polish
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-9g22 attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-23T22:05:55Z attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: base_branch=main **Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 2 **Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "Future phases → Phase 2" **Parallelizable with**: phase-2a. No hard dependency. Phase 1 stat strip stubs dimensions with em-dash. This bead computes the bbox, fills `RenderResult.dimensions`, displays in the stat strip, and polishes the loading + empty states that phase 1 left as thin placeholders. ## Scope ### 1. Bbox computation After a successful WASM render, compute the STL's bounding box. Options: **A.** Parse the STL bytes client-side. STL binary format: 80-byte header, 4-byte triangle count, then 50 bytes per triangle (12 floats = 3 vertices × 3 coords + a 3-float normal + a 2-byte attribute). Iterate all triangles, track min/max per axis. Roughly 50 lines. No dependency. **B.** Use `three`'s existing `STLLoader` + `Box3` — already imported in `StlViewer`. Import the loader into `useRenderer` (or a shared helper) and compute bbox from the Three.js geometry. **C.** Run a secondary OpenSCAD pass with the `--info` flag which dumps geometry stats. Heavier; skip. **Prefer A** — pure function, no three.js coupling in the renderer, fastest. Wire into `useRenderer`: ```ts // hooks/useRenderer.ts — extend RenderResult type RenderResult = { stlBytes: Uint8Array; triCount: number; ms: number; dimensions: { x: number; y: number; z: number }; // now required (was optional) }; ``` Compute dimensions inline after the render resolves, before dispatching the `ready` state. Dimensions in mm, rounded to 1 decimal (e.g. `213.5`, `157.5`, `253.0`). Add `hooks/useRenderer.test.ts` case: given a known STL fixture (use an existing `exports/.stl` from CI), `useRenderer`'s result has the expected bbox within ±0.1mm. ### 2. Stat strip displays dimensions `components/ViewerChrome.tsx` stat strip currently shows tri count · render ms · STL size. Add dimensions as the first field: ``` 213.5 × 157.5 × 253.0 mm · 27,756 tris · 421 ms · 48.3 kb ``` Format: `W × D × H mm` with 1 decimal, `×` is a real × symbol (not `x`), mm unit suffix. Comma-separated thousand-separators on `triCount` and `sizeBytes` (already may or may not be — verify and add if missing). ### 3. Loading state polish Per `docs/design/caliper/README.md` § "Loading" (line ~56): > **Loading:** Viewer shows render progress bar + compile log streaming in the bottom stat strip. Param rail stays interactive. Implementation scope for this bead: - **Progress bar** — a thin (2px) animated bar at the bottom of the viewer container, above the stat strip. Infinite indeterminate animation via CSS (no progress data is actually available from WASM — it's indeterminate): ```tsx {state.kind === 'loading' && (
)} ``` Define `@keyframes caliper-slide { 0%, 100% { transform: translateX(-100%); } 50% { transform: translateX(300%); } }` in `app/globals.css`. - **Compile-log streaming in stat strip** — when loading, the stat strip's last-render text (`N ms · N kb`) becomes a single-line "compiling..." or the last stderr line (if WASM is emitting progress text; otherwise just "compiling..."). Don't try to genuinely stream stderr — WASM emits all-at-once in most cases. Just replace the stat strip copy with "compiling..." in dim text while loading. - **Param rail stays interactive** — already true in phase 1 (there's no disable). Just verify. ### 4. Empty / idle state polish Per spec § "Empty / idle": > **Empty / idle:** Before first render of a fresh model — blank viewer with a small "Press ⏎ to render" hint and disabled export button. Currently phase 1 shows `idle`. Replace: ```tsx {state.kind === "idle" && (
no render yet
press ⏎ to render
)} ``` And the "Download STL" button: disabled when `state.kind !== 'ready'`: ```tsx ``` (Already may partly be the case — verify via current prop and enforce.) Wire **Enter** key: when focus is within the viewer `
` (tabIndex=0) AND `state.kind === 'idle'`, pressing Enter triggers the first render. Extend the existing `handleKeyDown` in `ViewerChrome.tsx`: ```ts if (e.key === "Enter" && state.kind === "idle") { render.refresh(); // useRenderer already exposes refresh } ``` `render.refresh()` already exists (phase 1b added it for phase-2 use) — just wire it. Pass `refresh` down as a prop if ViewerChrome doesn't have it yet. ### 5. E2E Extend existing specs, don't create a new file per state: - `tests/e2e/viewer-chrome.spec.ts` — add: - Stat strip shows `\d+(\.\d+)?\s*×\s*\d+(\.\d+)?\s*×\s*\d+(\.\d+)?\s*mm` after a render completes. - When no render has run (fresh page load), the empty-state shows "press ⏎ to render" and Download STL button is disabled. - Pressing Enter on the viewer triggers a render (assert transition out of idle). - `tests/e2e/live-preview.spec.ts` — extend the existing render-text regex check to include dimensions, e.g. `/\d+(\.\d+)?\s*×\s*\d+(\.\d+)?\s*×\s*\d+(\.\d+)?\s*mm · \d+(,\d{3})* tris · \d+ ms · [\d.]+kb/`. ### 6. Update `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` invariant check `scripts/check-invariants.py`'s built-in `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX ± 1mm` check should keep passing — since now we're showing dimensions in the UI and the anchor is the claim, the user can visually compare. No code change to the invariant itself; just ensure this bead doesn't accidentally change what `bbox` means (must remain "dimensions in mm, per-axis extent"). ## Acceptance - `RenderResult.dimensions` always populated on `ready` state (no longer optional, no em-dash fallback in UI). - Stat strip shows `W × D × H mm · tris · ms · kb` format on ready; "compiling..." on loading. - Loading state shows an indeterminate progress bar. - Idle state shows "press ⏎ to render" hint + disabled Download STL button; Enter triggers render. - E2E extended; all green. Unit test for bbox computation passes against known fixture STL. - Next.js build + tsc --noEmit clean. ## Do not - Display the compile log live-streaming in the idle state's viewer (phase-2a handles logs in error context; loading has "compiling..." only). - Implement compile-error line parsing (phase-2a). - Add animation libraries. - Change the `useRenderer` return shape beyond `dimensions` becoming required. - Add per-axis labels to dimensions text ("width: 213.5mm, depth: 157.5mm...") — the `W × D × H` compact form is the spec. ## References - docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "Render pipeline contract" (dimensions optional for phase 1; required in phase 2) - docs/design/caliper/README.md § "Loading / Error / Empty" (state contract), § "Model detail (desktop) → stat strip" - docs/design/caliper/screenshots/05-state-loading.png, 07-state-empty.png (pixel refs) - STL binary format: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/STL_(file_format)#Binary_STL - hooks/useRenderer.ts (current RenderResult shape) - components/ViewerChrome.tsx (stat strip + state branches)

title: [redesign/phase-2a] Compile-error line parsing + error-strip UI on last-good render
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-gii5 attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-23T21:59:26Z attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: base_branch=main **Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 2 **Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "Future phases → Phase 2" **Parallelizable with**: phase-2b. No hard dependency. Phase 1 stubbed `RenderError.line = null` everywhere. This bead fills it by parsing OpenSCAD's stderr/compile log for line numbers, and ships the error-state UI per spec: last-good render dimmed behind, error strip sliding in over the stat bar, "view full log" action. ## Scope ### 1. Error-line parsing — `lib/wasm/render-error.ts` (or extend existing render.ts) OpenSCAD error messages have predictable shapes: ``` ERROR: Parser error in file "input.scad", line 42: syntax error WARNING: ... ERROR: Ignoring unknown function 'missing()', in file input.scad, line 17 ERROR: Unterminated multi-line comment ``` Parse: - **line number** — match `line (\d+)` (case-insensitive), take first match in the first `ERROR:` block. - **message** — the rest of the first `ERROR:` line, trimmed. - **log** — the full stderr block verbatim (already captured — phase 1 stubs it in `RenderError.log`). - Fall back to `line = null` when no line number parseable (e.g. "Unterminated multi-line comment" has no line). UI must render cleanly when line is null. Add a unit test file `lib/wasm/render-error.test.ts` with 4-5 fixtures: - Parser error with line number - Ignored-function error with line number - Unterminated-comment (no line number) - Error with multiple WARNING lines before it - Empty stderr (no error — should not be called, but defensive: returns null). ### 2. Wire into `useRenderer` `hooks/useRenderer.ts` currently returns `state.kind === 'error'` with `{ line: null, message, log }`. Replace the `null` with the parsed line number when available. The existing smoke test for token-cancel stays green. Add one test case: when OpenSCAD returns an error stderr, `renderError.line` is populated correctly. ### 3. Error-strip UI in `` Per `docs/design/caliper/README.md` § "Loading / Error / Empty" (lines ~54–58): > **Error:** Viewer shows the last good render dimmed; an error strip slides in over the stat bar with the first compiler error, line number, and a "view full log" action. Implementation: - **Last-good-render dimming** — when `state.kind === 'error'`, keep rendering the `` with the last successful STL (source from `render.history[history.length-1]?.stlBytes`), but overlay a `bg-bg/70` translucent layer to dim it. If `history` is empty (error on first render), show a plain empty state with just the error strip. - **Error strip** — absolutely positioned at the viewer bottom, above the existing 36px stat strip (or replacing it visually while in error state — pick whichever reads cleaner against the spec screenshot). Shape: ```tsx
error {state.error.line != null && ( line {state.error.line} )} {state.error.message}
``` Use Tailwind tokens only (no hex). `bg-red/10` assumes v4 supports that opacity syntax; if not, use an `rgba` via inline `style={{ background: "color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-red) 10%, transparent)" }}` — or add a `--color-red-tint` token in `app/globals.css`. - **"view full log" modal** — clicking the button opens a dialog over the viewer showing `state.error.log` verbatim in a `
`. Modal uses the Caliper radius `rounded-6` + shadow `shadow-[0_20px_60px_rgba(0,0,0,0.55)]` per spec § "Shadows: only on modals". Close on Escape or backdrop click. State is component-local (`const [logOpen, setLogOpen] = useState(false)`) — no global modal system.

- **Slide animation** — spec says "slides in". Phase-2 scope: use a CSS transition on `translate-y` (from `translate-y-full` when `state.kind !== 'error'` to `translate-y-0` when error). Standard 180ms ease-out. Don't pull in framer-motion.

### 4. E2E

Add `tests/e2e/error-state.spec.ts`:

- Given a model with a deliberately-broken param combo (e.g. negative `can_diameter` if the model has validation that triggers an OpenSCAD error on that, or inject a syntax error into a temp fixture model), render, assert:
  - The error strip appears.
  - The error message is displayed.
  - If the error has a line number, the line number is shown.
  - The "view full log" button is clickable and opens a dialog containing the full error log.
  - Closing the dialog (Escape or backdrop click) dismisses it.
- Ensure the existing render-log regex (`/\d+ms · [\d.]+kb/`) remains untouched when an error occurs — the stat strip's error-state copy is the error strip, not the normal strip.

If a deliberately-broken input isn't straightforward, add a test-only fixture `tests/fixtures/broken_syntax.scad` with a syntax error, point a test-only route to it.

## Acceptance

- OpenSCAD errors surface with line number (when available) and message in the UI.
- Last-good render dims behind the error strip when one exists; clean empty state when error occurs on first render.
- Error log modal opens on "view full log", closes on Escape or backdrop click.
- All existing E2E + vitest green. New E2E + unit tests pass.
- Zero inline styles except for any `color-mix` fallback as noted.
- Next.js build + tsc --noEmit clean.

## Do not

- Implement the loading progress bar (that's phase-2b).
- Implement the "Press ⏎ to render" empty hint (phase-2b).
- Add bbox / dimensions to `RenderResult` (phase-2b).
- Introduce a global modal registry — the log modal is local to ViewerChrome.
- Pull in animation libraries — Tailwind transitions are enough.
- Change the `RenderError` type shape beyond filling `line` (the field already exists).

## References

- docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "D2" (useRenderer contract) and § "Future phases → Phase 2"
- docs/design/caliper/README.md § "Loading / Error / Empty" (spec), § "Design Tokens → Colors" (red token), § "Shadows"
- docs/design/caliper/screenshots/06-state-error.png (pixel reference)
- hooks/useRenderer.ts (current state machine; `RenderError.line` currently null)
- components/ViewerChrome.tsx (target of UI changes)
- lib/wasm/render.ts or equivalent (stderr source)
close_reason: no-changes: already landed as b33aa0f on main (feat: compile-error line parsing + error-strip UI). Parser, useRenderer wire-in, error-strip UI, full-log modal, and unit/component tests all present on origin/main. Worktree was stale on polecat/obsidian/st-v7k.

title: [bug] Model titles show 'SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0' instead of model name (regression from st-871 license headers)
[bug] Restore handle arch Y-face fillet with manifold-safe geometry (st-hnd regression reverted by st-so7)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-2hho
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T15:58:47Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Context

**st-hnd** (commit d801e9a) originally fixed the post→arch fillet discontinuity by sweeping a rounded rectangle (`path_sweep`) along a semicircular path. Visually that closed the seam. But the new invariants system from **st-cjn** (a4b88ce) caught that the path_sweep output was **non-watertight** — the sweep endpoint caps didn't close into a manifold solid.

**st-so7** (41302e1, "fillet base top-rim edges") silently reverted st-hnd's arch code back to the pre-fillet `linear_extrude(handle_arch_2d())` form so the invariants would pass. This was out of scope for st-so7 — that bead should have just done the base fillet and filed this follow-up. The revert resolved watertight but the user-visible arc regression is back.

**Current state on main:** base-plate top rim is filleted (st-so7 ✓); handle-arch Y-face edges are sharp again (regression).

## Goal

Re-implement the st-hnd fillet intent **without** breaking watertight. Arch must be a closed manifold solid AND its Y-face edges must carry the same `fillet_r` as the post's Z-edge fillet so the post→arch junction is continuous in preview.

## Approaches to try (in rough order of likely manifold-safety)

Try these in order; first one that passes all checks wins. Document which you picked and why in the commit message.

### 1. BOSL2 `rotate_extrude` on a filleted 2D profile

Construct a 2D cross-section in XZ that is the semi-annular arch profile with rounded Y-face edges baked in (a rect with rounding, subtracted from another rect), then `rotate_extrude(angle=180, ...)` around an axis. Rotate-extrude produces a closed solid by construction. Challenge: the Y-face fillet lives along the sweep direction, not the profile — may not be expressible in a pure profile. If this limits the shape in a way that visually misses the point, skip.

### 2. BOSL2 `sweep()` / `path_sweep()` with explicit `caps=true` or endpoint-closing disks

`path_sweep` in newer BOSL2 supports `caps=true`. If the version bundled in `libs/BOSL2/` supports it, add it. Failing that: generate the sweep as-is, then `union()` it with two filleted end-cap disks at θ=0 and θ=180 positioned where the sweep endpoints sit. Verify watertight with trimesh.

### 3. Hand-built `torus`-with-rounded-section

BOSL2 has `torus(r_maj, r_min)` for round-section tori. That gives a closed manifold for free but the section is a circle, not a rounded rectangle — loses the rectangle's flat Y-faces. If we also want flat Y-faces with rounded Y-edges, construct the arch as a union of:
- An outer annular prism (linear_extrude of the semi-annular XZ profile along Y by handle_thickness — this is what main has today),
- Plus a filleted "rim" on each of the two Y-faces (two rounded-edge tori or swept rounded strips along the semi-circle path on the Y=+handle_thickness/2 and Y=−handle_thickness/2 planes).

Each piece is closed → the union is watertight. This is essentially "add a fillet bead" on top of the existing sharp-Y-face arch.

### 4. Boolean fillet via BOSL2 `offset_sweep` or `fillet` primitives

If BOSL2 exposes a primitive that applies a fillet to an edge of an existing solid (check `libs/BOSL2/rounding.scad` for `offset_sweep`, `edge_profile`, `edge_mask`), use it on the existing sharp arch. This is the most general but BOSL2's edge-profile tools on curved surfaces may not work on the arch's topology.

## Acceptance

- Handle arch Y-face edges visibly filleted with radius matching `fillet_r` at the post→arch junction. Continuous with the post's Z-edge fillet (same visual test st-hnd originally satisfied).
- `python3 scripts/check-invariants.py spraycan_carrier_6x50mm` exits 0. Specifically: watertight ✓, `connected_solids == 1` ✓, triangle count sane, bbox within PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX ± 1mm.
- `python3 scripts/check-invariants-all.py` green (no regression elsewhere).
- All E2E + vitest green.
- CI render thumbnails show the fillet — inspect the iso and side PNGs.
- Update the spraycan header comment (lines ~21–30) to accurately describe the final solution. Restore the "Y-face edges are filleted" claim only once the fix is landed AND invariant-passing.
- Commit message cites: reverts the silent revert from st-so7, restores st-hnd's intent with manifold-safe approach X (whichever of 1–4 worked).

## Process note

st-so7 bundled this revert into an unrelated bead. The right process was: land st-so7's base-fillet change without touching the arch; file THIS bead as the follow-up; iterate on the manifold-safe approach separately. See st-prc (process bead) landing alongside.

## Do not

- Accept a non-watertight STL. Watertight is load-bearing for slicer compatibility.
- Revert st-so7's base-plate top-rim fillet in the process.
- Weaken or delete the watertight invariant to make a broken fix pass.
- Modify anything in `libs/BOSL2/`.

## References

- st-hnd commit (the original fillet fix, with the watertight bug): d801e9a
- st-so7 commit (the silent revert + base fillet): 41302e1
- st-cjn commit (the invariants system that caught the bug): a4b88ce
- models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad lines ~217–265 (handle() and the arch module); header comment ~21–30
- libs/BOSL2/rounding.scad, libs/BOSL2/skin.scad (path_sweep, offset_sweep)
- docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md § "st-hnd... visual continuity class — no idiom short of structural rewrite"
close_reason: no-changes: already landed as b855a35 on main (fix: restore arch Y-face fillet with rotate_extrude manifold). Approach 1 (BOSL2 rotate_extrude on a filleted rect profile) is in models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad handle() at lines ~252-258; header comment updated; invariants + tests all green per the commit message.

title: [bug] Detail page grows past viewport when param list is long — pin viewer to viewport and scroll param rail internally
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-x6ca attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-23T15:29:13Z attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: base_branch=main ## Symptom On `/models/` at ≥1200px, models with many params push the page height past the viewport. The whole page scrolls, so the 3D viewer (which is the thing the user is watching while they tweak) disappears above the fold. Param rail is long → user scrolls page to reach params → viewer is offscreen → they can't see the effect of their change without scrolling back up. Expected: the viewer stays pinned within the visible area, the param rail (and left rail) scroll internally within their own columns. ## Root cause No viewport-height constraint on the detail layout: - `components/AppShell.tsx` — root is `min-h-screen`, top bar is `sticky h-38`. Good (top bar stays), but the main below has no max height. - `components/DetailPage.tsx` line ~42–58 — outer wrapper is `flex flex-col`, then `DetailHeader` natural height, then the `grid` has no height constraint. Grid cells grow to content height → long ParamRail grows the grid → grid grows the page. - `
` wrapping `ParamRail` (~67) has no `overflow-y`. - Same for `DetailLeftRail` — render log could also exceed viewport. ## Fix direction Make DetailPage pin to `calc(100vh - 38px)` (viewport minus sticky top bar), lay out as a flex column so the grid row fills remaining space, give the left rail + param rail `overflow-y: auto`, let the viewer column stay `overflow-hidden` (ViewerChrome already fills via its own flex). Approximate shape: ```tsx // DetailPage.tsx return (
); ``` The `min-h-0` on flex/grid children is load-bearing — without it, children refuse to shrink below their content size and the outer constraint becomes moot. Also: verify the `` `
`'s `min-h-[480px]` on the inner viewer container (`components/ViewerChrome.tsx`) doesn't force 480px when the viewport is shorter. If it does, change to `min-h-0 flex-1` and let the viewer fill whatever the column gives it. The ResizeObserver already handles dynamic sizing. ### Mobile / < 1200px (stacked grid) Below 1200px the grid collapses to 1 column. Phase 4 owns the real mobile bottom-sheet design. For this bead: at stacked-mode, **keep the current natural-scroll behavior** (don't try to pin the viewer there — the 3-col invariant doesn't exist below xl). Scope this fix to `min-[1200px]:` and above. Add a comment in the JSX that says so. ## Acceptance - At 1440×860 viewport with `popcorn_kernel` (or a synthetic model with ≥30 params if none are long enough): param rail scrolls internally; viewer stays visible; page body does not scroll. - At 1200×800 (low-edge xl): same behavior. - At 1199×800 (just below xl): grid stacks as before; page scrolls naturally; no regression. - `tests/e2e/mobile-smoke.spec.ts` at 390×844 still green (stacked mode unchanged). - Add to `tests/e2e/param-rail.spec.ts` or create `tests/e2e/detail-layout.spec.ts`: at 1280×800 with an artificially long param list (e.g. the cylindrical_holder_slot model), assert `document.documentElement.scrollTop` does not change when scrolling inside the param rail (or equivalent — the aside has an internal scrollbar, the document does not). - Resize smoke: resizing the window from 1400 to 1250 keeps the viewer visible and the rail scrolling; no layout shift of the top bar. ## Do not - Change `AppShell` structure. The fix is scoped to DetailPage. - Touch the sticky top-bar behavior or its 38px height. - Redesign the mobile-stacked behavior (phase 4). - Introduce a CSS variable for the 38px offset — just use the literal `calc(100vh - 38px)` for now. If the top bar height ever changes we'll sweep call sites. ## References - components/DetailPage.tsx line ~42–58 (grid root), ~66–71 (aside around ParamRail) - components/AppShell.tsx (top bar 38px height, `min-h-screen` on root) - components/ViewerChrome.tsx (`min-h-[480px]` on inner viewer — may need to relax) - Introduced by st-boa (commit bb6a4cb)

title: [bug] Grid overlay renders on top of the 3D model in ViewerChrome (should be a background)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-5692 attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-23T15:12:08Z attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: base_branch=main ## Symptom User reports: in `/models/`, toggling the grid (G key or it being on by default) renders the CAD grid pattern on top of the 3D model, not behind it. Distracting and not the design intent — the grid should read as a subtle background the model occludes. ## Root cause `components/ViewerChrome.tsx` JSX order in the viewer container (around lines 88–104 of the merged `bb6a4cb`): ```tsx
{state.kind === "ready" && } {state.kind === "loading" && rendering…} ... {showGrid && } // ← rendered AFTER StlViewer — paints on top // ← correct: UI chrome on top // ← correct: UI chrome on top ...
``` Both `StlViewer` and `GridOverlay` use `absolute inset-0`. Later siblings win in the stacking context → grid sits above the canvas. Separately, even if order is fixed, the Three.js renderer's clear color may be opaque (`bg-panel2` equivalent), which would hide the grid entirely. Need to verify and likely set the renderer to clear with alpha so the grid shows through around the model. ## Fix direction Two-part fix: 1. **JSX order** — move `` to render BEFORE any StlViewer/placeholder in the viewer container div. AxesIndicator and ViewPresetTabs stay where they are (they're intentional top-layer UI). Approximate: ```tsx
{showGrid && } // first — behind {state.kind === "ready" && } {/* ...other state.kind branches... */} // on top // on top {showDims && }
``` 2. **Canvas clear color** — check `components/StlViewer.tsx` around the renderer init (`bootstrapScene`, lines ~67–180). Confirm `renderer.setClearColor(…, alpha)` uses alpha 0 (transparent) so the grid shows through WHERE there is no model. Three.js default is 0 alpha when the canvas has a transparent background, but the renderer options need `alpha: true` on `new WebGLRenderer({ alpha: true, antialias: true, ... })`. If it's currently opaque, flip it. Alternative (if the clear-alpha change is risky): ship #1 only + lower the grid's stroke colors (already on `stroke-line-soft` and `stroke-line` which are subtle — should be OK) so if clear color turns out opaque and grid remains above for any reason (e.g. the canvas gets moved back to top later), it's at least visually non-intrusive. But goal is #1+#2 done properly — grid reads as a background. ## Acceptance - Toggling `G` (or initial `showGrid=true` if that's the default) shows the grid underneath the model. The model fully occludes grid where it covers. - Turning grid off still works (no artifacts). - `tests/e2e/viewer-chrome.spec.ts` still green. Consider adding a visual assertion: `data-testid="viewer-grid"` on the GridOverlay SVG + assert it has a lower `z-index` / DOM order than the canvas (or at least exists before it in the container's children). - No regression of the ResizeObserver (`StlViewer.tsx:31`) — don't restructure the container beyond the direct children reorder. - Pixel parity with `docs/design/caliper/screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png` where the grid is visible behind the model. ## Do not - Remove the grid feature. - Touch the `showGrid` state or the `G` keyboard shortcut. - Restructure `ViewerChrome` beyond the grid reorder + the StlViewer clear-alpha change. ## References - components/ViewerChrome.tsx (lines around 88–104 per merged bb6a4cb) - components/StlViewer.tsx (renderer init for clearColor/alpha) - docs/design/caliper/screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png (design reference — grid as background) - docs/design/caliper/design_files/caliper.jsx:432–447 (reference grid SVG technique — it's used as an overlay in the reference too, so the clear-alpha fix is the essential piece) - Introduced by st-boa (commit bb6a4cb)

title: [redesign/phase-1c] Detail chrome — viewer overlays + grouped param rail
description:
**Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 1
**Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md (bead 1.c section)
**Depends on**: st-b5s (MERGED as 24bc513). You can start immediately.

**SCOPE UPDATED 2026-04-23** — added E2E hardening + mobile smoke. See § Testing below.

Fills in the viewer chrome and grouped param rail per docs/design/caliper/screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png. Both live on `DetailPage` and wire into `useDetailState` + `useRenderer` from 1.b.

## Scope — viewer chrome

Expand the 1.b `<ViewerChrome>` placeholder:

- **CAD grid overlay** — inline SVG `<pattern>` in a `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` div. Toggles on `showGrid` from `useDetailState`.
- **X/Y/Z axes indicator** — bottom-left inline SVG ~56×56px, labels `font-mono text-10 text-text-mute`. Rotates with `camera`.
- **View-preset tabs** — top-right pills for `top / front / iso / fullscreen` in `font-mono text-10 uppercase tracking-wide`. Active pill `bg-accent-soft border-accent-line`. Fullscreen uses the native Fullscreen API on the viewer container.
- **36px stat strip** — bottom, `h-36 border-t border-line bg-panel flex items-center px-12 gap-18 font-mono text-10`. Fields: tri count · render ms · STL size (`state.kind === 'ready' ? (state.result.stlBytes.byteLength / 1024).toFixed(1) + 'kb' : '—'`). **No dimensions** — phase 2 adds when bbox is computed.
- **Extend the `__stlViewer` debug handle** at `components/StlViewer.tsx:95` with `setCameraPreset(preset: 'top'|'front'|'iso')`. Do NOT replace the existing `{camera, controls}` shape — only add methods. `<ViewerChrome>` calls it on camera tab change.
- **Viewer-scoped keyboard** — `onKeyDown` on the `<section tabIndex={0}>`: `1/2/3` → camera top/front/iso; `G` → toggle grid; `D` → toggle dims; `F` → fullscreen. **No `R` reset-camera** (phase 3 global shortcut map handles reset).

Overlays are `absolute` siblings of the viewer canvas inside a `relative` container — do not break `StlViewer.tsx:31`'s ResizeObserver.

## Scope — param rail

Replace 1.b's `<ParamRail>` (currently a thin wrapper around `<ParamForm>`) with the real grouped rail:

- **Group by** `param.group`. Ungrouped bucket displayed last. Order within each `.scad` = first-occurrence order.
- **Group header** — `font-mono text-10 uppercase tracking-wide text-text-dim`, chevron, collapsible. Local `open` state per group, defaults open.
- **`components/ParamRow.tsx`** — single component switching on `param.kind`:
  - Number: label on left; unit `font-mono text-10 text-text-mute`; numeric input (`<input type="number" id={`param-${param.name}`}>`); slider `<input type="range">` below; min/max ticks `font-mono text-10 text-text-mute`. Both inputs controlled, both call the same `onChange(name, value)`. **Typing out-of-range is accepted — do not clamp**. Slider hides when `min` or `max` undefined; numeric input stays.
  - Boolean: label on left; pill toggle on right (`<button role="switch">`, tokenized accent ink on active).
  - Enum: label on left; styled `<select>` on right (`appearance-none` with chevron overlay).
- If `ParamRow`'s internal switch body exceeds ~80 lines, extract `NumberRow` / `BooleanRow` / `EnumRow` as unexported sub-components in the same file with a `satisfies never` exhaustiveness check.
- **Move "Download STL" button** from wherever 1.b left it to match screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png — likely stat-strip right-end or a top-right action slot on the viewer. **Preserve literal button text "Download STL"**.
- **Delete `components/ParamForm.tsx`** at end of bead.

## Testing (SCOPE EXTENSION — 2026-04-23)

Phase 1 ships new interactive surface area. Rather than waiting for a separate hardening bead, 1.c absorbs E2E + smoke coverage for everything it introduces. Phase 4 still owns the real mobile bottom-sheet design; this bead only ensures mobile doesn't silently regress.

### E2E additions (`tests/e2e/`)

Use existing fixtures. Split into logical specs; keep each assertion small.

**Create `tests/e2e/viewer-chrome.spec.ts`** covering:
1. `1` / `2` / `3` keys set the camera to top / front / iso — assert on `canvas.__stlViewer.camera` (three separate assertions, or one parametric test).
2. `G` key toggles the grid overlay — assert the SVG pattern's visibility (presence of a stable `data-testid="viewer-grid"` that appears/hides).
3. `D` key toggles the dims flag — assert `data-testid="viewer-dims-toggle"` aria-state or `useDetailState.showDims`.
4. `F` key enters fullscreen — assert `document.fullscreenElement` is the viewer container (or on CI that lacks fullscreen, that the fullscreen request was called; use a fake if needed).
5. Clicking each view-preset tab (top/front/iso/fullscreen) triggers the corresponding camera change.
6. Stat strip displays tri count, render ms, and kb size after a render completes — assert text contents match `/\d+ tris/`, `/\d+ms/`, `/[\d.]+kb/`.

**Create or extend `tests/e2e/param-rail.spec.ts`** covering:
7. Param rail groups render with correct headers (assert header text for each group from the annotated models).
8. Clicking a group header collapses/expands its params (assert row visibility before/after).
9. Dragging the slider updates the numeric input value (bidirectional — simulate `input` event on slider, read number input value).
10. Typing in the numeric input updates the slider thumb position (bidirectional — set number input value, read slider value).
11. Typing a value outside [min, max] in the numeric input is accepted (not clamped). Assert the value the input reports is the typed value.
12. Clicking the boolean pill toggle flips its state (assert `aria-checked` or `role="switch"` state).
13. Changing the enum select fires a param change (assert new value reflected in URL or next render).

**Extend `tests/e2e/gallery.spec.ts`** covering:
14. Clicking a category in the library rail scrolls/anchors to that category's shelf (assert the shelf is in viewport or URL hash updates).

**Create `tests/e2e/mobile-smoke.spec.ts`** using Playwright's `test.use({ viewport: { width: 390, height: 844 } })` (or the iPhone 12 device preset):
15. Library page at 390×844 renders — no horizontal scroll (`document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth`); cards stack in 1 column; a category list is reachable (may be collapsed/stacked; just assert non-broken).
16. Detail page at 390×844 renders — no horizontal scroll; the viewer + param rail are both reachable (acceptable if they stack rather than side-by-side; phase 4 owns the real bottom sheet). STL download button is clickable.
17. No uncaught errors during navigation between library and detail at mobile viewport.

### Unit additions (`components/ParamRow.test.ts`, or inline)

- `<ParamRow>` accepts an out-of-range typed numeric value without clamping (plan's original test).
- `<ParamRow>` boolean pill toggle fires `onChange(name, !currentValue)` when clicked.
- `<ParamRow>` enum select fires `onChange(name, newChoice)` when a new option is selected.

### Test invariants to preserve

No changes required; all from prior beads stay green:
- `id="param-<name>"` on every numeric input (`model-page.spec.ts:13`)
- Literal "Download STL" button label (`model-page.spec.ts:22`)
- `canvas.__stlViewer` debug handle exposing `{ camera, controls }`, EXTENDED (not replaced) with `setCameraPreset` (`preview-controls.spec.ts:21`)
- URL format `/models/<slug>` with `_` → `-` (`gallery.spec.ts:7–12`)
- Render-log format regex `/\d+ms · [\d.]+kb/` established by 1.b (`live-preview.spec.ts`, `model-page.spec.ts`)

## Acceptance

- All prior E2E green unchanged (no regex drift).
- New E2E tests 1–17 above all pass.
- New unit tests for `<ParamRow>` (3 cases) all pass.
- Pixel-parity check against `screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png`: 360px right rail, 36px stat strip, view tabs in top-right, grid overlay visible when `G` pressed.
- `components/ParamForm.tsx` deleted.
- Zero inline styles; zero hex literals.
- Next build green; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

## Do not

- Implement `R` reset-camera shortcut.
- Add a global keyboard shortcut registry (phase 3).
- Touch `useRenderer` or `useDetailState` signatures.
- Add a `dimensions` field to `RenderResult`.
- Reserve `<span data-slot="modified-dot">`.
- Implement the real mobile bottom sheet (phase 4 owns that design). Mobile smoke tests only check "doesn't break", not fidelity.

## References

- docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "Bead 1.c" and D3
- docs/design/caliper/README.md § "Model detail (desktop)" and "Keyboard Shortcuts"
- docs/design/caliper/screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png (pixel reference)
- docs/design/caliper/design_files/caliper.jsx:232–417 (composition hints only — do NOT copy verbatim), :432–462 (grid+axes SVG technique), :537–592 (group collapse pattern)
- components/StlViewer.tsx:95 (debug handle — extend, don't replace)
- playwright.config.ts (devices and viewport presets)
close_reason: Merged to main as bb6a4cb. Viewer chrome (grid/axes/tabs/stat strip), grouped param rail with ParamRow, extended E2E (viewer-chrome, param-rail, gallery anchor) + mobile-smoke spec at 390x844. Phase 1 of Caliper redesign complete.

title: [redesign/phase-1b] Detail shell + useRenderer + param metadata
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-58ln
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T12:31:21Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

**Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 1
**Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md (bead 1.b section)
**Parallelizable with**: 1.a. **Blocks**: 1.c.

Extracts the render pipeline into `useRenderer`, introduces `useDetailState`, replaces the monolithic `ModelStudio` with a 3-col `DetailPage` shell, and extends `@param` annotations with `unit=` and `group=`. Pure refactor + architecture — cosmetic chrome (real viewer chrome + real param rail) comes in 1.c.

## Scope

### 1. Parser extension (`lib/scad-params/parse.ts` line 48)

Refactor to `ParamBase` + per-kind interfaces:

```ts
interface ParamBase { name: string; label?: string; group?: string; unit?: string; }
interface NumberParam  extends ParamBase { kind: 'number';  default: number; min?: number; max?: number; step?: number; }
interface BooleanParam extends ParamBase { kind: 'boolean'; default: boolean; }
interface StringParam  extends ParamBase { kind: 'string';  default: string; }
interface EnumParam    extends ParamBase { kind: 'enum';    default: string; choices: string[]; }
type Param = NumberParam | BooleanParam | StringParam | EnumParam;
export type ParamValue = number | boolean | string;
```

Parser recognizes `unit=<free-form>` and `group=<slug>` on any `@param` line. Both optional. Add tests for: `unit` only; `group` only; both absent; both present.

### 2. Annotate models

Add `unit=` and `group=` to every `@param` line in `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, `models/popcorn_kernel.scad`, `models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad`. Use natural per-model groupings (e.g. spraycan carrier: `cans / geometry / handle`). Group display order = first-occurrence order.

### 3. `hooks/useRenderer.ts`

```ts
type RenderState =
  | { kind: 'idle' }
  | { kind: 'loading'; since: number }
  | { kind: 'ready'; result: RenderResult }
  | { kind: 'error'; error: RenderError };
type RenderResult = { stlBytes: Uint8Array; triCount: number; ms: number };
type RenderError  = { line: number | null; message: string; log: string };

function useRenderer(input: { modelPath: string; source: string; params: Record<string, ParamValue> }): {
  state: RenderState;
  history: RenderResult[];   // ring buffer, last 5 successful renders
  refresh: () => void;       // for phase 2 use
};
```

Port logic verbatim from `components/ModelStudio.tsx:25–66`: 250 ms debounce, `renderToken` cancel, `applyParamOverrides` SCAD rewrite. Keep STL size off the type — compute at call sites via `stlBytes.byteLength`. Phase 1 always sets `error.line = null`.

### 4. `hooks/useRenderer.test.ts` — ONE smoke test

Fire render A (slow), fire render B (fast), assert B's result wins — stale A is discarded via the token path. (E2E is too slow to debug this subtlety when it regresses.)

### 5. `hooks/useDetailState.ts`

```ts
type DetailState = { params: Record<string, ParamValue>; camera: 'top'|'front'|'iso'; showGrid: boolean; showDims: boolean; };
```

Return `{ state, setParam, setCamera, toggleGrid, toggleDims }`. No preset fields.

### 6. `components/DetailPage.tsx` (client, new)

Composes three placeholder children — real implementations in 1.c:
- `<DetailLeftRail>` (240 px): source-file quick-jump (reuse toggle logic from `ModelStudio.tsx:129–149`); render log from `useRenderer().history`, 5 entries, `font-mono text-10`. Warnings array appears here as dim-colored entries.
- `<ViewerChrome>` (flex): renders `<StlViewer stl={state.kind === 'ready' ? state.result.stlBytes : undefined}/>` only. No grid/axes/tabs/stat-strip yet.
- `<ParamRail>` (360 px): wraps existing `<ParamForm>` verbatim, restyled minimally (swap inline hex to token-backed Tailwind classes) so dark palette doesn't look broken.

3-col grid: `grid-cols-[240px_1fr_360px]` at `xl:`; single column below.

### 7. Server shell

`app/models/[slug]/page.tsx`: shrink to `loadModel(slug)` + `<DetailPage model={model}/>`. Delete inline chrome at lines 24–48. Warnings array passes via props.

### 8. Delete `components/ModelStudio.tsx` at end of bead.

### 9. E2E

- `tests/e2e/model-page.spec.ts:24` regex → new render-log format (e.g. `/\d+ms · [\d.]+kb/`).
- `tests/e2e/live-preview.spec.ts:10` regex → same update.
- Keep `#param-base_cut` selector and "Download STL" button literal working.

## Acceptance

- All 10 E2E specs green (with the two regex updates).
- `components/ModelStudio.tsx` deleted.
- `StlViewer.tsx` untouched — `canvas.__stlViewer` handle at line 95 still works.
- `useRenderer` smoke test passes stale-token scenario.
- Vitest green including new parser tests and new smoke test.

## Do not

- Add preset fields (`activePresetId`, `modifiedSincePreset`) to any type.
- Add `dimensions` or `sizeBytes` to `RenderResult`.
- Add the `R` reset-camera keyboard shortcut.
- Reserve a `<span data-slot="modified-dot">` in the DOM.
- Touch `ParamForm.tsx` beyond minimal restyle to not look broken.

## References

- docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md § "Architectural decisions" D1, D2, D6 and § "Bead 1.b"
- docs/design/caliper/README.md § "State Management"
- components/ModelStudio.tsx:25–66 (logic to port), :129–149 (source toggle)
- components/StlViewer.tsx:31 (ResizeObserver — do not break), :95 (debug handle)
- lib/scad-params/parse.ts:48 (union to refactor)
close_reason: Merged to main as 24bc513. ParamBase refactor, useRenderer with discriminated-union state + history ring buffer + token-cancel smoke test, ModelStudio deleted, 17 E2E passed / 40 vitest green.

title: [redesign/phase-1a] Library page + catalog + categories
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-v5lk
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T12:30:34Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

**Part of**: st-gtb (Caliper redesign epic), phase 1
**Plan**: docs/plans/2026-04-22-feat-caliper-redesign-phase-1-plan.md (bead 1.a section)
**Parallelizable with**: 1.b — no shared files in scope

Rewrites the home library per docs/design/caliper/screenshots/01-library-desktop.png. Introduces the catalog data model (categories + per-model blurbs) that all later beads depend on for the left-rail category list.

## Scope

1. Add `clsx` dependency (use directly; no `lib/cn.ts` wrapper).
2. Create `lib/models/catalog.ts` — `MODEL_CATEGORIES` list + `CATALOG` record keyed by stem with `{ categoryId, blurb }`. Seed all three current stems: `cylindrical_holder_slot`, `popcorn_kernel`, `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm`. Fail loud on missing entry — no `'misc'` fallback.

title: [bug] Spraycan carrier 2×3: handle floats detached from base in preview
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-iyau
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T03:41:49Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: unknown
formula_vars: base_branch=main

**Model**: /models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad (after st-8ac fix)

**Observed in**: live OpenSCAD preview (screenshot from user 2026-04-22 21:17)

**Problem**: After the st-8ac fix (cell_spacing_x/y split + handle_height 125→250), the handle arch is rendered floating high above the base with no connection to the carrier walls. Handle and 2×3 base are two visually disconnected solids — the arch's legs do not land on / union with the cell walls or baseplate.

**Likely cause**: Raising handle_height to 250mm while keeping the arch geometry anchored to a fixed Z that no longer reaches the top of the cell walls. The legs that should extend down into the Y-gap between rows are either (a) sized off the old handle_height and now too short, or (b) the arch is translated up by the full 250 without a matching extrusion of the legs.

**Acceptance**: Handle legs meet / union with the baseplate (or cell walls), producing a single manifold solid when rendered. Arch apex still clears a 195mm can + grip space. No overhang floating in mid-air.

**Reference**: screenshot at ~/Screenshot 2026-04-22 at 9.17.51 PM.png on the user's workstation. Two visible bodies: a green arch in upper half, a 2×3 carrier tray in lower half, nothing between them.

**Constraints unchanged**: 2×3 layout, 50mm slots, kid-safe, wet-safe, tall-can (195mm) clearance (from st-8ac).
notes: Fix already landed in commit 7a57db4 before this bead reached obsidian. Verified: handle() module now sets post_h = base_thickness + arch_z_start + 1, so posts span z=0 through the arch junction with 1mm overlap on both ends (prevents zero-thickness coincident planes that confused preview renderers). Re-rendered models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad at defaults — iso and side views show a single continuous solid from baseplate through posts to arch. No floating bodies. Closing no-changes.
close_reason: Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)

title: Spraycan carrier 2×3: handle interferes with full-height cans
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-ortk

attached_at: 2026-04-23T03:42:00Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: unknown
formula_vars: base_branch=main

**Model**: /models/spraycan-carrier-6x50mm (shipped as st-wisp-4dy by quartz)

**Observed in**: viewer/STL (not printed yet)

**Problem**: Handle sits too low over the can holders. Full-height tall spray cans (~195mm, 50mm diameter) don't clear the handle — you can't seat them into the holders or lift the carrier with the cans in place.

**Fix direction (either or both, polecat's choice)**:
1. Reposition/spread the can holders so the handle runs through space between them rather than over them — e.g., 2×3 footprint with handle spanning the long axis between two rows of 3, clear of the top of any can.
2. Switch to a side hand-hold (cutout in the long wall or external lip grip) instead of an overhead bail handle. This eliminates the clearance problem entirely.

**Design constraints (unchanged from original bead hq-cv-2h2os)**: 2×3 layout, 50mm can slots, kid-safe, wet-safe, handled carry.

**Target can**: tall (~195mm). If a geometry choice forces short-only support, flag it before shipping and we'll re-scope.

**Acceptance**: Full-height tall cans seat fully, and the carrier can be lifted (either by overhead handle with clearance above the tallest can top, or by a side grip).
close_reason: no-changes: already fixed on main in 292eb39 (split cell_spacing_x=60 / cell_spacing_y=90 so handle runs in the gap between the two rows; raised handle_height 125→250 to clear 195mm cans + 55mm grip) and follow-up 7a57db4 (welded posts through base/arch). Current branch is up to date with origin/main; no further work to do.

title: CI: render preview images for listing page on every deploy
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-fu32
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T22:35:51Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main","base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main
base_branch=main

Full plan: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-preview-image-ci-plan.md

## Problem
Listing page (`app/page.tsx`) serves thumbnails via `/api/thumbnail?model=<slug>` which reads `renders/<stem>/top.png` off disk. `renders/` is gitignored, so Vercel deployments ship zero thumbnails and every tile 404s in prod.

## Scope (4 implementation units — see plan for details)
1. `scripts/render-all.*` — driver that renders every `.scad` in `models/` and prunes stale `renders/` dirs. Wraps existing `.claude/skills/scad-render/scripts/render.py`.
2. New `render` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — apt-installs `openscad` + `xvfb`, vendors SCAD libs (BOSL2, QuackWorks — match existing e2e job pattern), runs `render-all`, commits diff back to PR branch via `github-actions[bot]`. Path-filtered on PRs (`models/**`, `.claude/skills/scad-render/**`, `scripts/render-all.*`), unconditional on push to main.
3. Un-gitignore `renders/*/` (keep `exports/*.stl` ignored). Commit baseline PNGs.
4. Short contributor note in `AGENTS.md` explaining the auto-render flow.

## Key decisions (from the plan)
- Storage: commit PNGs to repo, NOT Vercel Blob. Catalog is ~2.4 MB today, slow growth.
- Renderer: apt `openscad` 2021.01 in CI (spike confirmed PNG parity with AppImage).
- Thumbnail route stays unchanged.
- Job needs `permissions: contents: write` for commit-back.

## Done when
- PR against main shows a green `render` CI check.
- After merge, Vercel prod deploy serves all 6 current thumbnails with 200 (no 404s on listing page).
- A test PR that edits a `.scad` file produces an auto-commit from `github-actions[bot]` with regenerated PNGs visible in the PR diff.

## Notes
- Deferred to implementation (polecat: use judgment): Python vs bash for `render-all`; fail-fast vs render-all-then-fail semantics; whether commit-back needs signing.
- Do NOT change `app/api/thumbnail/route.ts`, do NOT migrate to `next/image`, do NOT introduce Vercel Blob.
notes: Starting implementation. Plan: (1) scripts/render-all.py driver wrapping .claude/skills/scad-render/scripts/render.py, renders all models/*.scad + prunes stale renders/<stem>/. (2) render job in .github/workflows/ci.yml: apt openscad+xvfb, scripts/vendor-libs.sh, render-all, commit-back. (3) Un-gitignore renders/*/, keep exports/*.stl ignored. (4) Baseline PNGs for 3 current models. (5) AGENTS.md note.

title: Phase 0: WASM feasibility spike — openscad-wasm + BOSL2 + QuackWorks
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-tntf
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T00:54:34Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal
Research spike. Determine whether `openscad-wasm` can render the library stack we already depend on. This is the single biggest architectural risk in the parametric-web-app project — if WASM can't handle BOSL2 + QuackWorks, the hybrid architecture collapses into server-only and every downstream decision shifts.

## Scope
**Research only.** Stand up the smallest possible WASM harness, run three rendering probes in order, and deliver a verdict.

## Probes (in order — stop as soon as one fails)
1. **Trivial cube.** `cube([10,10,10]);` — baseline that WASM handles at all.
2. **BOSL2.** Render `models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad` (pure BOSL2, no QuackWorks). Confirm include resolution works.
3. **QuackWorks + BOSL2.** Render `models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad` (uses `multiconnectGenerator` from QuackWorks). Confirm the Multiconnect backer appears in the output.

At each probe capture: render time, memory peak, output size, any warnings, any errors.

## Deliverable
Appendix on this bead with:
- Which `openscad-wasm` build was used (URL + version + date)
- Per-probe: pass/fail, render time, notes
- Library-loading approach that worked (virtual filesystem layout for `include <BOSL2/std.scad>` etc.)
- **Verdict:** one of
  - `go-hybrid` — all 3 probes pass, proceed with WASM for live preview
  - `go-server-only` — BOSL2 and/or QuackWorks fail; drop live WASM preview, route all rendering through server function
  - `unclear` — partial success needing further investigation

## Non-deliverable
No Next.js app. No UI. No deployment. No integration with existing scripts. Pure WASM harness.

## Suggested starting points
- `openscad-wasm` on GitHub (search for the most-starred / recently-maintained fork)
- Its example runner usually shows how to mount virtual fs libraries

## Out of scope (future phases)
Blocks hq-? (Phase 1). Do not start Next.js work from this bead.

## Context
Parent project: turn `stuff` into a Vercel-deployed parametric OpenSCAD web app. Hybrid WASM (live preview) + server function (STL download). Inline `// @param` parser, filesystem discovery, public access.
notes:
Verdict: go-hybrid — all 3 probes pass.

BUILD: openscad-wasm-prebuilt@1.2.0 (npm, published 2025-01-22) from https://github.com/lorenzowritescode/openscad-wasm — packages OpenSCAD 2025.01.19 with both CGAL + Manifold backends. The official openscad/openscad-wasm release page is stale (last release 2022.03.20, fails on BOSL2).

LIBRARY MOUNT: Mount repo libs/ tree into Emscripten FS at /libraries/<libname>/... — that's OpenSCAD's built-in search path, so include <BOSL2/std.scad> resolves with zero -I flags. Symlinks must be followed (libs/BOSL2 etc. are symlinks here).

PROBE RESULTS (all PASS):
- 1 trivial cube                : 54 ms wall, 1.5 KB STL
- 2a BOSL2-only synthetic        : 1.2 s wall, 7.9 KB STL (added — see note)
- 2 spraycan_holder_70mm.scad    : 62.8 s wall (1.0 s internal), 2.84 MB STL, 12 098 facets
- 3 cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad : 9.0 s wall (0.4 s internal), 781 KB STL, 2 892 facets

KEY GOTCHA: --backend Manifold is mandatory. CGAL (the default) blew RSS to 3.3 GB on a trivial BOSL2 cuboid and aborted. Manifold kept it under 250 MB.

BEAD ACCURACY NOTE: spraycan_holder_70mm.scad is described as 'pure BOSL2', but it actually has 'use <QuackWorks/Modules/snapConnector.scad>' — there are no BOSL2-only models in the repo. Probe 2a (synthetic inline cuboid w/ rounding) was added to isolate BOSL2 cleanly from QuackWorks.

PERFORMANCE PROFILE: Wall-clock dominated by parse + include resolution, NOT CSG. OpenSCAD's own internal render time is sub-second for every probe. The 60 s wall-clock for spraycan is the include resolver scanning all 576 mounted .scad files. A Phase 1 include-closure mounter (parse the model's own includes, mount only the dep graph) should get this under 5 s.

PHASE 1 RECOMMENDATIONS:
1. Pin openscad-wasm-prebuilt@1.2.0
2. Always pass --backend Manifold
3. Build a closure-only mounter to fix the parse-time problem
4. Hybrid render policy: WASM first w/ 5 s budget, server-side fallback for slow models
5. Keep server CGAL build for parity / fallback

ARTIFACTS: experiments/wasm-spike/ (commit bc0207d) — harness.mjs (reproducible probes), findings.md (full writeup), README.md. Reproduce with: cd experiments/wasm-spike && npm install && node harness.mjs

title: Blu valve mount v2 — hex saddle bore + larger base footprint
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-rl3b
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-16T01:14:11Z
attached_args: v2 rework of st-i32 (valve mount on main at b187a6a). Two changes:

1. Convert circular saddle bore to HEX. The 31.5/29.25 mm dimensions the operator provided are flat-to-flat hex measurements, not circular diameters. Hex orientation: flats horizontal (top + bottom), split between saddle bottom and cap at the equator (passes through left+right vertices). Polecat must verify orientation visually — OpenSCAD's `cylinder($fn=6)` defaults to vertex-at-+x and the rotate to make the hex axis = global X may need an extra rotation around X to get flats horizontal.

2. Enlarge base defaults: base_w 70→90, base_d 50→60. Current footprint is barely bigger than the saddle stack so the assembly view looks like the base is missing.

Photos at models/refs/blu_valve_v1_*.jpeg (44e1f91) show the actual hex profile. Look at them.

Don't change hardware (M3×30 SHCS, bottom-entry, cap heat-sets) and don't touch the flow meter mount. Single feat commit. Invariants harness must pass.

Per project_obsidian_stale_dispatch_pattern: this is fresh post-print review feedback — the v1 model on main does need real SCAD edits. Don't false-close as "already shipped".

Budget ~half a polecat-day.
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-pugdc
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Blu black-tank valve mount v2 — rework after first SCAD review

Operator review of st-i32 (model at `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.scad`, merged b187a6a) caught two real geometry issues. Photos at `models/refs/blu_valve_v1_*.jpeg` show the actual valve hex profile.

## Problem 1 — Circular bore is wrong; fittings are HEX nuts

The 31.5 mm and 29.25 mm dimensions the operator provided are **flat-to-flat measurements across a hex nut**, NOT circular diameters. Current SCAD line 254-263 uses `cylinder(h=length, r=r)` for the pipe channel cut — that's a round bore that won't grip the hex flats.

### Concrete deltas

- Replace `_pipe_channel_cut` cylinder with a **hexagonal prism** cut:
  - Hex axis = X (along inline fittings, same as current cylinder axis)
  - Hex orientation: **flats horizontal** — one flat at top (+Z = +flat/2 from center), one flat at bottom (-Z = -flat/2 from center)
  - The split between saddle_bottom and cap falls AT THE EQUATOR (z = pipe_center_z), passing through the two side vertices of the hex
  - Saddle bottom holds: bottom horizontal flat + two angled flats below equator + the two equator-level vertices
  - Cap holds: top horizontal flat + two angled flats above equator + same equator vertices (mirror)
- Asymmetric: left bore = 31.5 mm flat-to-flat, right bore = 29.25 mm flat-to-flat (UNCHANGED parameter values, just reinterpret)
- Rename SCAD params from `bore_left` / `bore_right` to `hex_ftf_left` / `hex_ftf_right` (flat-to-flat) for clarity. Same for `pipe_channel_r_left` → `hex_apothem_left` (= ftf/2) — or keep the existing names with updated comments. Polecat's choice; consistency matters more than the exact rename.
- Update `pipe_center_z` calc: replace `max_r` (circular radius) with `max_apothem` (= max_ftf / 2). The saddle wall below the channel is `wall_t + max_apothem`.
- Update `saddle_bottom_h`: same formula but using apothem.
- Update slop interpretation: `slop` is now a per-flat radial pad (same value 0.2 mm default). Effective channel ftf = `hex_ftf + 2 * slop`. Document this in the header.
- Implementation hint: `cylinder(h=length, r=ftf/sqrt(3), $fn=6)` gives a hex prism, BUT OpenSCAD's default $fn=6 orientation has a VERTEX at the +x axis (in the cylinder's local frame). After rotating [0, 90, 0] to align hex axis with global X, you may need to add an extra rotation around X to get flats horizontal. **Verify orientation visually in the assembly view** before declaring done — flats must be parallel to the bench top.

## Problem 2 — Base footprint is barely larger than the saddle stack

`base_w=70, base_d=50` while saddle outer extent is 67 × 49. Only 1.5 mm and 0.5 mm overhang past the saddle outer faces, so in assembly view the base is visually "missing" behind the cap+saddle stack.

### Concrete deltas

- Default `base_w = 90` (matches flow meter mount's pattern; gives ~11 mm overhang past saddles in X)
- Default `base_d = 60` (matches flow meter mount; gives ~5.5 mm overhang past saddle/cap Y)
- Update `@preset id="default"` line accordingly
- Update the docstring's `=== Geometry ===` section to reflect the new defaults

Don't blow it up bigger than that. The flow meter convention is the right size for desk stability without wasting filament.

## Side outlet clearance (verify, don't change unless needed)

The valve's third fitting points laterally (perpendicular to inline pair). With the new wider base (60 mm depth), verify the side outlet — including its QD plug if attached — clears the base edge. Photos in `models/refs/blu_valve_v1_top_three_fittings.jpeg` show the orientation. If 60 mm depth introduces collision, narrow / notch the base on that single side. Otherwise leave it symmetric.

## Handle clearance (no change expected)

The handle exclusion cylinder (`handle_excl_r=20, handle_excl_h=40`) carves a vertical column through both caps. Hex vs circular doesn't affect this. Re-verify it still produces a thin relief on cap inboard tops, no more, no less.

## What to deliver

1. **Modify `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.scad`** in place. New header section explaining hex orientation + flat-to-flat tolerance interpretation. Update `=== Derived ===` block. Update `_pipe_channel_cut` to hex prism. Update base defaults.
2. **Update `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.invariants.py`** with assertions for the new hex geometry (verify cut profile has 6 distinct flats per saddle, base footprint extends past saddle outer faces).
3. **Re-render `models/renders/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.png`** to show the corrected assembly view (with visible base + hex bores).
4. **Single feat commit** referencing this bead.

## Verification

- OpenSCAD assembly view: base is clearly visible past the cap+saddle stack on all sides (the "missing base" symptom is gone)
- Hex bores: clearly hexagonal cross-section, flats horizontal, ftf matches param values (with slop)
- Asymmetry preserved: left cap bore is wider than right (31.5 vs 29.25 mm ftf)
- Invariants harness passes
- Per memory `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`: header docstring axis mapping still accurate

## Hard constraints

- **Don't change bolt entry direction or hardware spec.** Bottom-entry M3 SHCS + cap heat-sets stays. The hex change shouldn't move the bolt geometry; verify the heat-set pockets still clear the new hex bore (apothem may bring the bore wall closer to the inserts than the old circular radius did).
- **Don't touch the flow meter mount.** This bead is valve-mount-specific.
- **Don't change tolerances.** slop stays at 0.2 mm; first-print feedback dictates whether to tighten.
- **PETG/ASA default**, same as st-i32.
- Per memory `feedback_polecat_no_silent_revert`: this is v2 of st-i32. Modify, don't revert.
- Budget: half a polecat-day.

## Refs

- v1 model: `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.scad` (b187a6a, st-i32)
- Reference photos: `models/refs/blu_valve_v1_*.jpeg` (44e1f91)
- Sibling: `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad` (v2, st-246)
- BOSL2: `libs/BOSL2/` (vendored)
- Memory: `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`

## Blu Technology black-tank flush valve — captive bench mount

Operator request 2026-05-15. Sibling part to the Blu flow meter mount (st-jtn / st-246). Print as a companion mount that grips the **inner fittings** of a 3-way black-tank flush valve, with the valve body and T-handle clear of the saddles for easy actuation.

## Part being mounted

Blu Technology black-tank flush valve — a brass/stainless 3-way diverter with:
- Quick-disconnect inline pair: female socket on one end, male plug on the other
- Side outlet (perpendicular to inline axis) — a third fitting
- T-handle on top, perpendicular to the inline axis, that rotates ~90° to switch flow paths

**Critical dimensions (operator-confirmed 2026-05-15):**
- Width between inner fittings (inner faces gap): **37 mm**
- Left inner fitting diameter: **31.5 mm**
- Right inner fitting diameter: **29.25 mm**

(Asymmetric. The flow meter mount's symmetric pipe channel does NOT apply here — each saddle is sized to its specific fitting.)

## Three design constraints from the operator

1. **No usable bare-pipe section** — unlike the flow meter, the valve doesn't expose a clean cylindrical pipe section between the QD ends. The mount must grip **the innermost fittings themselves** (the cylindrical sections immediately inboard of the QD ends), which are the ones with the 31.5 mm and 29.25 mm diameters above.
2. **All three fittings parallel to the mounting surface.** The valve sits with its inline axis along +X, and its third (side) outlet axis along ±Y. The T-handle is the ONLY element perpendicular to the bench (+Z axis). The base must leave the side outlet free to extend out laterally without colliding.
3. **Clearance around the valve control switch (T-handle).** The handle rotates ~90° around the vertical axis to actuate the valve. Mount geometry must leave the handle and its full rotational swept volume free.

## Design — adapted from `blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad` v2

Reuse the established pattern from the v2 flow meter mount (st-246, on main at 57713b4):

- **Base**: flat-bottom-down. Bottom-entry M3 SHCS counter-bored flush with the bench-contact surface (same hardware approach as v2).
- **Two saddle pairs** straddling the inline axis. **Asymmetric** — left saddle ID for the 31.5 mm fitting, right saddle ID for the 29.25 mm fitting (each `pipe_dia + 2·slop`).
- **Heat-set inserts in the caps** (not in the base). Same M3×30 SHCS spec as v2 (or recompute the stack — see Hardware below).
- **Cap relief / chamfer** on the inboard-facing edges if needed to clear the valve body's bulge in the middle (less critical here since the valve body is centered, not rotating, but check during preview).

### New: handle clearance

The T-handle sits above the valve body in the middle ~37 mm gap between saddles. Its swept volume must NOT intersect any cap face. Spec: ensure the **inboard top faces** of the two caps stay clear of a swept volume centered at the valve-body centerline, extending ~40 mm upward (handle height) and ~20 mm radial from the handle pivot in any direction (handle reach). Polecat to refine — visualize an "exclusion cylinder" at the handle pivot and confirm no cap geometry intersects it.

### New: side-outlet clearance

The third (perpendicular) fitting extends out one side. The base **should NOT** be wider than `2 × saddle_y_half` in Y on the side where the outlet exits — let the outlet hang off the base edge into open space. Default: base_d (Y depth) matches the flow meter pattern (60 mm), but the polecat should verify the side outlet, with QD plug attached, doesn't intersect the base. **If needed, narrow the base on the outlet side or notch it.**

## Hardware

Per st-246 stack math (`3.2 head + 4.8 base + 16.7 saddle_bottom + 5 insert = 29.7 mm`), v2's M3×30 SHCS is the baseline. Recompute for this part — saddle_bottom_h depends on `wall_t + max(pipe_channel_r_left, pipe_channel_r_right)`, so it'll be slightly different. Document the corrected stack math in the SCAD header.

## What to deliver

1. **New SCAD file**: `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.scad`. Don't extend the flow-meter file — separate parts, separate STLs.
2. **Invariants harness**: `models/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.invariants.py` mirroring the flow-meter file's pattern (counter-bores on base bottom, inserts in caps, asymmetric saddle IDs, handle exclusion zone check).
3. **STL exports** under `exports/` for the two unique parts: base (1 print), cap-left (31.5 mm bore, 1 print), cap-right (29.25 mm bore, 1 print). Three STLs since the caps are NOT identical here.
4. **Header docstring** following st-246 conventions:
   - Part identification (Blu black-tank flush valve)
   - Critical dimensions (37 mm gap, 31.5 mm + 29.25 mm fittings)
   - Install orientation (inline axis = X, side outlet = Y, handle = +Z; bench surface at z=0)
   - Hardware spec with corrected stack math
   - Print orientation (base flat-bottom-down, caps flat-top-down)
5. **Render** at `models/renders/blu_black_tank_valve_mount.png` if `openscad --render` is set up.

## Verification

- OpenSCAD assembly view: visualize two asymmetric saddles holding mock cylinders of 31.5 and 29.25 mm OD, 37 mm gap. Confirm the caps don't intersect a handle-exclusion cylinder centered between them (~40 mm tall, ~20 mm radius).
- Invariants harness passes.
- Asymmetric clarity: confirm the left vs right cap STLs are distinguishable (different bore IDs) and labeled in filenames.
- Per memory `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`: header docstring MUST document axes.

## Hard constraints

- **Don't merge with the flow-meter file.** New part, new file.
- **Don't average the saddle IDs.** They're 2.25 mm apart; symmetric saddles would either rock on the smaller fitting or not seat on the larger one.
- **Don't add wall mounting holes / tie-down tabs.** This is a free-standing bench mount only.
- **PETG/ASA default** (water-adjacent like the flow meter).
- **Photos may arrive at `models/refs/blu_valve_v1_*.jpg` from the operator post-bead-creation.** If they do, look at them before pushing. If they don't arrive by the time you're done modeling, build from spec and ship.
- Budget: half a polecat-day.

## Refs

- Sibling: `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad` (v2, st-246, 57713b4) — pattern to adapt from
- Original ancestor: st-jtn (v1 flow meter, 60d9356)
- BOSL2 vendored at `libs/BOSL2/`
- Memory: `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`
- Memory: `feedback_commit_plan_docs_before_sling` — single feat commit, no kitchen-sink

attached_args: Two real-world fit problems from operator's first print of the v1 mount. Photos under models/refs/ on main (commit 0b80c97). Look at them first.

Problem 1: LCD bezel overhangs cap M3 heads — driver can't reach. Fix: invert screw entry. M3 SHCS enter from BOTTOM of base, counter-bored flush, into heat-set inserts now pressed into the CAPS. Hardware count unchanged.

Problem 2: Cap geometry collides with display body when meter rotates around pipe axis. Operator wants forward tilt (~25-35°) at install time. Fix: chamfer the inboard (display-side) top edges of each cap. New param display_relief_angle=35.

Full spec in the bead. Hard constraint: don't deviate from those two changes without mailing mayor first. Single feat commit. Invariants harness must pass. ~Half a polecat-day.
