## Blu Technology flow meter mount — v2 rework after first print

The v1 mount (st-jtn, model at `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad`, merged 60d9356) printed and assembled, but the operator hit two real-world geometry problems on test fit. Both are visible in `models/refs/blu_mount_v1_screw_clearance.jpeg` and `models/refs/blu_mount_v1_desired_tilt.jpeg` — **look at the photos first** before touching the SCAD.

## Problem 1 — M3 driver cannot reach the cap screws

The LCD bezel of the meter overhangs the cap's outer top face. With the v1 design (M3 SHCS pass DOWN through each cap into heat-set inserts in the base, head-on-top), a hex driver coming in from +Z hits the LCD bezel before it can engage the SHCS head. The operator cannot tighten or service the caps in situ.

**Decision (operator-confirmed): invert the screw entry.** M3 SHCS now enter from the **bottom of the base**, pass UP through the base, and thread into heat-set inserts pressed into the **caps**. Head sits flush with (or recessed into) the base bottom; inserts move from base to caps.

### Concrete deltas

- Heat-set insert pockets: remove from base, add to caps. Insert OD/depth unchanged (5 mm × 5 mm). Insert pocket opens from the cap's pipe-equator-facing face (the face that mates against the saddle bottom) — so the insert installs into the cap with the cap oriented for printing (flat-top-down).
- Bolt clearance hole: remove from caps, add to base. Pass-through through the full 8 mm base thickness; aligned with the insert in the cap above.
- Counter-bore in base BOTTOM for the M3 SHCS head: 5.8 mm diameter, 3.2 mm depth (same `m3_head_d` / `m3_head_depth` parameters, just on the opposite face). Head sits flush or below the bench-contact surface so the base doesn't rock.
- Print orientation: base still prints flat-bottom-down, but the bottom face now has the counter-bores. They print as small overhangs at the bottom of the base (3.2 mm deep × 5.8 mm wide); fine without supports — short bridge.
- Cap print orientation unchanged (flat-top-down), but its inner face (mating face) now has the heat-set pocket. That pocket opens upward when the cap is printed top-down, so it prints as a clean blind hole.
- M3×10 SHCS length still fits the new sandwich (base 8 mm + cap insert depth 5 mm = 13 mm of usable thread + head, head sits in 3.2 mm counter-bore → total 16.2 mm budget for an M3×10). Verify in the comment block; if too tight, the operator can bump to M3×12 or shave 1 mm of counter-bore depth.

## Problem 2 — display cannot tilt forward

The operator wants the meter rotated around its pipe axis so the LCD faces partly toward the viewer (see `blu_mount_v1_desired_tilt.jpeg` — meter is held at roughly 25-35° forward of vertical). The current cap geometry has a sharp corner on the display-side (inboard) edges of each cap. When the meter rotates forward, the display body's flank swings down and collides with that corner.

**Decision (operator-confirmed): cap relief, operator-adjustable.** Keep the pipe channel horizontal so the saddle still grips the bare pipe cleanly; **add a chamfer/relief on the inboard-facing edges of each cap** so the display body has clearance to swing forward (and back) by 30-40° from vertical, operator's choice at install time.

### Concrete deltas

- The cap's inboard face (the face facing the display gap — perpendicular to pipe axis) gets a chamfer along its top edge that opens up clearance into the display zone.
- Geometry suggestion (polecat to refine — must check it doesn't break the bolt-hole / insert-pocket geometry):
  - On each cap's inboard face, chamfer the top edge by removing material at 45° (or steeper if the polecat finds 45° insufficient).
  - Chamfer depth should let the display body — which extends radially past 27 mm/2 = 13.5 mm and forward of the pipe axis — rotate at least ±30° around the pipe axis without contacting the cap.
  - The chamfer must NOT eat into the heat-set insert pocket. Insert sits at the cap's center in Y, and the chamfer is on the inboard (display-side) face — geometric check: chamfer plane must clear the insert pocket by ≥1 mm of wall material.
- Parameter: add `display_relief_angle = 35` (degrees from vertical) — the chamfer angle that the display can swing to. Default 35°. Operator-tunable.
- Don't relieve the outboard (saddle-end) face of the caps — only the two display-facing inboard faces. Those are the only ones the display body can swing into.

## What to deliver

1. **Modify `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad`** in place. Update the header docstring's Hardware + Install orientation + Geometry sections to match the v2 design. Add the new `display_relief_angle` parameter. Update the `default` @preset with any new defaults.
2. **Update `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.invariants.py`** with assertions for the new geometry (counter-bore in base bottom, heat-set pocket in cap, chamfer geometry). The existing invariants harness in this rig is the right way to gate against regression — don't write a new one.
3. **Verify visually** in OpenSCAD: assembly view should show counter-bores on base bottom, inserts in caps, and the inboard cap chamfers. Render `models/renders/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.png` and commit if the polecat's `openscad --render` is set up.
4. **Commit with a single `feat(models): ...` message** referencing this bead. Per memory `feedback_use_beads_not_todos` and `feedback_commit_plan_docs_before_sling`, no plan-doc kitchen-sinking.

## Verification

- **Geometric**: In OpenSCAD assembly view, simulate rotating the meter around the pipe axis (mentally or with an `assembly_tilt` debug parameter — fine to add, gated to assembly view only). Confirm the display body clears the cap at ±30° from vertical. Confirm counter-bores are visible on the base bottom and the SHCS heads recess fully.
- **Hardware fit**: confirm M3×10 still bridges the new sandwich. State the exact thread engagement length in a comment.
- **Print orientation unchanged**: base flat-bottom-down (counter-bores print as short overhangs), cap flat-top-down (insert pockets print as clean blind holes). State this explicitly in the docstring.
- **Invariants harness**: must pass. Run it from the model's directory before pushing.
- **No design deviation beyond the two confirmed changes**. If something else feels off when looking at the photos (e.g. the operator might also want the saddle slightly slimmer to give the display MORE swing-room), **STOP and `gt mail mayor`** before re-scoping. Per memory `feedback_polecat_no_silent_revert` and `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`.

## Hard constraints

- Stay PETG/ASA default in the file. Don't switch materials.
- Don't add adjustability for the bolt entry direction — bottom-entry is the new default, full stop.
- Don't add adjustability for the tilt mechanism beyond the chamfer angle. The chamfer is operator-adjustable via rotation at install; no spring-loaded ratchets or tilt-locks.
- Per memory `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`: header docstring axis mapping MUST be reaffirmed (and updated where the bolt direction changes).
- Budget: half a polecat-day. This is two focused parametric tweaks.

## Refs

- v1 model: `models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad` (60d9356, st-jtn)
- v1 print photos: `models/refs/blu_mount_v1_screw_clearance.jpeg`, `models/refs/blu_mount_v1_desired_tilt.jpeg` (this rig, 0b80c97)
- Prior art for chamfers on cap-style parts: `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad`
- BOSL2 docs (vendored): `libs/BOSL2/`
- Memory: `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts` — header MUST document axes
- Memory: `feedback_commit_plan_docs_before_sling` — no kitchen-sink plan docs, single feat commit

title: Blu Technology flow meter — captive bench mount (80mm pipe, 27mm dia, display gap, 4× M3 caps)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-muqg
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-15T12:36:43Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-b7ky4
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Blu Technology water flow meter — captive bench mount

Design and print a captive bench-top mount for a Blu Technology digital water flow meter. Free-standing, no fasteners into a surface — sits on a bench with a wide flat base. Captive M3/heat-set retention so the meter only comes out for service. Brainstormed with operator 2026-05-15.

## Part being mounted

The "Blu Technology Flow Meter" — a stainless steel pipe-body flow meter with an off-center digital LCD display saddle in the middle. Quick-disconnect fittings on both ends (one female socket end, one male plug end). The display has a separate translucent blue lens cover that can be lifted off without affecting the rest of the meter.

**Critical dimensions (from operator):**
- Pipe section diameter: **27mm**
- Pipe section length, between fittings: **80mm**
- Bare pipe before the display-saddle shoulder begins: **18mm from each end**
- → Middle ~44mm of the pipe is occupied by the display saddle (rises off-center)

Reference photos: operator has them at `~/bt_flow_{1,2,3}.webp` (mayor-local, not committed). The dimensional spec above is sufficient to model the mount; ask via `gt mail` if you want the photos sourced into the rig for visual reference.

## Design — "low cradle, hose-dangle, 4-bolt captive"

```
TOP VIEW                                                SIDE VIEW (looking along pipe axis)
                                                          ┌────┐
   ┌─[~]─╔═══╗═════════════╔═══╗─[~]─┐                    │ M3 │
   │      ║ ◯ ║   44mm     ║ ◯ ║      │                ┌──┴─◯──┴──┐
   │ HOSE ╚═══╝ display gap╚═══╝ HOSE │  ←── ~80mm     │  CAP     │  ← removable top half
   ├─15mm─┤              ├─15mm─┤                       ├─────────┤
   │      saddle           saddle     │                 │ SADDLE  │  ← integral bottom half
   └──────────────────────────────────┘                 ├─────────┤
   ←──────────── 80mm ──────────────→                   │  BASE   │  ← flat wide foot
                                                        └─────────┘
                                                         ←─60mm─→
```

**Geometry:**
- Base: 80mm × 60mm × 8mm thick, flat bottom (no feet — TPU pad pockets are deferred).
- Two saddle-pairs sitting on the base, each 15mm wide, centered at x ≈ 10mm and x ≈ 70mm from a base end (so they grip the 18mm bare-pipe bands).
- Pipe channel: 27.2mm diameter (0.2mm slip fit on the 27mm pipe). Each saddle splits at the equator: bottom half is integral to the base, top half is a removable cap.
- 44mm clear span between the saddles — fully open. The display saddle hangs in this gap without interference (and the operator can lift the lens cover off without unmounting).
- Saddle/cap arch height above base top surface: ~33mm (enough to fully enclose 27mm pipe + cap wall).

**Hardware:**
- 4× M3×10mm SHCS (socket-head cap screws), one through each corner of each cap into the base.
- 4× M3 brass heat-set inserts (5mm OD × 5mm depth) in the base, accepting the SHCS from above through the cap.
- Bolt centerlines: ~6mm in from each saddle face (so they don't intersect the pipe channel).

**Material + print:**
- PETG default (water-adjacent, UV-tolerant, easy to print). ASA also fine.
- 3mm walls.
- Base: print flat-bottom-down. No supports needed for the saddle bottom halves (their inner curve overhangs are <45° if you stop the saddle at the pipe equator).
- Caps: print flat-top-down (the cap "ceiling" becomes the build plate; the inner curve overhangs are <45° from there). No supports.
- Tolerances: BOSL2's `cyl()` + `slop` parameter is the cleanest way to dial 27.2mm; allow the polecat to tune from first-print feedback.

## What to deliver

1. **`models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad`** — OpenSCAD source. Use BOSL2 (already vendored in `libs/BOSL2/`). Parameterize the critical dimensions (pipe_dia, pipe_len, bare_pipe_band, saddle_width, base_thickness, m3_insert_dia, slop) at the top of the file with comments. Default values match this bead.
2. **STL exports** under `exports/` for: base (1×), cap (1×, printed twice). Two separate STLs, not a combined one — caps are identical so one STL printed twice is what the operator wants.
3. **Header docstring** in the SCAD file capturing: the meter it fits, the operator's three critical dimensions, the install orientation (horizontal pipe, display up, free-standing on bench), and the hardware spec (4× M3×10mm SHCS, 4× M3 heat-set inserts).
4. **A quick photo or render** committed under `models/renders/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.png` if your `openscad --render` is set up. Optional but nice; defer if it adds significant time.

## Verification

- Visual: load the SCAD in OpenSCAD GUI (or polecat-side via `openscad -o /tmp/check.stl` + `openscad -o /tmp/preview.png`), check that the saddle pair captures the pipe correctly, the display gap is genuinely 44mm clear, and the caps fit on top with their bolt holes aligning with the base inserts.
- Dimensional: print a 5mm-thick test section of one saddle and confirm a 27mm dowel/rod slip-fits cleanly. (Optional — operator may iterate post-first-print.)
- Per `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts`: the SCAD header MUST document axis mapping. +Z = up (away from bench), +X = along pipe axis (flow direction), +Y = lateral. This is for sanity when re-exporting STLs in future.

## Hard constraints

- **No design deviations from the dimensions above without operator confirmation.** If something feels wrong (e.g., the 44mm gap conflicts with another feature you spot in the photos), STOP and `gt mail` mayor; do not silently rescope.
- **Don't add unrequested features.** The operator considered and rejected hose-end channels and tie-down tabs. The mount is *just the meter*. No mission creep.
- **Don't tune the slop until first print.** Default 0.2mm clearance; first-print feedback determines whether to tighten or loosen. Document the slop parameter clearly so the operator can iterate without recompiling logic.
- **Don't pick a non-default material in the file.** PETG is the assumption; print settings are operator's call.
- Budget: 1 polecat-day. Most of this is geometry; the BOSL2 patterns for cradled saddles are well-trodden (see `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` for prior art).

## Refs

- Prior art in this rig: `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad` — similar split-saddle patterns
- BOSL2 docs (vendored): `libs/BOSL2/`; particularly `shapes3d.scad` for `cuboid()` and `tube()`, and `screws.scad` for heat-set insert pockets
- Memory: `feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts` — header MUST document axes
- Operator reference photos: `~/bt_flow_{1,2,3}.webp` (mayor-local; mail mayor if you need them in the rig)

title: goBlu filter holder: print without supports — extend tongue to base + taper slot/tongue tops
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-hdzg
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-11T16:18:06Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-ke5tg
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Make pods printable without support material. Assume print orientation = pod stood upright with its bottom face on the build plate (pocket axis vertical, +Z up). Two features currently require support:

1. **Tongue bottom**: tongue starts at `z = dovetail_cz - dovetail_height/2` ≈ 21.5 mm, floating in air. The bottom face of the tongue is a horizontal overhang at z=21.5 — needs support to print.
2. **Slot top**: slot has a flat roof at `z = dovetail_cz + dovetail_height/2` ≈ 101.5 mm over an open cavity that runs all the way down through the pod base. Roof underside is a long horizontal overhang — needs support.

## Fix (user-recommended + a small refinement)

### Tongue — extend down to the build plate

Current: tongue spans z ≈ 21.5 to z ≈ 101.5 (centered on pod mid-height).

New: tongue spans **z = 0 to z = 101.5**. Anchored to the pod base, prints continuously from layer 1. No bottom overhang. The tongue's top stays at z ≈ 101.5 so the mating geometry (slot top, drop-and-stop alignment) is preserved.

The tongue's **top edge** at z = 101.5 still has a small overhang where the trapezoid's +X tip ends in air (`dovetail_depth = 6 mm` of horizontal overhang). 6 mm is well within FDM bridging capability — no support needed for this edge alone. If a print test shows ugly sag here, add a 1–2 mm chamfer on the top of the tongue's outer edge. Default to no chamfer; verify by print test before adding complexity.

### Slot — taper the top to a point (self-supporting close)

Current: slot is a constant-cross-section void from z = -0.1 to z = 101.5, with a flat roof at z = 101.5.

New: slot is constant cross-section from z = -0.1 up to z = (slot_top − taper_h), then **tapers (scales) to a point at z = slot_top**. The taper is the printable close — each successive layer (as the printer builds up) shrinks the void's footprint, until at z = slot_top the void closes to zero. No horizontal overhang inside the cavity.

Implementation in OpenSCAD: use `linear_extrude(height=taper_h, scale=0)` for the top tapered region, stacked on top of `linear_extrude(height=slot_h - taper_h)` for the straight bottom region. `scale=0` collapses the top to a point — clean self-supporting close.

```scad
// Slot subtraction with tapered top (replaces the current single linear_extrude):
slot_top_z      = dovetail_cz + dovetail_height / 2;
slot_bot_z      = -0.1;
slot_taper_h    = max(dovetail_w_tip / 2 + dovetail_clearance, 4);  // ≥45° for w_tip=18 → ≈9 mm
slot_straight_h = (slot_top_z - slot_bot_z) - slot_taper_h;

translate([-pod_w / 2, 0, slot_bot_z]) {
    // Straight bottom portion
    linear_extrude(height = slot_straight_h)
        slot_polygon();
    // Tapered top — collapses to a point at slot_top_z
    translate([0, 0, slot_straight_h])
        linear_extrude(height = slot_taper_h, scale = 0)
            slot_polygon();
}
```

(`slot_polygon()` = the 2D dovetail trapezoid currently used by `dovetail_tongue`; pull it out into a 2D module so both `dovetail_tongue` and the new slot path can share it.)

### Tongue must taper to match

Critical: if the slot is tapered but the tongue is constant cross-section, the tongue's straight top (z = slot_top − taper_h to z = slot_top) will collide with the slot's tapered walls instead of fitting. **Symmetric taper on the tongue's top region** — same `slot_taper_h`, same scale=0 collapse — is required so the tongue and slot mate cleanly in the tapered region as well as the straight region.

Mechanically this is also a small win: as pod B descends onto pod A, the tapered ends self-center (the tapered tongue tip slides into the tapered slot opening) and provide a wedging engagement at the top, in addition to the flat sliding engagement along the rest of the dovetail length.

Apply the same `linear_extrude` + scale-0 pattern to the tongue's top portion.

## Drop-and-stop alignment — preserved

The descent stops when pod B's slot interior surface engages pod A's tongue exterior surface. With matching tapers, that engagement now happens **across the tapered region** instead of at a single horizontal contact line. Pod B descends until the tapered surfaces fully mate (which happens at the same Z as before — when both tapers reach their collapsed points at z = slot_top in their respective frames). No change to assembly UX.

## Parameter to expose

Add a `@param` for the taper height so the user (or a future polecat) can tune it:

```scad
dovetail_top_taper_h = 9;  // @param number min=0 max=30 step=0.5 unit=mm group=array label="Dovetail top taper (0 = flat top, ≥w_tip/2 for support-free print)"
```

Default = `max(dovetail_w_tip / 2 + dovetail_clearance, 4)` ≈ 9 mm. Setting to 0 reverts to the flat-top behavior (and brings supports back).

Note: with `dovetail_top_taper_h = 0`, both tongue and slot have flat tops — drop-and-stop still works, but the print needs support material. Document this trade-off in the file header.

## Side effects to verify

1. **Mating fit**: print two pods, slide them together. The tapered tongue tip should enter the tapered slot opening cleanly; the dovetail should slide smoothly to full engagement; drop-and-stop should arrest at the correct height with both bottoms aligned.
2. **Slot's drain ring interaction (st-6xj follow-on)**: extending the tongue down to z=0 means the tongue is part of the pod's bottom face too. The bottom retaining ring (z=0 to z=5) is unaffected on the slot side (slot still notches through it), but on the +X face the bottom 5 mm now has the tongue protruding. Verify the bottom face still has a usable build-plate-flat region (the back wall + sides + ring perimeter; the tongue just sticks out off one corner of the bottom face — that should still print fine).
3. **No regression to existing presets**: `stock_3up` (pods touching, gap=0), `slicer_3up` (pods spaced 10 mm), `single_pod`, `flush_3up` (no dovetails). All should render and export cleanly. Visually verify in the gallery web UI.
4. **45° self-support angle**: choose `slot_taper_h ≥ dovetail_w_tip / 2`. With default `w_tip = 18`, that's `≥ 9 mm`. The taper sides should be ≤45° from vertical for FDM-safe overhang. If the formula in the default expression produces something steeper for unusual parameter combos, clamp to ensure 45°.

## Invariants update

`models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.invariants.py`:

1. Assert that the tongue's bottom edge is at z=0 (within float tolerance). Cheapest check: render and BBOX-check that the tongue's protrusion footprint starts at z≈0 rather than z≈21.5.
2. Assert that the slot's top is a point, not a flat edge. Proxy check: the rendered geometry's slot footprint should narrow to ≤ 1 mm² cross-section near z = slot_top (vs. ~150 mm² for the full trapezoid). Or: assert no horizontal triangle face exists in the slot region above z=(slot_top - 0.5).
3. Both checks gated on `dovetail_top_taper_h > 0` so the legacy flat-top mode can still be tested separately if needed.

## Verify before closing

1. Render `stock_3up` in the gallery — pods look correct, dovetails visible with smoothly tapered tops.
2. Render `slicer_3up` — same.
3. Export `slicer_3up.stl`, slice in PrusaSlicer / OrcaSlicer / Bambu Studio with **supports DISABLED**. Inspect the preview layer-by-layer — no overhangs flagged red over the tongue or slot.
4. Print test (operator step, manual): export `slicer_3up`, print one pod with supports off, verify the tongue + slot print without sag / curl. Slide-fit test between two printed pods.

## Refs

- st-6xj (shipped `c368689`) — slot opens through pod bottom; this bead is the natural follow-on
- st-yuu (shipped `1c97565`) — slot cuts INTO wall
- st-toz, st-hxk, st-r3t — earlier filter holder fixes
- User suggestion 2026-05-11: "extending the dovetail to the bottom of the model and curving the top of the dovetail so the receiving cutout doesn't need support"
- OpenSCAD `linear_extrude(scale=0)` doc: https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/OpenSCAD_User_Manual/Transformations#linear_extrude

title: goBlu filter holder: keep dovetails on inner faces when pod_gap > 0 (st-hxk follow-up)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-phvw
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-11T14:31:31Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-arnhe
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Problem

st-hxk (commit `a118873`) added `pod_gap` and `slicer_3up` preset, but the dovetails are globally suppressed when `pod_gap > 0`. This contradicts the user's clarified spec: when pods are spaced out for slicer-separable export, **dovetails must remain on inner faces** so the printed pods can physically mate post-print. Outer faces of end pods stay flat (capped) — that's the existing end-cap suppression logic and it's correct for both gap modes.

Net: the `slicer_3up` preset currently produces 3 spaced pods with no interconnect features at all. User can print them as separate parts but has no way to physically connect them.

## Root cause in code

`models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad:149`:

```scad
dovetails_active = dovetail_enabled && pod_gap == 0;
```

The `&& pod_gap == 0` clause is wrong. The end-cap suppression in `array()` (`expose_left_slot = (i > 0)`, `expose_right_tongue = (i < pod_count - 1)`) ALREADY makes outer pods present flat outer faces — that logic is independent of `pod_gap` and correctly handles the inner-face-only-on-end-pods requirement. The global suppression is double-disabling and is what's producing the no-dovetail output.

## Fix

1. Change line 149 to:
   ```scad
   dovetails_active = dovetail_enabled;
   ```
2. Update the `slicer_3up` preset label (line 131) from `"3-up for slicer (10mm gap, dovetails off)"` → `"3-up for slicer (10mm gap, dovetails on inner faces)"`.
3. Update the file-header comment block (the `// === pod_gap and the slicer_3up preset (st-hxk) ===` section around lines 78–94) to describe the corrected behavior: gap is print-time air space only; dovetails stay on every inner face; end pods retain flat outer faces; user prints pods independently then slides them together post-print to engage the dovetails.

## Invariants update

`models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.invariants.py`: assert that for **both** `stock_3up` and `slicer_3up` presets, the rendered geometry contains exactly **4** dovetail features for `pod_count=3`:
- Leftmost pod: 1 tongue (+X face, inner). Outer -X face flat.
- Middle pod: 1 slot (-X) + 1 tongue (+X) = 2 features.
- Rightmost pod: 1 slot (-X face, inner). Outer +X face flat.

Total = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4. Same in both gap modes (the only thing that changes between modes is the X-spacing of the pods, not their dovetail features).

If the invariants script can't easily count dovetail features directly, an acceptable proxy: BBOX X-extent equals `pod_count * pod_w + (pod_count - 1) * pod_gap` (which the existing PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX should already track), AND the per-pod count is correct for end-cap vs middle (could check via rendering individual pod presets and diffing).

## Verify before closing

1. Load `/models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm` in the dev gallery (or Vercel preview after merge), pick the `slicer_3up` preset, confirm visually that:
   - 3 pods are visible
   - There's clear air space between adjacent pods
   - Inner faces of all 3 pods show dovetail features (tongue on left-of-middle / right-of-middle; slots facing them)
   - Outer faces of left and right end pods are FLAT (no protruding tongue, no slot recess)
2. Verify `stock_3up` preset is **unchanged** from `a118873` — pods touch, end caps, dovetails mate.
3. Export both presets to STL; load both in a slicer:
   - `stock_3up`: 1 connected body (or treated as such by the slicer).
   - `slicer_3up`: 3 disjoint bodies, manipulable independently.
4. Verify `pod_gap` parameter appears in the web UI's param rail (it should — the `@param` annotation is already correct; if it doesn't, it's a parser / cache issue, not a model issue).

## Refs

- st-hxk (closed, shipped as `a118873`) — partial fix that this is correcting
- st-r3t (closed, `0921e35`) — base implementation
- `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad:149` — the offending line
- `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad:131` — `slicer_3up` preset label needing relabeling
- User feedback in mayor session 2026-05-11: "outer pods should only have dovetails on the inner face and the middle pod should have two dovetails"
notes:
## 2026-05-11 mayor — bundle st-yuu fix

While in pod() for the dovetails_active simplification, please also apply the fix in st-yuu (slot-subtraction cuts outside the pod body — latent bug since st-r3t). Both are small one-line changes on the same module. Shipping together avoids merge conflicts and lets one verification pass cover both: render the single_pod preset and confirm a complete dovetail PAIR is visible (+X tongue protruding, -X slot recessed) on the same pod.

st-yuu fix summary: in pod() module's slot subtraction branch, delete the spurious `rotate([0, 0, 180])` line. Tongue branch stays as-is.

title: goBlu filter holder: add pod_gap param so multi-pod export is slicer-separable
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-7nxe
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-11T14:08:58Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-dano6
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Problem

User loaded the stock `goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad` (preset `stock_3up`) STL into the slicer and the 3 pods come in as a single body. Per-pod manipulation (rotation, repositioning, applying different supports per pod) is what the user expected to be able to do — that's the whole point of designing it modular.

Today the layout is "pods flush at `cell_spacing = pod_w`" (st-r3t array module). Pods share their +X / -X face geometry through the dovetail mating, so the slicer can't easily separate them as independent objects.

## Fix

Add a `pod_gap` parameter so the user can render the multi-pod array with visible spacing between adjacent pods, suitable for "separate parts" export to the slicer.

### Spec

1. **New `@param`**: `pod_gap` (number, min=0, max=50, step=1, unit=mm, group=array, label="Gap between adjacent pods (for slicer export)"). Default = **0** (current behavior — touching, dovetails mate).
2. **Layout change**: in `pod_x(i)`, the spacing per index becomes `pod_w + pod_gap` instead of `pod_w`. Adjust the centring offset so the array stays centred on X=0.
3. **Dovetail handling when gap > 0**: suppress dovetails (both tongue + slot) on all pods when `pod_gap > 0`. Rationale: a non-zero gap means the user wants slicer-friendly separated parts, NOT a physically-mating assembly. Leaving the dovetail tongues protruding into the gap creates confusing geometry (especially if `pod_gap < dovetail_depth`, where the tongue would visibly overhang into the next pod's air space).
4. **Outer-cap logic** when `pod_gap == 0`: unchanged (leftmost pod's -X slot capped, rightmost pod's +X tongue suppressed).

### Presets

- **`stock_3up`** (existing): keep `pod_gap = 0`. Assembled view, dovetails active. Production geometry.
- **`single_pod`** (existing): unchanged.
- **`flush_3up`** (existing): unchanged.
- **NEW `slicer_3up`**: `pod_gap = 10`, all other params same as `stock_3up`, dovetails effectively suppressed by the gap-driven branch. Label: "3-up for slicer (10mm gap, dovetails off)". This is what the user loads when they want separate parts.

### Geometry

At `pod_gap = 10`, `pod_count = 3`, defaults otherwise:
- `cell_spacing = pod_w + pod_gap = 121 + 10 = 131`
- Pod X-centres: −131, 0, +131
- Total X span: `pod_count * pod_w + (pod_count - 1) * pod_gap = 3*121 + 2*10 = 383`

### Invariants update

`models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.invariants.py` needs:
1. Update `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` derivation to account for `pod_gap`: `X = pod_count * pod_w + (pod_count - 1) * pod_gap`.
2. Add assertion: when `pod_gap > 0`, no pod's bounding-box overlaps any other pod's bounding-box. Cleanest way is to render the `slicer_3up` preset and verify the STL contains `pod_count` disjoint connected components (or, simpler, assert that the X-extent of the assembly equals `pod_count * pod_w + (pod_count - 1) * pod_gap`).
3. Add assertion: when `pod_gap == 0`, the layout matches the current (touching) form — guards against accidental regression of the default.

## Acceptance

1. `pnpm test` (or whatever runs the invariants pipeline — see AGENTS.md) passes for both `stock_3up` (gap=0, touching, dovetails) and `slicer_3up` (gap=10, separated, no dovetails).
2. The web UI gallery at `/models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm` lets the user toggle between presets and see the gap take effect in the live render.
3. Export `slicer_3up.stl` and load it in a slicer; per-pod selection / move works without splitting bodies manually.
4. Stock production geometry (assembled, dovetails active) is unchanged — diff against `stock_3up` STL from st-r3t commit `0921e35` should be empty.

## Out of scope

- Print-orientation rotation (pods stay back-face-down on the bed; user can rotate in slicer if they want).
- Auto-injecting individual base rafts per pod (slicer handles that).

## Refs

- st-r3t (closed, shipped as `0921e35`) — base implementation
- `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad:131` — `pod_x(i)` (the spacing function to update)
- `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad:226-235` — `array()` module (dovetail-suppression branch needs the `pod_gap > 0` case)
- `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.invariants.py` — sibling invariants check
notes:
## 2026-05-11 mayor — spec correction (dovetails MUST stay)

Original spec was wrong on the dovetail handling. Corrected:

**When `pod_gap > 0`:** Pods rendered with gap, AND **every pod retains both its dovetail tongue (+X) and slot (-X)**. NO outer-cap suppression. Every printed pod is a complete, self-contained unit. The user prints them as N separate objects, then slides them together by hand after the print — the dovetails do the physical mating at assembly time.

**When `pod_gap == 0`:** Current behavior. Pods touch flush, end pods cap (leftmost -X slot filled, rightmost +X tongue suppressed), dovetails mate through coincident geometry. This is the 'assembled view' for renders/preview only.

**Why the correction:** Modular pods exist so the user can print 1 → 3 pods individually, then physically assemble via the slide-together dovetails. The dovetails are load-bearing for the design intent (they take lateral / VHB-peel loads). Suppressing them in the slicer-export view defeats the modularity.

**`slicer_3up` preset:** `pod_gap = 10` (must be > `dovetail_depth = 6` so tongues don't visually intrude into the air gap), dovetails enabled, no end-caps.

**Invariants update:** when `pod_gap > 0`, assert every pod has BOTH tongue and slot features present (i.e., 6 dovetail features total for pod_count=3, not 4 as the end-capped layout). When `pod_gap == 0`, assert the current end-capped count (4 features for pod_count=3).

Everything else in the original spec stands.
## 2026-05-11 mayor — spec re-correction (KEEP end-cap suppression)

I overcorrected. Final, correct spec for the dovetail logic in BOTH gap-zero and gap-positive branches:

- **Leftmost pod**: dovetail tongue on +X face (inner, faces middle). **Flat outer -X face (no slot).**
- **Middle pod(s)**: dovetail slot on -X face AND tongue on +X face (both inner faces).
- **Rightmost pod**: dovetail slot on -X face (inner, faces middle). **Flat outer +X face (no tongue).**

Feature count for pod_count=3 = 1 + 2 + 1 = **4 dovetail features total**, in BOTH gap modes.

The current `expose_left_slot = (i > 0)` and `expose_right_tongue = (i < pod_count - 1)` logic in array() is correct and must be **preserved unchanged** when pod_gap > 0. Only the spacing changes:

```scad
function pod_x(i) = (i - (pod_count - 1) / 2) * (pod_w + pod_gap);
```

**Invariants:** in both gap modes, assert exactly 4 dovetail features for pod_count=3 (NOT 6 as I said in the prior note — disregard that). Per-pod: leftmost has 1, middles have 2, rightmost has 1.

Ignore the previous note's claim about '6 features when gap > 0'. The end-cap logic stays.

title: goBlu RV water filter holder — side-mount, 3x90mm cylindrical pockets, modular 3-up
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-uq0z
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-11T03:51:26Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-4h32e
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Design a parametric, side-mount **RV water filter holder** for a goBlu 3-filter assembly. Replaces the stock top-mount blue bracket. Holder cradles the lower ~115 mm of each cylindrical filter housing; mounts to a vertical RV interior surface via VHB tape on its back wall.

## Filter dimensions (load-bearing — confirmed by user)

- **3 identical stainless filter housings**, vertical, in a row.
- **Housing diameter:** 90 mm (cylindrical body, below the stainless collar at top).
- **Housing body height:** 118 mm (collar-bottom to housing-bottom).
- **Gap between adjacent housings:** 30 mm (surface-to-surface).
- **Center-to-center spacing:** 120 mm.
- Total cylinder-axis-to-cylinder-axis span = 240 mm; outermost cylinder surfaces 330 mm apart.

Reference images (the existing top-mount blue bracket being replaced): see goBlu product pages — top bracket is L-section with two parallel slotted plates that the collars bolt through. Plumbing (PEX in/out + a side valve on the outlet filter) is routed at the top through the existing bracket and is **out of scope** for this holder. The new holder supports the bottom half of the housings only; the user keeps the top plumbing as-is or rehosts it separately.

## Design decisions (user-confirmed)

1. **Pocket depth: full ~115 mm**, right up under the collar. Max anti-sway support; cylinders are nearly fully hidden in the holder up to the collar. Leave 2–3 mm headroom between top of pocket and underside of collar so the holder doesn't bind on the collar lip.
2. **Bottom: open through-hole** (drain). Each pocket is a ring, not a cup. Bottom inner diameter matches the pocket ID (or slightly smaller to leave a 3–5 mm lip the cylinder rests on if the user ever flips the assembly — discuss). Wet-safe per the stuff conventions (`aquor_bib_drip_deflector`, `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm` precedents).
3. **Modular 3-up**: three identical single-cylinder pods that mate together. Each pod ~ 120 mm wide × 90+wall depth × 115+ring-thickness tall. Fits any common printer (≤256 mm bed). Pods clip / bolt / dovetail together — see open question on interconnect below.
4. **Top retention: snug slide-fit with radiused / chamfered top rim.** Pocket ID = cylinder OD + clearance (suggest 1.0–1.5 mm radial slop, i.e. pocket ID 91–93 mm). Top edge of pocket gets a generous lead-in chamfer/radius (suggest 3–5 mm radius) so user can guide the cylinder in without catching, and so the edge can't cut a finger or scratch the stainless.

## Mounting

- **Back wall** of the holder is the VHB mounting surface. Must be:
  - **< 10 mm thick** (so the assembly doesn't stand off the RV wall too far).
  - **Flat** (no ribs, fillets, or holes on the back face — VHB needs a continuous flat bond area). Aim for ≥ 70% bond coverage across the back.
- VHB tape stays on the back face; no fasteners through the wall (user-stated).
- Gravity load = 3 × (filter housing + water + any cartridge) hanging in the pockets. Estimate ~3–4 kg total. Load is pure shear on the VHB (best case for VHB).
- Lateral load (someone bumping a filter): the pocket walls + top retention take this; small moment on the VHB.

## Other constraints / conventions

- **Material**: target PETG or ASA. RV interior, intermittent moisture. PLA acceptable in shaded interior installs but document the temperature/UV caveat.
- **Print orientation**: pockets up (axis vertical), back face down. The pocket cylindrical walls print as concentric perimeters, the back face is the build plate face. The top-rim radius will be in-plane and prints cleanly.
- Follow the existing stuff `.scad` conventions:
  - SPDX header, copyright.
  - Load-bearing invariants block in the file header (this spec → header comments).
  - `include <BOSL2/std.scad>` + `BOSL2/rounding.scad`.
  - `@param` annotations on every user-tunable, with `min`/`max`/`step`/`unit`/`group`/`label`.
  - At least one `@preset` capturing the stock goBlu config (90 mm × 118 mm × 30 mm gap).
  - `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX = [W, D, H]` at defaults.
  - Sibling `<stem>.invariants.py` asserting machine-checkable claims (BBOX, pocket count, pocket ID = cylinder OD + 2·clearance, back wall thickness ≤ 10 mm).

## Suggested model name

`goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad` — follows the naming pattern of `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad`.

## Open design questions (for the polecat to resolve, not the user)

1. **Pod-to-pod interconnect**: dovetail slide-fit, M3/M4 bolts through aligned holes, or a snap clip? Recommended: **vertical-slide dovetail** on each side face (one male, one female; end pods have a flat cap on the outer face). No fasteners → cleaner aesthetic + faster to assemble. Render both single pod and 3-up assembly previews.
2. **Wall thicknesses**:
   - Back (VHB) wall: 7–8 mm (≤ 10 mm spec).
   - Side walls between adjacent pods: design so the **30 mm gap** is filled by **wall + wall** of two adjacent pods. Each pod side wall = 15 mm, pods touch flush. Or alternatively 7 mm side walls + an air gap — but flush is cleaner and stronger.
   - Front wall (cylinder face): 5–7 mm.
   - Bottom ring: 5 mm thick, ID matched to pocket ID (drain-through).
3. **Top-rim lead-in**: a 3–5 mm radius is generous but burns ~5 mm of pocket depth at the top. If pocket-depth = 115 mm and lead-in = 5 mm, the cylinder's bottom 110 mm is in true pocket and the top 5 mm is in the radiused taper. Document the trade-off; tunable via `top_lead_in_r` param.
4. **VHB pad layout**: leave the entire back face flat for one continuous strip, OR provide two thinner strips (top and bottom) with a recessed channel between for routing zip ties as a backup restraint. Polecat's call; recommend continuous flat back for simplicity, mention zip-tie option in invariants header as a future-revision note.

## Done = …

1. `models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.scad` checked in, renders cleanly, opens in the gallery web UI at `/models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm`.
2. Sibling `goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm.invariants.py` passes (`python3 scripts/invariants.py models/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm`).
3. STL exported under `renders/goblu_filter_holder_3x90mm/stock.stl` for inspection / slicing.
4. Stock preset renders match the spec (3 pods, 91 mm pocket ID, 115 mm pocket depth, 7 mm back wall, etc.).
5. PR body shows render thumbnails of: (a) single pod, (b) 3-up assembled, (c) cross-section through one pocket showing the lead-in radius + drain-through bottom.
6. Per-pod print orientation (back face down, axis up) confirmed buildable on a 256 × 256 bed.

## Refs

- `models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` — closest cradle-array precedent (open-front C-rings + base drains).
- `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` — single cylindrical pocket precedent.
- `models/aquor_bib_drip_deflector.scad` — drain / wet-environment precedent.
- AGENTS.md — render + invariants pipeline.
- User's reference images saved at `/home/seanoconnor/gt/mayor/` mayor-session transcript 2026-05-10 21:40 PT (goBlu product photos).

title: [P2][feat] Add Disney Ear Hanger model with attribution to vendored MakerWorld design
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-zcax
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-01T18:54:14Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Add the Disney Ear Hanger model to `models/` with proper attribution to the original MakerWorld designer. This is a vendored external design, not original work.

## Source

- **Local SCAD file:** `/home/seanoconnor/Ear Hanger.scad` (downloaded; user provided the path).
- **Original publication:** https://makerworld.com/en/models/551375-disney-ear-hanger?from=search#profileId-469918
- **First lines of the file** (for context):

  \`\`\`scad
  \$fn = 50;
  hangerLength = 28;
  padding = 0.1;
  earHanger();
  module earHanger() { ... }
  \`\`\`

  Small parametric model — couple of params, single `earHanger()` module.

## Steps

### 1. Fetch attribution metadata

Open the MakerWorld URL with WebFetch (or `curl`) to capture:
- Original creator's name / handle (the `profileId-469918` in the URL maps to the user; the page's profile section shows the display name)
- License the creator chose (MakerWorld defaults are usually CC-BY, CC-BY-SA, CC-BY-NC, or similar — read the page's license badge)
- Original model title + a one-line description
- Any reference to print orientation or install context

If the page can't be fetched (auth required), record the URL + profile-id + capture date in the header and proceed; user can fill in name later.

### 2. Vendor the file

Copy `/home/seanoconnor/Ear Hanger.scad` → `models/disney_ear_hanger.scad` (snake_case per existing convention; see `models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad`, `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, etc).

Don't reformat the body. **Keep the original code as-is**; only prepend an attribution header.

### 3. Add the attribution header

Top of the new file:

\`\`\`scad
// SPDX-License-Identifier: <whatever the MakerWorld page declares>
// Vendored model — original by <author name / handle> on MakerWorld.
// Source: https://makerworld.com/en/models/551375-disney-ear-hanger
// Imported: 2026-05-01
//
// We did not author this model. Contributions to this file should be
// minimal and respect the upstream author's design intent. If the
// model needs structural changes, prefer reaching out to the upstream
// author or forking with explicit derivative attribution.
//
// Print orientation: <document if knowable from MakerWorld page; else
// note "see upstream listing">.
// Install context: ear hanger — likely a wall-mount or shelf-edge clip.
//   Polecat: confirm with user before adding install-side fillets,
//   keying, or surface treatments. (See mayor memory
//   feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts.md.)
\`\`\`

Mirror the format of existing model headers (see `models/gridfinity_bin.scad:1-15` for a working vendored-model attribution example — that one credits kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad).

If the MakerWorld license is one of the CC variants, the SPDX header should match (e.g. \`CC-BY-4.0\`, \`CC-BY-NC-4.0\`, \`CC-BY-SA-4.0\`). If it's a custom MakerWorld license, write \`LicenseRef-MakerWorld-<short-tag>\` and link the page in the header. **Don't claim copyright.**

### 4. Add the invariants sidecar

Per `AGENTS.md`, every model in `models/` needs a `<stem>.invariants.py` sidecar. For a vendored model where we don't have strong claims about geometry, a minimal sidecar is fine:

\`\`\`python
# models/disney_ear_hanger.invariants.py
from scripts.invariants import Failure, as_default_params, expect_connected_solids

def check(ctx):
    failures = []
    # Vendored model — no original invariants. Keep the topology check so
    # render regressions still flag.
    failures.extend(expect_connected_solids(ctx, 1) or [])
    return failures
\`\`\`

If the rendered STL turns out to be multi-body (e.g., the hanger has separate clip + bracket pieces), bump \`n=1\` to whatever it actually is.

### 5. Render + verify

\`\`\`bash
python3 scripts/render-all.py models/disney_ear_hanger.scad   # or whatever the local renderer expects
python3 scripts/check-invariants.py disney_ear_hanger
\`\`\`

If the render fails or the invariants flag, debug. If it renders cleanly, the CI render job will commit the thumbnail back to the PR branch automatically.

### 6. Update gallery / docs (only if needed)

The gallery at `app/page.tsx` (or wherever) reads from `models/` automatically per `AGENTS.md` (\"the gallery pulls tile thumbnails from `renders/<stem>/top.png`\"). Confirm the new model shows up in the dev server (\`npm run dev\`); if it doesn't, find the gallery's model registry and add an entry.

If there's a `models/README.md` or similar docs surface listing models, add a one-liner with the attribution link.

### 7. Lint / build / test

\`\`\`bash
npm run build
npm test
\`\`\`

All green.

## Don't

- Don't reformat or refactor the original SCAD body. Header only.
- Don't claim copyright on the model. Attribution must point at the upstream author.
- Don't change print orientation, parameter defaults, or geometry without explicit user confirmation (see \`feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts.md\`).
- Don't pollute the branch with unrelated tracked-file changes (\`pnpm-lock.yaml\`, \`docs/plans/*\`, \`docs/mocks/*\`, etc.) per umbrella \`hq-ynjpo\`.
- Don't commit the source download path (\`/home/seanoconnor/Ear Hanger.scad\`); only the in-repo copy.

## Acceptance

- \`models/disney_ear_hanger.scad\` exists with attribution header pointing at the MakerWorld URL + creator + license.
- \`models/disney_ear_hanger.invariants.py\` exists.
- \`exports/disney_ear_hanger.stl\` renders cleanly (let CI handle this if local OpenSCAD isn't available).
- \`scripts/check-invariants.py disney_ear_hanger\` passes (or CI passes after merge).
- Model appears in the gallery's tile listing.
- npm run lint / build / test green.
- PR description includes the MakerWorld URL + the creator's display name + the chosen license.

## References

- Source SCAD: \`/home/seanoconnor/Ear Hanger.scad\`
- MakerWorld URL: https://makerworld.com/en/models/551375-disney-ear-hanger?from=search#profileId-469918
- Existing vendored-model precedent: \`models/gridfinity_bin.scad\` (attributes kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad)
- Invariants pattern: \`AGENTS.md\` § \"Per-model invariants\"
- Print/install orientation guidance: mayor memory \`feedback_confirm_install_orientation_for_wallmount_parts.md\`

title: [P2][feat] Vendor gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad + parametric gridfinity_bin model
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-gsye
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-28T02:58:07Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Vendor **`kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad`** as a pinned library and ship a parametric Gridfinity bin model that wraps it. Lets users generate custom bins for their workshop drawers / Multiboard surfaces directly from the stuff catalog.

## Why this library

Brief mayor-side research (2026-04-28):

- **`kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad`** — 2.1k stars, MIT, actively maintained (v2.0.0 released 2025-09-01). Multi-file: `gridfinity-rebuilt-bins.scad`, `gridfinity-rebuilt-baseplate.scad`, `gridfinity-rebuilt-lite.scad`. One dep: `threads-scad` (**already vendored in `libs/threads-scad/`**). MIT license slots cleanly into stuff's existing permissive-libs set.
- Alternatives considered: `yawkat/GridFlock` (77 stars, baseplate-focused), `wromijn/openscad-gridfinity-block` (43 stars, smaller scope). Neither is the right primary.

Source: https://github.com/kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad

## What to build

### 1. Vendor the library

Add to `libs/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad/` pinning the latest stable tag (v2.0.0 or whatever the current release is; verify and pin the matching SHA — same pattern as the existing `libs/README.md` table). Use `git subtree` or a direct copy with the SHA recorded — match the pattern the existing libs use (check how `BOSL2`, `QuackWorks`, `NopSCADlib` were vendored; mirror it).

Update `libs/README.md`:

- Add a new row to the table: library name, purpose ("Gridfinity bins, baseplates, and lite variants"), pinned SHA, license (MIT)
- Add a new section below the others (after `BOSL2`, before any `**License`-flagged libraries) describing the import paths users would use:
  ```scad
  use <gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad/gridfinity-rebuilt-bins.scad>;
  ```
- Note that `threads-scad` is already vendored separately and is what gridfinity-rebuilt's threaded-hole feature consumes.

### 2. Parametric bin model

Add `models/gridfinity_bin.scad`. Mirror the structure of an existing model (e.g. `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`) for:

- License + copyright header
- Module-level comment block describing what the bin does and any historic-presets list
- `@param` annotations for every user-tunable parameter so stuff's parameter UI can render them
- A single render at the bottom that generates one bin

**Parameters to expose** (don't add anything not in this list without a reason — start small):

- `grid_x` — bin footprint in U columns (default 1, range 1–6)
- `grid_y` — bin footprint in U rows (default 1, range 1–6)
- `height_units` — bin height in 7mm units (default 3, range 2–8). Common picks: 2u (shallow), 3u (default), 6u (deep).
- `divisions_x` — internal compartments in X (default 1, range 1–6)
- `divisions_y` — internal compartments in Y (default 1, range 1–6)
- `enable_scoop` — front lip that helps scoop small parts out (default true)
- `enable_label_tab` — angled tab for label maker tape (default true)
- `magnet_holes` — refined magnet hole pattern in the base (default false; toggling adds 6mm-magnet sockets)
- `screw_holes` — screw holes through magnet sockets (default false; requires `magnet_holes`)
- `wall_thickness` — outer wall thickness in mm (default the library's stock; document the library's chosen value)

If the library's API names differ, translate at the wrapper layer — keep these parameter names stable for the stuff UI. Document the mapping at the top of the .scad in a comment block.

Don't expose every internal knob the library has. The above set covers ≈95% of real bin needs.

### 3. Catalog entry

Add to `lib/models/catalog.ts`:

```typescript
{
  stem: "gridfinity_bin",
  categoryId: "<the right existing category — workshop / storage / containers / whatever exists; if nothing fits, add a new 'gridfinity' category>",
  blurb: "Parametric Gridfinity bin: pick a footprint, height, compartments, and base options. Snaps onto any standard 42mm Gridfinity baseplate.",
}
```

If a Gridfinity-specific category makes sense (e.g. for a future baseplate model), add it; otherwise file under whatever existing category fits. Don't proliferate categories for a single model — that's a sign of premature taxonomy.

### 4. Optional invariants test

Existing models like `cylindrical_holder_slot` have an `.invariants.py` companion. If that's the project pattern for shape contracts, add `models/gridfinity_bin.invariants.py` that asserts a render at default parameters has the expected bounding-box dimensions (1U × 1U × 21mm — 42×42×21 with the standard tolerance band). Skip if the project doesn't enforce this for new models.

### 5. Verify render + UI

- Render `models/gridfinity_bin.scad` at default parameters using the project's existing render command (mirror what other models use). Confirm the STL output is sane (~21mm tall, ~42×42mm footprint).
- Open the model page locally (`pnpm dev` or whatever the existing flow is) and confirm:
  - All exposed @param parameters render in the UI
  - Changing `grid_x` and `grid_y` produces a re-render that matches expectations
  - The accessories section (recently added in `st-bao`) is empty for this model — no crash, no header

### 6. Tests

- If `lib/models/discover.test.ts` walks all models, ensure `gridfinity_bin` appears in the catalog list with the expected blurb + category
- Existing render snapshot tests, if any, get a default-params row added

## Out of scope

- **No baseplate model.** Bin is the obvious first add; baseplate is its own future bead.
- **No Lite variant.** Same — separate model, separate bead.
- **No magnet specs.** The library's defaults handle 6mm × 2mm magnets — common pick. If users need other sizes, follow-up bead.
- **No accessories link.** The bin doesn't ship with accessories yet; the M:N accessory plumbing from `st-bao` already handles "no accessories for this model" cleanly.
- **No "stack-of-bins" or multi-bin compositions.** One bin per render.
- **No Multiboard mounting variant.** Gridfinity bins live on Gridfinity baseplates by design; mounting onto Multiboard is a different problem domain.

## Acceptance criteria

- `libs/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad/` exists with pinned SHA documented
- `libs/README.md` table + section added
- `models/gridfinity_bin.scad` exists with `@param` frontmatter for all listed parameters
- `lib/models/catalog.ts` has the new entry
- Render at default params produces a valid STL with the right bounding box
- Stuff UI shows the model card on the catalog page; detail page renders all parameters
- `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test` all green
- Linting / formatting matches existing model files

## Completion

Open MR titled `feat(models): vendor kennetek/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad + parametric gridfinity_bin`. Refinery reviews. After merge, follow-up beads can add a baseplate variant, lite variant, or any specialized lid types.
acceptance_criteria: libs/gridfinity-rebuilt-openscad/ vendored at pinned SHA; libs/README.md updated; models/gridfinity_bin.scad with @param frontmatter for grid_x/y, height_units, divisions_x/y, enable_scoop, enable_label_tab, magnet_holes, screw_holes, wall_thickness; lib/models/catalog.ts entry; default-params render produces ~42×42×21mm STL; UI shows the model + parameter editor; lint/typecheck/build/test green

title: [P2][feat] Model accessories — static STL files surfaced on model detail page (M:N)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-79se
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-28T00:54:24Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Add **model accessories** to stuff: static STL files that get listed alongside compatible parametric models on the model detail page so users can download both with one trip. The seed example: `openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl` (mounts the cylindrical holder via the openconnect modular system) shows up on the `cylindrical_holder_slot` model page.

User decisions baked in (already made):
- Accessories are **external static STLs**, not parametric stuff models. No need to recreate as `.scad`.
- Relation is **M:N** — an accessory can attach to many models.
- **No parametric co-variation** — accessories are fixed-size files.

## Read first

- `lib/models/discover.ts` and `lib/models/catalog.ts` — the existing pattern; mirror it for accessories
- `app/models/[slug]/page.tsx` — where the new "Accessories" UI lands
- `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` — the seed target model
- `~/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl` — the seed accessory file (lives in mayor's home dir; copy into the repo as part of this bead)

## What to build

### 1. Storage convention

- Static STLs live at `accessories/<slug>.stl` (flat directory; one file per accessory).
- One central catalog at `lib/accessories/catalog.ts` defines each accessory's metadata + compatible-models list. Mirror the shape of `lib/models/catalog.ts`:

```typescript
export interface AccessoryCatalogEntry {
  slug: string;
  title: string;
  blurb: string;          // one-line description for the model page
  stlPath: string;        // repo-relative, e.g. "accessories/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl"
  compatibleModels: string[]; // model stems (NOT slugs) — match the .scad filename without extension
  attribution?: string;   // optional: original source / designer / license
}

export const ACCESSORIES: AccessoryCatalogEntry[] = [...];
```

Keep the catalog in TypeScript (not JSON) so refactors get type-checked when model stems get renamed.

### 2. Discovery + helpers

`lib/accessories/discover.ts` mirroring `lib/models/discover.ts`:

```typescript
export interface AccessoryEntry extends AccessoryCatalogEntry {
  /** Resolved download URL, e.g. "/accessories/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl". */
  downloadUrl: string;
  /** Bytes on disk; useful for "16.4 MB" labels in the UI. */
  fileSize: number;
}

export async function listAccessories(): Promise<AccessoryEntry[]>;
export async function getAccessoryBySlug(slug: string): Promise<AccessoryEntry | null>;
export async function getAccessoriesForModel(modelStem: string): Promise<AccessoryEntry[]>;
export async function getModelsForAccessory(slug: string): Promise<string[]>; // returns model stems
```

Walks `accessories/` to verify each catalog entry's STL exists (warn loudly if a catalog entry references a missing file). Compute `fileSize` via `fs.stat` so the UI can show it.

### 3. Static-file serving

STL files at `accessories/*.stl` need to be browser-downloadable. Two options:

- **(a) Move to `public/accessories/`** — Next.js serves `public/` automatically; the URL becomes `/accessories/<slug>.stl`. Simplest. Caveat: moves binary STLs into `public/` which is unusual for the existing layout.
- **(b) Add a Next.js route handler `app/api/accessories/[slug]/route.ts`** that streams the file from `accessories/`. More code; keeps binaries out of `public/`.

Pick (a) unless the codebase already has a strong "no binaries in public" convention. Document the choice in a code comment.

### 4. UI on `/models/[slug]/page.tsx`

Add an "Accessories" section after the existing model preview/params/download block. Show only when `getAccessoriesForModel(stem)` returns ≥1 entry. Each row:

- Title (bold)
- Blurb (one line)
- File size (small, dimmed)
- Download button — `<a href={downloadUrl} download>` with the existing button styling
- Optional attribution line if present

Heading should be small/secondary — accessories complement the main model, they don't compete with it. Follow the existing visual hierarchy on the page.

If no accessories, render nothing (no empty state, no header).

### 5. Seed: openconnect-flush-mid-coin

- Copy `/home/seanoconnor/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl` into the repo at the chosen path (`public/accessories/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl` if option (a), `accessories/openconnect-flush-mid-coin.stl` if option (b)).
- Add an entry to `lib/accessories/catalog.ts`:
  ```typescript
  {
    slug: "openconnect-flush-mid-coin",
    title: "Openconnect Flush Mid Coin",
    blurb: "Mounts cylindrical holders to a multiboard via the openconnect modular system.",
    stlPath: "<chosen path>",
    compatibleModels: ["cylindrical_holder_slot"],
  }
  ```
  Adjust `title` and `blurb` if needed — pick something concise that reads cleanly under the model card. Verify the actual model stem (`cylindrical_holder_slot` is in `models/`).

Commit the STL file with the catalog entry.

### 6. Tests

- `lib/accessories/discover.test.ts` mirroring the existing models test:
  - `listAccessories` returns the seed entry with `fileSize > 0` and a valid `downloadUrl`
  - `getAccessoriesForModel("cylindrical_holder_slot")` returns the seed entry
  - `getAccessoriesForModel("nonexistent_model")` returns `[]`
  - `getModelsForAccessory("openconnect-flush-mid-coin")` returns `["cylindrical_holder_slot"]`
  - A catalog entry referencing a missing STL surfaces a clear error or warning
- A page-level test (using the existing test pattern in this repo) confirming the cylindrical-holder model page renders the accessory section with the seed entry.

## Out of scope

- **Dedicated `/accessories` index page** and `/accessories/[slug]` detail pages. Useful future work for the M:N reverse direction; defer to a follow-up bead.
- **Preview thumbnails** for accessories. The download link is enough for v1.
- **Parametric co-variation** — explicitly user-rejected; accessories are fixed.
- **Recreating openconnect-flush-mid-coin as a `.scad` parametric model.** Explicitly user-rejected.
- **Attribution scraping / license inference.** Manual catalog entry only.
- **Accessory categories / filtering / search.** Not yet useful with one accessory.
- **Bulk "download model + all accessories as a zip"** — separate links per item is enough for v1.

## Acceptance criteria

- `lib/accessories/{catalog,discover}.ts` + tests exist.
- The four `getAccessoriesForModel` / `getModelsForAccessory` / `listAccessories` / `getAccessoryBySlug` helpers exist with covered tests.
- `app/models/[slug]/page.tsx` renders an "Accessories" section when the model has compatible accessories; renders nothing when it doesn't.
- The cylindrical-holder slot model page shows the openconnect-flush-mid-coin accessory with a working download link (the file actually downloads when clicked locally).
- The STL is committed to the repo at the chosen path.
- `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test` all green.
- Linting / formatting matches the existing codebase.

## Completion

Open MR titled `feat(accessories): static STL accessories with per-model M:N relation`. Refinery reviews. After merge, future accessory additions are a one-PR-per-accessory pattern: drop STL into `public/accessories/`, add catalog entry, ship.
acceptance_criteria: lib/accessories/{catalog,discover}.ts + tests; 4 helpers (list/getBySlug/forModel/forAccessory) covered; /models/[slug] page renders Accessories section conditionally; openconnect-flush-mid-coin seeded and downloadable from cylindrical_holder_slot page; STL committed; lint/typecheck/build/test green

title: [revise/v5] Spraycan carrier: prune legacy params + add separate handle crossbar diameter
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-d6ct
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-26T02:52:46Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Why

The carrier model has accumulated vestigial params from the arch-style iteration history (st-qt4 ogive, st-djm semicircular, st-kyz/st-4ac/st-y1q squared). The squared-cylindrical handle is the settled design; the legacy paths are dormant code with their own params, presets, and tests adding cognitive load.

User also wants to tune the handle's grip thickness independently of the support post thickness — currently \`handle_post_d\` controls both.

## Scope

### 1. Delete legacy arch-style code + params

The following are now confirmed dormant — \`arch_style\` defaults to \`squared\` and the user has settled on it. Remove:

**Modules:**
- \`_arch_ogive\`, \`_arch_ogive_band_2d\`, \`_arch_ogive_filled_2d\`
- \`_arch_semicircle\`
- \`_post_rect\` (only used by ogive/semicircular)

**The \`handle()\` dispatcher** (\`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad\` around line 336): collapse to just call \`_handle_squared_cyl\`. No more \`if (arch_style == ...)\` branches.

**Params to remove:**
- \`arch_style\` (the enum itself; only one option remains, so the param has no purpose)
- \`arch_point_offset\` (ogive-only k-factor)
- \`handle_post_w\` (legacy ogive/semicircular post width X)
- \`handle_thickness\` (legacy ogive/semicircular post thickness Y)
- \`corner_fillet\` (already labeled "no-op since st-y1q")

**Presets:**
- Remove \`legacy-ogive\` preset
- Remove \`legacy-semicircular\` preset
- Update \`stock\` preset to drop the deleted params

**Header comments:**
- Strip the "Arch styles" section (the bullet list at lines ~28-44 of the file describing squared/ogive/semicircular)
- Strip the references to ogive/semicircular self-supporting analysis
- Strip the legacy-arch invariants
- Keep the load-bearing invariants for the squared flow (Y=0 corridor, post position math, cell spacing)

**Anything that derives from these params** (e.g., \`arch_apex_rise\` ternary that branches on \`arch_style\`) — collapse to the squared branch.

**Tests:**
- Delete invariants tests covering the legacy paths (\`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.invariants.py\` if it has ogive/semicircular cases)
- Update CI render targets if they ever rendered legacy presets

### 2. Add \`handle_crossbar_d\` param

The "handle" in the squared design is the horizontal crossbar between the two posts. Currently both posts and crossbar use \`handle_post_d\`. User wants to tune the grip thickness independently.

**New param:**

\`\`\`scad
handle_crossbar_d = 14;  // @param number min=10 max=40 step=0.5 unit=mm group=handle label="Handle crossbar diameter (grip thickness)"
\`\`\`

Default \`14\` to match the current \`handle_post_d\`; existing renders unchanged.

**Update \`_crossbar_cyl\`** to use \`handle_crossbar_d / 2\` for the crossbar radius (instead of \`post_r\`).

**Update the corner sweep** (\`_handle_corner_fillet\`) to work when \`post_d != crossbar_d\`. The quarter-torus blend currently assumes both are the same diameter (single \`post_r\`). When they differ, two reasonable options:

- **A (recommended):** corner sweep tube radius = \`max(post_r, crossbar_r)\`. Tangent to whichever is larger; the smaller side has a small visible fillet at the junction. Geometrically robust.
- **B:** use a frustum-of-torus shape that smoothly transitions tube radius along the sweep. Cleaner-looking but more complex; only worth it if the diameter ratio gets extreme.

Pick A. If the visual result looks bad at large diameter ratios (e.g. 14mm post with 25mm crossbar), bump to B.

**Update \`_handle_squared_cyl\`** to thread the crossbar radius separately:

\`\`\`scad
module _handle_squared_cyl() {
    post_r          = handle_post_d / 2;
    crossbar_r      = handle_crossbar_d / 2;
    apex_z          = base_thickness + handle_height;
    crossbar_axis_z = apex_z - crossbar_r;
    post_total_h    = crossbar_axis_z;

    for (sx = [-1, 1]) {
        _post_cyl(sx, post_r, post_total_h);
        _handle_corner_fillet(sx, post_r, crossbar_r, crossbar_axis_z);
    }
    _crossbar_cyl(crossbar_r, crossbar_axis_z);
}
\`\`\`

**Update \`arch_apex_rise\`** to use \`handle_crossbar_d\` (the apex's height-above-post-tops is determined by the crossbar diameter, not the post diameter).

### 3. Header / preset updates

- Header: relabel \`handle_post_d\` to "Handle support post diameter" (it no longer sets the crossbar). Add a one-line note on \`handle_crossbar_d\`.
- Stock preset: include \`handle_crossbar_d=14\`.
- Add a new preset \`thicker-grip\` with \`handle_crossbar_d=20\` to demonstrate the use case (optional, but nice to have).

## Don't

- Don't change the ring/cradle/baseplate/drain logic.
- Don't change print orientation (\`print_orientation\` for aquor is a different model).
- Don't touch \`post_center_x\`, \`cell_spacing_y\`, or any load-bearing invariant.
- Don't preserve \`handle_post_w\`/\`handle_thickness\`/\`arch_style\` as no-op aliases. Just delete; we have version control if anyone wants to revive them.
- Don't break the \`PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX\` calculation — verify it still resolves correctly after the simplification.

## Verify

- [ ] Default render unchanged from current main (handle_crossbar_d=14 = handle_post_d=14, no visual diff).
- [ ] \`openscad -o /tmp/v5.stl models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad\` builds clean.
- [ ] Invariants script passes.
- [ ] Trial render with \`handle_crossbar_d=20\`: visibly thicker grip, sweeping corners still tangent.
- [ ] Trial render with \`handle_crossbar_d=10\`: thinner grip, also tangent.
- [ ] No references to the deleted params anywhere in the file (header, modules, presets, tests).
- [ ] \`grep -rE "arch_style|handle_post_w|handle_thickness|arch_point_offset|corner_fillet" models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.invariants.py\` returns nothing.
- [ ] Brief commit msg: \`revise: spraycan v5 — prune legacy arch params + handle_crossbar_d (<bd-id>)\`.

## References

- \`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad\` — current model
- Recent history: st-y1q (sweeping corners + organic flare), st-4ac (cylindrical posts + flush base), st-kyz (squared/toolbox handle), st-qt4 (ogive — deleting)
- \`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.invariants.py\` — companion invariants

comments.author: mayor
comments.text:
[mayor] Correction (2026-04-25): the user's ask in §2 is **NOT** crossbar diameter (grip thickness). It's **handle width** — the horizontal span between the two posts.

Today `post_center_x` controls each post's X centre, so the handle span is `2 * post_center_x` (default 80 mm). The crossbar already follows post_center_x automatically (`_crossbar_cyl` length is derived from it).

User wants a discoverable, user-facing **`handle_width`** param. Implement as:

```scad
handle_width = 80; // @param number min=60 max=180 step=1 unit=mm group=handle label="Handle width — outside-to-outside post centre span"
```

Then derive internally:
```scad
post_center_x = handle_width / 2;
```

§2 of the bead becomes:

- **Replace** the existing `post_center_x` @param with the new `handle_width` @param.
- Internal `post_center_x` becomes a derived `function` or `const` expression based on `handle_width`.
- Default `handle_width = 80` so existing renders are unchanged (matches today's `post_center_x = 40 \* 2`).
- Update the load-bearing-invariants header section: the constraint that `post_center_x` must stay within the Y=0 corridor becomes a constraint on `handle_width` (must keep handle_width / 2 within the corridor).
- Crossbar follows automatically — no `_crossbar_cyl` changes needed beyond consuming the new derived value.
- The corner sweep doesn't need to handle different post/crossbar diameters; **DROP the corner-sweep logic added in the original §2** (don't introduce `handle_crossbar_d`, don't change `_handle_corner_fillet`, don't pick A vs B). Posts and crossbar stay the same diameter.

§1 (legacy cleanup) is unchanged — still delete ogive/semicircular paths, dead params (`handle_post_w`, `handle_thickness`, `arch_point_offset`, `corner_fillet`), and legacy presets.

Add a preset `wide-grip` with `handle_width = 120` to demonstrate.

Verification step changes:
- Trial render with `handle_width=120`: posts spread further apart, crossbar bridges the longer span, no overhang or printability regression.
- Trial render with `handle_width=60` (narrower than default): posts pull inward; verify the inner-edge clearance to middle-column rings remains positive (today's middle ring is at X=0, ring_od ≈ 57.5; post inner edge at handle_width/2 - post_w/2 = 23 vs ring outer edge at 28.75 → fail). So min may need to be 70+ to maintain clearance. Document or enforce in the @param min=.

title: [feat/ux] Caliper: collapse metadata pane on mobile + move presets into params pane
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-7dxg
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-25T16:59:11Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Why

Two related UX nits in the Caliper detail view:

1. **Mobile metadata pane shouldn't dominate.** Below 1200px the layout stacks to single column and `DetailLeftRail` renders all its content inline (`DetailPage.tsx:283-326`). The collapse affordance only exists at ≥1200px. On a phone, the user lands on a wall of preset list + render log + warnings + source path BEFORE they ever see the viewer or params. They have to scroll past it every time.

2. **Presets belong with params, not metadata.** Conceptually, picking a preset = "set these params." The metadata pane (left rail) groups presets with render history, warnings, and a source-file quick-jump — three observability surfaces and one config surface. The config surface is misfiled. Move it next to the param controls where users actually look when changing values.

## Scope

### Part 1 — Mobile: collapse metadata pane by default

Below 1200px, the metadata pane (left rail) stacks above the viewer. Make that stacked variant collapse-by-default with a tap-to-expand affordance.

- In `DetailLeftRailContent`, on viewports < 1200px (or via responsive class equivalents), render a single-row disclosure header showing a summary line (e.g., "Metadata · {history.length} renders, {warnings.length > 0 ? `${warnings.length} warnings` : 'clean'}") and a chevron. Default closed.
- Tap to expand → the existing content slides into view. Expansion state is local component state (React `useState`), persisted to `localStorage` only if it's already a pattern in the codebase — otherwise per-session.
- The existing ≥1200px collapse behavior stays unchanged.
- Use a native `<details>` for the disclosure, OR a shadcn-equivalent in this codebase if there's already an accordion primitive (check first; don't add a new dependency).
- The collapsed state must NOT collide with the existing `leftRailCollapsed` ≥1200px state — they're orthogonal: the ≥1200px one collapses to a 32px rail with a chevron icon; the mobile one collapses to a single disclosure row above the viewer.

Files:
- `components/DetailPage.tsx` — `DetailLeftRail` and `DetailLeftRailContent`
- Possibly extract a small `<MetadataDisclosure>` wrapper to keep the rail content reusable.

### Part 2 — Move presets to ParamRail

Pull the preset section out of `DetailLeftRailContent` and into `ParamRail`. The preset section includes:
- The preset list (stock + user)
- The active-preset highlight
- The "modified" badge
- The Save / Save As / Delete affordances
- The save-row open/closed state (`saveRowOpen`, `setSaveRowOpen`)

After the move, `ParamRail` renders **presets first** (e.g., a sticky header inside the rail or a top section), then the param rows below. Keep visual hierarchy clean: a thin section divider between presets and params.

The left rail then contains: render history + warnings + source modelPath quick-jump. Smaller, lighter, less competing with the viewer.

Files:
- `components/ParamRail.tsx` — accept presets + handlers + activePresetId + modified + saveRowOpen + setSaveRowOpen as new props (mirror what `DetailLeftRailContent` takes today). Render a `<PresetSection>` at the top.
- `components/DetailPage.tsx` — remove preset-related props from the `DetailLeftRail` render call; pass them to the `ParamRail` render call instead.
- `components/DetailPage.tsx` — `DetailLeftRail` and `DetailLeftRailContent` lose their preset-related parameters and inner JSX. Update the type signatures.
- Tests: `components/ParamRow.test.tsx` is the only existing test on the rail components — verify it still passes and add a small test that ParamRail renders presets when given them.

### Keep working

- `⌘1`–`⌘9` shortcuts at `DetailPage.tsx:134-142` still load presets — they reference `detail.loadPresetByIndex` which is unchanged. Verify they keep working after the move (the shortcut handler doesn't care which component renders presets, but verify nothing breaks).
- `CommandPalette` exposes presets via the bridge at `DetailPage.tsx:79-99`. That bridge also doesn't care about render location; verify the palette still works.

## Don't

- Don't move render history, warnings, or source quick-jump out of the left rail — they belong in the metadata pane.
- Don't add a new accordion dependency; reuse what's there or use native `<details>`.
- Don't change preset behavior, save logic, or shortcut bindings.
- Don't add `localStorage` persistence for the mobile-disclosure state unless the codebase already has a pattern for per-component UI persistence — if it does, mirror it; if not, leave as per-session.
- Don't restyle the existing param rows or the ParamRail visual treatment beyond adding the new preset section above them.
- Don't change the ≥1200px collapse behavior on the left rail.

## Verify

- [ ] Mobile (< 1200px) viewport: detail page lands with a single "Metadata" disclosure row, viewer immediately below, params below that. Disclosure tap expands to show render log + warnings + source.
- [ ] Mobile: presets visible inside the params pane (not in metadata).
- [ ] Desktop (≥ 1200px): three-column layout unchanged in shape; left rail no longer contains presets; right rail (params) shows presets at top.
- [ ] `⌘1`–`⌘9` still load presets.
- [ ] Command palette still loads presets.
- [ ] Save / Save As / Delete preset still work from inside the params pane.
- [ ] No test regressions.
- [ ] Screenshots at 390×844 (mobile) and 1440×900 (desktop) attached to the PR or under `docs/design/` if there's a convention.
- [ ] Lint + typecheck + unit + e2e green.
- [ ] Brief commit msg: `feat: collapse metadata on mobile + move presets to params pane (<bd-id>)`.

## References

- `components/DetailPage.tsx:166-228` — main 3-col layout
- `components/DetailPage.tsx:252-345` — `DetailLeftRail` + `DetailLeftRailContent`
- `components/DetailPage.tsx:134-142` — preset shortcut bindings
- `components/DetailPage.tsx:79-99` — command palette preset bridge
- `components/ParamRail.tsx` — params pane, gains the preset section
- `hooks/useDetailState` (or wherever `detail.loadPreset` lives) — handler signatures to thread through
