## Goal

Make the repo presentable for a public GitHub profile — not a full OSS project (no CONTRIBUTING, no issue templates, no code-of-conduct), just clean license coverage and a README a casual visitor can scan. Decisions confirmed with user 2026-04-23; execute, don't re-derive.

## Scope

### 1. LICENSE files

- **Root `LICENSE`** — MIT, copyright "Sean O'Connor". Use the canonical MIT text from https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT (SPDX `MIT`). This covers all code (TypeScript, Python scripts, Next.js app).
- **`models/LICENSE`** — Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 (SPDX `CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0`). Use the official CC text or a pointer link + summary. Attribution: "Sean O'Connor".
- **SPDX headers** on each `.scad` file at top:
  ```
  // SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0
  // Copyright (c) 2026 Sean O'Connor
  ```
  Add to all three: `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, `popcorn_kernel.scad`, `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad`. Place after any existing leading comment block or before the first \`include\` — pick whichever is tidier; be consistent.
- **Preserve** `libs/BOSL2/LICENSE` (BSD-2-Clause) and `libs/QuackWorks/LICENSE` (whatever it is — verify and note in README credits). Do NOT modify bundled library files.

### 2. package.json metadata

- Add `"license": "MIT"` to `package.json`.
- Add `"author": "Sean O'Connor"` if a real name is wanted (user's call — default YES per their public-facing intent).
- Add `"repository": { "type": "git", "url": "https://github.com/seanoconnor/stuff" }` — adjust username if different.
- Leave `"private": true` as-is (blocks accidental npm publish; has no effect on GitHub visibility).

### 3. README.md refresh

Current README is already decent. Update:
- **Model table** — add the third model `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` with a short description (parametric 2×3 spray-can tote carrier, 50mm cans, handled, wet-safe, kid-safe).
- **Caliper UI screenshot** — embed \`docs/design/caliper/screenshots/02-detail-desktop.png\` under a new "## Web app" sub-section "### Live preview", short caption. If a real runtime screenshot exists in `renders/` or is easy to capture, prefer that over the design mock; otherwise the mock screenshot works.
- **License** — new section near the bottom: "Code under MIT (see `LICENSE`). Models under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (see `models/LICENSE`). Bundled libraries retain their own licenses (`libs/BOSL2/LICENSE` BSD-2-Clause; `libs/QuackWorks/LICENSE`)."
- **"Built with"** — optional footer one-liner: "Built with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) using a multi-agent workflow." Keep it brief; no need to explain Gas Town.
- **Clean any stale internal references** — if the current README mentions `bd` / `gt` / witness / refinery / polecat, rephrase for public audience or drop. A casual visitor doesn't need to know the issue tracker is Dolt-backed.

### 4. .gitignore additions

Add these lines to `.gitignore`:

```
# Gas Town / Claude Code local-only context (runbook internals)
CLAUDE.local.md
.claude/commands/done.md
.claude/commands/handoff.md

# Regenerable STL exports
exports/*.stl
exports/*.stl.gz
```

**Then**: `git rm --cached` the tracked copies of these files (except the commands which may not be tracked — check). Verify `git status` after to ensure nothing else got pulled in.

For `exports/*.stl`: if any STLs are currently tracked, remove them from git (they're regenerable by the export pipeline). Keep `exports/.gitkeep` if needed so the directory persists.

### 5. Keep-and-trim internal AI files

- **Move** entire contents of `CLAUDE.local.md` → `.claude/LOCAL.md`. Update `CLAUDE.local.md` → delete (now gitignored). The new `.claude/LOCAL.md` is also gitignored via the pattern above (or add explicitly).

  Wait — cleaner: keep the file at path `CLAUDE.local.md` (root) and just gitignore it. Claude Code's convention is `CLAUDE.local.md` at root for local overrides; moving it breaks that convention and may break hook execution. **Just gitignore the existing file; leave its on-disk location at repo root.** Do `git rm --cached CLAUDE.local.md` and the content stays on the user's machine.

- **Same for `.claude/commands/done.md` and `handoff.md`** — gitignore + `git rm --cached`. These reference `gt done` / `gt handoff` specifically (Gas Town commands); they'll no-op for anyone else. Keep on disk, hide from git.
- **Keep public**: `CLAUDE.md` (bd quick-ref is actually useful context), `AGENTS.md` (now has invariants + render public docs), `.claude/commands/review.md` (generic review command), `.claude/skills/*` (scad-render/export/send — interesting AI-assisted dev artifacts), `.claude/settings.json` (no secrets; `bd prime` hooks no-op without gt), `.beads/` (issue tracking data).
- **Trim `CLAUDE.md`** — remove anything Gas-Town-runbook-ish. Current file is mostly the bd beads block + "Remote Artifact Browser" tip; that's fine. If any session-close ceremony leaks through, cut it. Final `CLAUDE.md` should read as: "this project uses bd for issue tracking; here's how to use the serve.py artifact browser" — and that's it.
- **Trim `AGENTS.md`** — current "Non-Interactive Shell Commands" section at line 77+ might be polecat-specific. Skim and keep only generic advice. Render-pipeline and invariants sections are useful public content.

### 6. Stale-content audit

- Grep for any remaining `gt ` / `polecat` / `refinery` / `witness` / `MQ` / `merge queue` / `Dolt` references in public-tracked files (not node_modules, not libs/, not .claude/ skills which are OK). Flag or rewrite each hit. Expected: a handful in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md that should be cleaned.
- Scan `docs/` — the `docs/design/caliper/` package includes a reference URL to `stuff.xyz/m/...` (placeholder). Keep as-is (it's in a design handoff doc, clearly illustrative).
- `.github/workflows/*.yml` — verify no secrets and no references to private infra. Should be fine (render job is public-friendly).

### 7. Verify after changes

- `git status` clean except intended changes.
- `npm run test` green (no test depends on the files we trimmed).
- `npm run build` green.
- `git grep -i "witness\|polecat\|refinery\|propulsion principle"` on tracked files returns ≤ 2 hits (both acceptable; note them in the commit message if any remain).
- Open the rendered README.md (via GitHub's md preview or `grip` if installed) and verify the model table, screenshot, and license section render correctly.

## Acceptance

- Root `LICENSE` (MIT) exists.
- `models/LICENSE` (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) exists; all three `.scad` files have SPDX headers.
- `package.json` has `license`, `author`, `repository`.
- README.md updated with 3-model table, Caliper screenshot, license section, "Built with Claude Code" footer.
- `.gitignore` updated; `CLAUDE.local.md`, `.claude/commands/done.md`, `.claude/commands/handoff.md`, and any tracked `exports/*.stl` untracked via `git rm --cached`.
- `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` trimmed of Gas-Town-specific runbook content.
- All tests pass; build green.
- `git grep` sweep for leaked internal terms clean.
- Commit message summarizes: "chore: prep repo for public visibility (licenses, README, gitignore, AI-file trim)".

## Do not

- Make it a "full OSS project" — no CONTRIBUTING.md, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, no issue templates, no PR templates, no GitHub Actions for stale issues/contributor welcomes.
- Move `CLAUDE.local.md` to a new path — just gitignore it at current location.
- Modify files under `libs/BOSL2/` or `libs/QuackWorks/`.
- Remove `.claude/skills/*` — they're interesting artifacts, keep them.
- Push to a remote repo. That's the user's step post-merge.

## References

- Current README: README.md
- CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 full text: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode
- MIT license SPDX: https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT
- BOSL2 license (preserve as-is): libs/BOSL2/LICENSE
- User decisions thread (pinned in mayor context): 2026-04-23 session

title: [feat] Spraycan carrier 2×3: fillet the top-rim edges of the base plate (currently only vertical corners are rounded)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-p2n2
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T15:40:05Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Ask

Add a fillet to the outside edges of the spraycan carrier base plate. Currently only the four vertical corner edges are rounded; the top-rim and bottom-rim edges (8 edges forming the top and bottom perimeters of the base prism) are sharp.

## Current state

`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad:117–118`:

```scad
cuboid([base_w, base_d, base_thickness],
       rounding = fillet_r,
       ...);
```

BOSL2's `cuboid(size, rounding=N)` with a scalar `rounding` rounds only the four **vertical (Z-axis) edges** by default. That matches the existing header-comment claim "base corners and handle posts are filleted" (line 24). Top and bottom perimeters remain sharp.

## Fix direction

Extend the rounding to include the **top-rim** (4 edges at `z = base_thickness` top face). Leave the **bottom-rim** (z=0) sharp — sharp bottom edges print cleanly against the build plate and a fillet there would create a small overhang that adhesion doesn't love.

In BOSL2 cuboid syntax, use `edges=` to specify which edges get rounded. Likely:

```scad
cuboid([base_w, base_d, base_thickness],
       rounding = fillet_r,
       edges    = [FRONT+TOP, BACK+TOP, LEFT+TOP, RIGHT+TOP,  // 4 top-rim edges
                   FRONT+LEFT, FRONT+RIGHT, BACK+LEFT, BACK+RIGHT], // 4 vertical corners (existing)
       ...);
```

(Consult `libs/BOSL2/shapes3d.scad` `cuboid()` for the actual `edges=` constant idioms — `TOP`, `BOT`, face/edge unions. `EDGES_ALL_MINUS_BOT` or equivalent may be a single-token way to say "every edge except the 4 bottom ones".)

## Acceptance

- Top rim of the base plate visibly rounded in iso + side renders. Bottom rim still sharp in side render.
- `fillet_r = 2` (default) produces a subtle but visible rim fillet. `fillet_r = 0` still works (no crash) and produces a sharp-cornered brick as today.
- Update the header comment (line 24–27) to reflect that the base has "rounded top and vertical corners, sharp bottom for bed adhesion" or similar.
- CI render thumbnails (`renders/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm/`) show the change; inspect the iso + side PNGs post-CI.
- st-cjn invariants (once merged) still pass: `connected_solids == 1`, watertight, bbox within `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX ± 1mm`. If this bead lands before st-cjn, skip — just make sure the manual trimesh stats still look sane.
- All E2E + vitest green.

## Do not

- Fillet the bottom rim — bed adhesion matters.
- Touch `fillet_r`'s range or step.
- Apply rounding to the cradle or drainage geometry (out of scope — cradles already have their own chamfer per header comment line 25).
- Add a separate `base_rim_fillet_r` param — reuse `fillet_r` for simplicity. If the spray-can carrier needs per-feature fillet control later, file as a separate bead.

## References

- models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad:24–27 (header comment on kid-safety, needs update), :75 (fillet_r param), :117–118 (cuboid call to extend)
- libs/BOSL2/shapes3d.scad — cuboid() + edges constants (FRONT / BACK / TOP / BOT / LEFT / RIGHT + union notation)
- Related prior beads: st-v7k, st-8ac, st-hnd, st-3ta — same file / same family of edits

title: [feat] Per-model invariants sidecar + pre-submit gate (P2 from st-ryj research)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-beao
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T15:15:25Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Origin

Implements the **P2 recommendation** from `docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md` (bead st-ryj). That research classified 4 of 5 recent model defects as CAD issues and concluded a per-model invariants sidecar is the highest-leverage next step: deterministic truth, no LLM drift, closes 3 of 4 CAD classes (st-v7k topology, st-8ac clearance, st-3ta wasted material). Does NOT address st-hnd-style C¹ continuity — that's a future P1 vision-checklist fold-in.

**Stop the bleeding, then iterate.** Ship infra + enough seed invariants to retroactively cover st-v7k/st-8ac/st-3ta; the st-hnd continuity class and the pre-`gt done` gate automation can layer on after.

## Deliverables

### 1. Core script — `scripts/check-invariants.py`

Invocation: `scripts/check-invariants.py <stem>` (e.g. `scripts/check-invariants.py spraycan_carrier_6x50mm`).

Responsibilities:
- Load the `.scad` source from `models/<stem>.scad`
- Parse `@param` annotations (reuse `lib/scad-params/parse.ts` logic or port a minimal Python equivalent — whichever is cheaper; Python port is fine since parser is small)
- Load the STL from `exports/<stem>.stl` (fail cleanly with "export not found — run scripts/render-all.py first" if missing)
- Use `trimesh` to compute: bbox, triangle count, `is_watertight`, **connected-body count**
- Dynamically import `models/<stem>.invariants.py` if it exists; call `check(model_ctx) -> list[Failure]`
- Always also apply a small set of **built-in invariants** that every model inherits (see §3)
- Exit 0 on all pass, nonzero on any failure with a report of which invariant(s) failed and why

`model_ctx` passed to per-model `check()`:
```python
{
  "stem": "spraycan_carrier_6x50mm",
  "source": "...",              # raw .scad text
  "params": {name: {kind, default, min, max, unit, group, ...}},
  "stl": trimesh.Trimesh,       # loaded mesh
  "bbox_mm": (x, y, z),
  "anchor_bbox_mm": (x, y, z) | None,  # parsed from PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX if present
  "connected_solids": int,
}
```

### 2. Per-model `models/<stem>.invariants.py`

Seed files for all three current models. Format:
```python
# models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.invariants.py
from scripts.invariants import Failure, as_default_params

def check(ctx):
  failures = []
  p = as_default_params(ctx["params"])
  # st-3ta class: base_d (Y) shouldn't exceed base_w (X) — handle sides need the room, other sides don't
  if ctx["bbox_mm"][1] > ctx["bbox_mm"][0]:
    failures.append(Failure("footprint", f"base_d={ctx['bbox_mm'][1]:.1f} > base_w={ctx['bbox_mm'][0]:.1f}, non-handle sides wasteful"))
  # st-8ac class: handle arch apex above cans
  if p["handle_height"] < p["can_height"] + 10:  # 10mm grip clearance
    failures.append(Failure("clearance", f"handle_height={p['handle_height']} < can_height+10={p['can_height']+10}"))
  return failures
```

For the first pass, each model's `invariants.py` only needs to cover the specific failure modes that have bitten (or that are obvious structural claims). Don't chase exhaustiveness — invariants compound over time as new defects teach us what to check.

**Seed minimum:**
- `spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.invariants.py`: the two shown above (st-3ta footprint, st-8ac clearance)
- `cylindrical_holder_slot.invariants.py`: `bbox_mm ≈ PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX ± 1mm` check if the model declares an anchor; otherwise a minimal `connected_solids == 1` placeholder
- `popcorn_kernel.invariants.py`: minimal — `connected_solids == 1` and a bbox sanity

### 3. Built-in invariants (apply to every model, no opt-in)

Inside `scripts/check-invariants.py` (or a shared `scripts/invariants/__init__.py`):
- `connected_solids == 1` (st-v7k class — catches preview-detachment when also exported; Manifold backend helps here too)
- `is_watertight == True` (already in export skill; include here for single gate)
- `triangle_count < 1_000_000` (sanity — catches runaway meshing)
- If `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` comment is present in `.scad`, `abs(bbox - anchor) ≤ 1mm` per axis

### 4. Wire into CI

`.github/workflows/ci.yml`: after the existing render/export job, run `scripts/check-invariants.py <stem>` for each model. Fail the job on any non-zero exit. Upload the failure report as a CI artifact.

### 5. Documentation

- Add a short § to `CLAUDE.md` or `AGENTS.md` describing: "When adding a model, create a `.invariants.py` sidecar with at least one claim." Include the `Failure` dataclass import path and a copy-pasteable skeleton.
- Optional: a short line in `CLAUDE.local.md`'s session-close protocol reminding polecats to run `scripts/check-invariants.py` before `gt done` (manual for now; automation is a future bead).

## Acceptance

- `scripts/check-invariants.py <any existing stem>` runs without error on current `main`, exits 0.
- Add a vitest/pytest for the core script: given a known-bad STL (or mock trimesh object with `connected_solids=2`), invariants fail with the expected message.
- **Retroactive check**: manually verify (e.g. with a git revert + temp run) that:
  - Running against a checkout of `main` pre-st-3ta would have flagged footprint aspect.
  - Running against a checkout pre-st-8ac would have flagged handle clearance (this one depends on the handle_height param existing at that revision — document if it didn't and mark catch as partial).
- CI job runs on every PR; failure is visible in the GitHub check.
- `docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md` gets a "## 6. Prototype landed" section appended pointing to this bead's commit.

## Out of scope (future beads)

- **Vision checklist (P1 from st-ryj)** — st-hnd-class continuity issues. File separately once P2 is landed.
- **Pre-`gt done` automation** — local hook that blocks `gt done` on invariant failure. The CI check above is the MVP gate; local automation layers on later.
- **`/review-scad` skill (P3 from st-ryj)** — the full sub-agent vision. Ship P2 + P1 as separate beads first; P3 bundles them later if still useful.
- Retrofitting all existing BOSL2 tests. Out of scope — those validate the library, not our models.

## References

- `docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md` § 4 (P2 proposal) and § 5 (recommendation + scope sketch)
- `.claude/skills/scad-render/SKILL.md:40–60` (existing render pipeline; reuse its outputs)
- `.claude/skills/scad-export/SKILL.md:41–60` (trimesh-based export checks to extend)
- `scripts/render-all.py`, `AGENTS.md:17–26` (CI render infra)
- `lib/scad-params/parse.ts` (param parser — port to Python or invoke as subprocess)
- Defect beads this would have caught: st-v7k, st-8ac, st-3ta
close_reason: no-changes: already landed on main as a4b88ce (feat: per-model invariants sidecar + pre-submit gate). scripts/invariants/{__init__.py,params.py,test_invariants.py} and per-model *.invariants.py sidecars are all present and exercised by check-invariants(-all).py and pytest scripts/invariants/.

title: [research] How can the AI model-authoring workflow catch geometric defects (base asymmetry, fillet discontinuity, etc.) before user review?
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-6lia
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T14:34:45Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
review_only: true
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Motivation

The AI workflow authoring .scad models in this repo has shipped at least five defects that the user spotted during visual review — not during the model's own self-checks:

- st-v7k: handle posts floated detached from base (preview)
- st-8ac: handle interfered with full-height cans (geometric)
- st-l2z: uncommitted work lost until gt-pvx safety net added (process)
- st-3ta (just filed): base margin uniform on 4 sides, visibly oversized on non-handle sides (wasteful, visible)
- st-hnd (just filed): handle post fillet doesn't carry through post→arch junction, visible seam (the file's own comment at lines 25–27 acknowledges this — shipped anyway)

Two brainstorms on 2026-04-16 explored vision-based validation (`docs/brainstorms/2026-04-16-openscad-vision-spike-results.md` and `…-ai-workflow-requirements.md`). Those experiments concluded: vision is strong for structural defects (count, presence, topology), weak for ~10% dimensional drift; multi-angle orthographic PNGs + pixel measurement with a bbox anchor is decisive. Neither has been wired into the standard workflow. Meanwhile the defects continue.

This research bead asks: **what would it take to make the polecat catch these before the user does?**

## Deliverable

A single markdown file at `docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md` (≤ 800 words) with:

1. **Defect taxonomy** — for the 5+ shipped defects above, classify each by type (geometric invariant violation, visual discontinuity, dimensional drift, topological / manifold bug, wasted material, print-safety violation). What fraction are catchable by which technique?

2. **Existing capability inventory** — what's already installed or available in this repo and the broader Claude Code ecosystem?
   - `renders/` pipeline (CI thumbnail renders — `AGENTS.md` lines 17–26 reference)
   - OpenSCAD CLI flags (`--render`, `--info`, convex-hull probes, STL mesh stats)
   - BOSL2 assertions / unit-test-like geometry checks
   - Claude's multimodal vision (brainstorm already characterized strengths/weaknesses)
   - Compound-engineering plugin skills — grep `~/.claude/plugins/compound-engineering/` for any skills about image comparison, visual diff, CAD-aware QA. Include `compound-engineering:gemini-imagegen`, `compound-engineering:agent-browser`, and any others that might apply.
   - Existing test harness (vitest + playwright + the render pipeline) — can it be extended to assert geometric invariants?

3. **Gap analysis** — for each defect class, what's missing between "catchable in principle" and "caught by a polecat pre-submit"?

4. **Three concrete proposals**, ranked by effort/payoff:
   - Lowest effort: one-shot vision review as a pre-`gt done` step — polecat renders model from 4 angles, asks itself "does anything look wrong?"
   - Medium effort: dimensional probe library (`bbox` comparison, rim-overhang check, handle-post-attached-to-base check) with per-model assertions baked into `<stem>.invariants.ts` sidecar.
   - Highest effort: a dedicated `/review-scad` skill or subagent that runs render + vision + invariant-probes against any modified `.scad` on PR open, and blocks merge on fails.

5. **Recommendation** — pick one to prototype as a follow-up bead, with a scope sketch.

## Research approach (suggested)

- `grep -r "vision\|ortho\|bbox\|skill" ~/.claude/plugins/compound-engineering/` for adjacent tooling
- `ls ~/.claude/plugins/*/plugin.json` for installed plugins
- Read the two 2026-04-16 brainstorms in full — don't duplicate their conclusions; cite them
- `grep -n "render\|png\|thumbnail\|assert" scripts/ AGENTS.md` for existing render infra
- Look at how tracker/ssos/stuff rigs test geometry-adjacent things (e2e snapshots? golden files?)
- WebSearch for "OpenSCAD CI validation" and "parametric CAD geometric invariants automated" — light search only, ≤ 6 queries

## Acceptance

- Single deliverable file at `docs/research/2026-04-23-ai-model-qa-workflow-research.md`.
- ≤ 800 words (prefer terse + cited over prose).
- Specific file citations (not vague "the workflow should…").
- For each of the 5 prior defects, states: would this proposal have caught it? Yes/no/maybe with reasoning.
- A concrete recommendation the mayor can turn into a follow-up bead.

## Out of scope

- Implementing any of the proposals. This is research only.
- Validating against models in other rigs (stuff-rig only).
- Training a custom vision model or anything involving model training.

## Sling as review-only

Use `--review-only` so the assignee produces the report and does not commit or push. If the polecat wants to land the research doc, it can file a follow-up bead for that.

## References

- docs/brainstorms/2026-04-16-openscad-vision-spike-results.md
- docs/brainstorms/2026-04-16-openscad-ai-workflow-requirements.md
- AGENTS.md (render pipeline) + scripts/serve.py
- Prior defect beads: st-v7k, st-8ac, st-l2z, st-3ta, st-hnd
design:
---
date: 2026-04-23
bead: st-ryj
status: research-only (not committed per bead `review_only: true`)
---

# AI model-QA workflow — catching geometric defects before user review

## 1. Defect taxonomy (the 5 shipped misses)

| Bead   | Symptom                                      | Class                            | Catchable by                                                          |
|--------|----------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| st-v7k | Handle posts floated detached in preview     | Topology (preview-only)          | Manifold/CSG mismatch probe; iso-render vision                        |
| st-8ac | Handle clashed with tall cans                | Geometric invariant (clearance)  | Explicit `arch_z ≥ can_height` assertion; side-render vision          |
| st-l2z | Session death lost work                      | Process (not CAD)                | N/A — out of scope                                                    |
| st-3ta | Uniform base margin wastes Y plastic         | Wasted-material invariant        | Aspect-ratio / per-axis margin assertion; top-render vision           |
| st-hnd | Fillet discontinuity at post→arch junction   | Visual/C¹ continuity             | Multi-angle vision (subtle); structural — `path_sweep` instead        |

Four of five are CAD defects. Of those: 2 are crisp numeric invariants (st-8ac, st-3ta), 1 is topological (st-v7k), 1 is a visual-continuity subtlety (st-hnd). The spike's claim — "vision strong for structural, weak for 10% dimensional drift" — holds: st-hnd is the one that vision alone struggles with.

## 2. Capability inventory

Already in repo:

- **Render pipeline** — `scripts/render-all.py` + `.claude/skills/scad-render/` (`SKILL.md:40–60`) emit orthographic top/front/side + iso PNGs and a JSON block with `bbox_mm`, `scale_mm_per_px`, and detected `circles_top`. Anchored by the `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` convention. CI regenerates on push/PR (`AGENTS.md:17–26`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`).
- **Export verification** — `.claude/skills/scad-export/SKILL.md:41–60` runs `trimesh` and reports bbox, triangle count, `is_watertight`. Exits non-zero on non-watertight.
- **OpenSCAD Manifold** — `--backend Manifold` is default; Manifold's output already fails closed on many topology errors that OpenCSG preview hides (exactly the st-v7k class).
- **Vitest + Playwright** — harness exists; no invariant-oriented suite yet.
- **BOSL2** — ships geometry tests under `libs/BOSL2/tests/` but they validate the library, not user models.

Not in repo: any CAD-aware compound-engineering plugin. Closest adjacents (`design-implementation-reviewer`, `design-iterator`) are Figma-only and don't transfer. `gemini-imagegen` generates, doesn't diff. No CAD skill exists upstream that we'd just install.

**Spike conclusions** (`docs/brainstorms/2026-04-16-openscad-vision-spike-results.md:66–75`, `…-ai-workflow-requirements.md:26`): multi-angle orthographic PNGs + pixel-measured anchor dimensions are decisive; the missing piece is that this loop is skill-invocable but not *mandatory* before `gt done`.

## 3. Gap analysis

- **Pre-submit gate**: None. `gt done` pushes without re-rendering. Nothing forces a polecat to look at its own output.
- **Topology invariants**: watertight is checked on export, but st-v7k was a *preview* mismatch — nothing currently compares OpenCSG vs Manifold output, nor asserts `num_connected_solids == 1` on the preview path.
- **Per-model invariants**: no place to declare `arch_z_start ≥ can_height` or `base_d ≤ base_w * 0.9`. `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` is the only machine-readable claim a model makes about itself.
- **Visual-continuity class (st-hnd)**: no idiom short of a vision pass or a structural rewrite to `path_sweep`. Invariants can't express C¹ smoothness cheaply.

## 4. Three proposals, ranked effort → payoff

**P1 — Pre-`gt done` vision self-review (LOW effort).** Polecat runs `scripts/render-all.py` on changed models, opens iso + top + side PNGs, and silently critiques against a 5-line checklist ("single body? footprint tight? proportions match spec? seams? clearances?"). Add to `CLAUDE.local.md` completion protocol. Catches st-v7k (iso), st-3ta (top). Misses st-hnd (too subtle) and st-8ac (needs clearance number, not vision).

**P2 — Invariants sidecar (MEDIUM).** Per-model `models/<stem>.invariants.py` (or `.json`) declares machine-checkable claims: bbox range, `num_connected_solids`, free-form lambdas over STL bbox + parsed params (`arch_z_start(params) >= params.can_height`). New `scripts/check-invariants.py` runs on the Manifold-export STL + source-parsed params, exits non-zero on fail. Wire into CI alongside the render job and as a mandatory pre-`gt done` step. Catches st-v7k (connected-body), st-8ac (clearance), st-3ta (per-axis margin). **Does not** catch st-hnd.

**P3 — `/review-scad` skill + sub-agent (HIGH).** On any modified `.scad`: render → export → invariants → vision pass → structured PASS/FAIL. Blocks `gt done`. Catches all four CAD classes. Bundles P1 + P2 plus LLM judgment.

## 5. Recommendation

**Prototype P2 as the next bead.** Rationale: (a) closes three of four defect classes with deterministic, reviewable truth — no LLM drift; (b) composes with P1 cheaply later (add the vision checklist to the same pre-`gt done` hook); (c) each invariant is a one-liner against already-available outputs (trimesh STL, parsed params, `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX`). Initial scope sketch:

- `scripts/check-invariants.py <model.scad>` — reads source, parses `@param`s and constants, loads STL from `exports/`, asserts claims.
- Per-model `models/<stem>.invariants.py` with a single `def check(model, stl): -> list[Failure]`.
- Seed invariants: `connected==1`, `bbox ≈ PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX ±1mm`, `base_d ≤ base_w` (catches st-3ta once seeded), `arch_z_start ≥ can_height` (st-8ac).
- CI: run on PR; block `gt done` locally via a hook in `CLAUDE.local.md`'s Completion Protocol.

Fold P1 (vision checklist) in the same hook for the st-hnd class, but ship P2 first — the invariants are what stop the bleeding.

title: [bug] Spraycan carrier 2×3: handle post fillet doesn't carry through the post→arch junction, visible seam in preview
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-n6oi
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T14:35:40Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Symptom

In live preview, the spraycan carrier's handle shows a bad transition where each handle post meets the handle arc. The post cross-section has a `fillet_r` (default 2 mm) applied via BOSL2 cuboid rounding; the semicircular arch does not apply a matching fillet to its Y-facing edges. The eye (and the print) sees a discontinuous seam at `z ≈ arch_z_start`.

The file's own header comment at lines 25–27 already acknowledges this:

> "Remaining Y-faces of the arch are the only sharp edges -- cheap to break with sandpaper."

Users shouldn't need sandpaper to hide a preview artifact.

## Root cause

`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` around lines 204–215 (post geometry) and the arch block (semicircular annular half-disc). Posts apply `rounding = fillet_r` at line 105-ish. The arch's cross-section is a constant rectangle extruded along the semicircle; its Y-faces have no fillet.

## Fix direction

Two viable approaches:

1. **Round the arch's Y-faces to match `fillet_r`.** Apply BOSL2 `rounding` to the arch's cross-section (the rectangle that gets extruded/revolved). This carries the same fillet radius across the post→arch junction continuously. Preferred — matches the physical design intent.
2. **Fillet the junction specifically.** Add a `rounding` or a small tangent fillet only at `z = arch_z_start`, where the post meets the arch. More localized, but may look abrupt if the arch's own Y-faces remain sharp.

Prefer #1. The existing `handle_thickness` (Y-dimension of the arch's cross-section) already exists; just round the edges.

## Acceptance

- Rendered preview (CI render thumbnails) shows continuous fillet from post side-face into arch side-face — no visible seam at the junction.
- `fillet_r` param label/comment updated to note it now applies to the arch too ("Fillet radius (posts + arch Y-faces)" or similar).
- No regression in the handle's max-overhang-45° print-safety claim (the arch's radial section stays a semicircle; only the Y-face corners get rounded).
- Update the header comment block (lines 25–27) to remove or revise the "sharp edges -- cheap to break with sandpaper" note.
- All E2E + vitest green.
- Verify the preview visually before submitting (render locally or inspect the thumbnails post-CI).

## References

- models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad:25–27 (comment acknowledging the issue), :65 (fillet_r param), :105 (post rounding), :204–215 (post geometry), arch geometry block (grep for `semicircle` / `torus` / `rotate_extrude`)
- Related prior beads: st-v7k (handle floats fix), st-8ac (handle clearance fix)
close_reason: no-changes: already landed on main as d801e9a (fix: sweep a rounded rectangle for the spraycan handle arch). The post→arch fillet carry-through was implemented; later refined by st-djm b855a35 to a manifold-safe rotate_extrude after st-so7's revert.

title: [bug] Spraycan carrier 2×3: base margin is uniform on all 4 sides, wastes plastic on non-handle sides
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-kxx7
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T14:21:51Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Symptom

On the 2×3 spraycan carrier, the drainage base plate extends the same distance (`base_margin` = 18 mm default) beyond the rings on all four sides. On the handle-axis sides (X), the margin is **required** — the handle posts sit in that region (`post_center_x = base_w/2 - handle_post_w/2 - fillet_r - 2`, line 82). On the non-handle sides (Y), the margin has no functional role: the rings don't need it, nothing mounts there.

Reducing `base_margin` to tighten the non-handle sides also pulls the handle mounting region inward, eventually detaching the posts from a valid seat. There's no current parameter that lets a user trim only the Y sides.

Net effect: visibly oversized baseplate with wasted plastic on the long sides.

## Root cause

`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` lines 51 + 76–77:

```scad
base_margin = 18;  // @param ... single axis-agnostic value
base_w = cell_spacing_x * (cols - 1) + ring_od + 2 * base_margin;
base_d = cell_spacing_y * (rows - 1) + ring_od + 2 * base_margin;
```

One scalar applied to both dimensions.

## Fix direction

Split into two params:

- `base_margin_handle_side` — applied to X (handle posts need the room). Keep default 18 mm; this preserves the current preset's handle geometry exactly.
- `base_margin_other_side` — applied to Y. Default to a smaller value (suggest 4–6 mm — enough for the corner rounding at `fillet_r` + a minimal rim, no more).

Rename references in `base_w` / `base_d`. Any downstream geometry keyed off `base_d` (e.g. drainage pattern extent, cradle placement Y) needs audit — `cell_spacing_y` sets the Y stride, not `base_d`, so cradles should place correctly regardless. Verify.

Accept stepping the migration: if changing the param name would break existing user URLs with `base_margin=X`, keep `base_margin` as an alias that sets both halves (for one release), or document the rename in the commit.

## Acceptance

- Two new params with `@param` annotations (unit=mm, group=geometry), min/max bounds sensible for each side's role. Reducing `base_margin_other_side` to 4 mm cleanly tightens the Y footprint; reducing `base_margin_handle_side` still detaches the posts at too-small values (the existing failure mode — document it).
- Default preset matches current footprint on X, tighter on Y (document the before/after dimensions in the commit).
- Rendered preview (CI render thumbnails or local `renders/`) shows the handle geometry unchanged and the Y footprint reduced.
- All E2E + vitest green.
- Document the old `base_margin` → two-param change in the commit message, and update the comment block at top of the .scad file that references defaults.

## References

- models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad:51, 76–77, 82
- Related prior beads: st-v7k (handle floats fix), st-8ac (handle clearance)

title: Parametric 6-can spray can carrier (2×3, 50mm, handled, wet-safe, kid-safe)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-lfkc
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-23T03:42:15Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: unknown
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Design a new parametric OpenSCAD model for a caddy that holds six 50mm-diameter spray cans.

## Spec (confirmed by sean 2026-04-22)

- **Layout**: 2×3 grid of cells (~160mm × 110mm footprint)
- **Can size**: 50mm diameter (with slip clearance — default 0.75mm like the 70mm spraycan holder, @param-exposed)
- **Carry style**: Standalone tote — rounded handle spanning the long axis, high enough for adult grip, no touch-point sharp corners
- **Cradle style**: Open-front C-ring per cell (matches existing `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` cradle geometry) — plus additional drain holes/slots in the cradle walls themselves (per sean's note)

## Hard requirements

1. **Wet-environment drainage**
   - Base must not pool water. Options: perforated floor, slotted floor, or full drain channels from each cell to the outer edge. Pick the approach that prints support-free.
   - Each C-ring cradle also gets drain holes/slots through its wall so water running down a can doesn't get trapped between can and cradle.

2. **Print-friendly (few or no supports)**
   - Target: single-orientation print with tree supports at worst, ideally none.
   - Overhangs capped at ~45°; handle bridging kept under ~25mm or split into two posts + a bridged top bar if longer.
   - If the handle can't be done without supports, consider a print-in-place or two-part (snap-fit) handle and document the choice.

3. **Kid-safe surfaces**
   - Every externally touchable edge chamfered or filleted (fillet preferred on the handle top, top rims of cradles, and base corners).
   - No sharp interior corners on hand-reach surfaces. Drain holes rounded (slots not rectangles).
   - Use BOSL2 `rounding=` / `chamfer=` primitives where practical.

## Parametric controls (@param)

Minimum set — add more as useful:
- `can_diameter` (default 50)
- `clearance` (default 0.75)
- `ring_height` (default 35)
- `wall` (default 3)
- `cell_spacing` (center-to-center between cells)
- `handle_height`, `handle_width`, `handle_thickness`
- `drain_hole_d`, `drain_hole_count` per cradle
- `base_drain_pattern` (e.g. 'slots' | 'holes' | 'open')
- `rows` / `cols` (defaults 2/3 — allows dial-in of other grids if someone wants a 2×2 or 2×4 later)
- `fillet_r` / `chamfer_r` for touchables

Re-use BOSL2 and the existing project conventions from `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` / `spraycan_holder_70mm.scad` (before its removal in st-3l0).

## Filename

`models/spraycan_carrier_6x50mm.scad` — distinct from the existing single-can `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` because the grid + handle + shared base make it a meaningfully different model family, not just another preset.

## Acceptance

- Renders without errors in OpenSCAD
- All @params show up correctly in the parametric web UI (id-scoped inputs, sliders)
- Export pipeline produces an STL that slices with no supports or only tree supports in the default print orientation
- Gallery/nav listing gets a new entry for this model (follow whatever convention obsidian sets up in st-3l0 for the popcorn-kernel replacement)
- PR against stuff main; refinery merges via MQ
close_reason: Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)

description:

New 3D model: a cartoonish popped popcorn kernel to replace lost pieces from a Disney play popcorn stand (for a 2yo).

**Requirements doc:** [docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-popcorn-kernel-model-requirements.md](../docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-popcorn-kernel-model-requirements.md) — read first.

## TL;DR hard requirements

- File: `models/popcorn_kernel.scad`
- One generic blobby "popped kernel" shape — cartoonish, not accurate
- No sharp edges, points, or thin protrusions anywhere (kid safety)
- Longest dim ≥ 1.5" (38 mm); target ~45 mm
- Solid interior (NOT hollow — toddler durability)
- Single-color, FDM-printable on 0.4 mm nozzle with no/trivial supports
- Sits flat on build plate by default (widest face down)
- Declares `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX = [X, Y, Z];` for the preview-image CI
- Shape should look OK from any orientation (kernels land however)

## Technical approach (open)

Pick whichever OpenSCAD technique produces the best cartoonish blob:
- `hull()` of jittered spheres (my default suggestion — naturally smooth, no sharps)
- `minkowski(sphere)` of a rounded polyhedron
- BOSL2 rounded primitives

All should satisfy the no-sharp-edges requirement. Go with the simplest one that looks good in the iso render.

## Verification

- Iso render (auto-generated by render-thumbnails CI from st-dd1) must show recognizable popcorn shape
- PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX declared so measurement pass runs
- Slices cleanly in a standard slicer (Cura/PrusaSlicer/Bambu) with no manifold errors
- No overhangs steep enough to need heroic supports

## Workflow

Submit as PR against main. Do NOT push directly — CI must go green first.

title: fix: listing preview uses top-down view; 3D viewer renders Z-up models on their side
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-dxic
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T23:35:50Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main","base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main
base_branch=main

Two orientation bugs in the stuff rig preview surfaces.

## Issue 1 — Listing thumbnail uses top-down PNG

`app/api/thumbnail/route.ts:25` hardcodes \`top.png\`. Top-down is rarely the orientation you want for a listing card — a perspective view shows form better.

\`scripts/render.py\` already emits \`iso.png\` alongside \`top.png\` (see ANGLES_DEFAULT and \`.claude/skills/_lib/openscad.py\` VIEWS — iso uses camera \`200,-200,150,0,0,0\` with perspective projection, which is the top-right perspective we want).

**Fix:** change \`top.png\` → \`iso.png\` in the thumbnail route.

## Issue 2 — StlViewer renders Z-up models on their side

\`components/StlViewer.tsx\` uses three.js defaults, which are Y-up. OpenSCAD STLs use Z-up (standard 3D-print convention). The model ends up lying on its side in the in-browser viewer. STL export itself is correct; only the viewer is wrong.

**Fix:** set \`camera.up = new THREE.Vector3(0, 0, 1)\` during \`bootstrapScene\` (before the initial \`lookAt\`) and re-apply in \`fitCameraToBbox\` before the second \`lookAt(center)\`. OrbitControls honors \`camera.up\`, so rotation gestures will behave correctly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Listing page at stuff-five-brown.vercel.app shows perspective (iso) thumbnails, not top-down
- [ ] Opening any model detail page, the STL viewer renders the model upright (Z-axis vertical)
- [ ] Orbit rotation still feels natural — dragging should orbit around the vertical axis
- [ ] No regression in other views (/api/thumbnail for non-existent slugs still 404s; existing tests pass)

## Workflow

Submit as PR against main. Do NOT push directly — CI must go green first (this bead is partly an exercise of the PR CI flow we just landed in st-czu/st-4mr).

title: Consolidate snap-backer holder into the parametric cylindrical_holder
description:
## Context
st-0br consolidated the four _slot variants into a single parametric model (`models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`). The last remaining holder — `models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad` — uses a Multiboard snap backer (QuackWorks `snapConnectBacker`) and was explicitly scoped out of that bead.

The cradle + drip cup + gusset geometry is largely shared between the two files; only the backer differs. Now that users can easily tweak params via the parametric editor, the snap variant no longer needs its own file.

## Goal
Fold the snap-backer variant into one parametric model so `models/` is down to one holder file (plus `smoke_motor_mount.scad` which is unrelated).

## Key design decision (please weigh in before a polecat picks this up)
Pick one:

**(a) One model with `backer_type` param** — rename `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` → `cylindrical_holder.scad`, add `backer_type = "slot" | "snap"`, switch backer module accordingly. Snap-specific params (`backer_cells_x/y`, `holding_tolerance`, `grid_pitch`) become visible only when relevant (or just always visible with sensible defaults for the other mode).

**(b) Shared cradle library** — extract cradle+gusset+cup into `models/_cradle.scad` (or `libs/cradle.scad`). Keep two thin model files (`cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`, `cylindrical_holder_snap.scad`) that each `use <cradle.scad>`. More files but cleaner param UIs.

(a) is simpler and matches the 'one model' framing; (b) is better if param-editor UX gets crowded.

## Prior art
- st-0br: consolidated the 4 slot variants
- commit 51795b7 (Consolidate four _slot holder variants into one parametric model)
- `models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad` — current snap-backer source
- `models/cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` — current consolidated slot source

## Done when
- `models/` contains at most one generic `cylindrical_holder*.scad` per chosen approach
- All four historic slot presets (42/46/70/77mm) AND the snap-backer 70mm Multiboard configuration are reachable via params
- `/models` listing page renders the consolidated model(s) with correct thumbnails
- PR shows diffs that demonstrate param-editor still reaches every prior configuration

## Not in scope
- Changing the cradle/gusset geometry (only restructuring)
- The unrelated `smoke_motor_mount.scad`
- Touching `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` logic beyond what's needed to match the merged geometry
close_reason: Obsolete: target file models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad was removed in st-3l0 (commit c610ceb). Nothing left to consolidate. Any future snap-backer work should be a fresh bead.

title: STL preview: orbit / pan / zoom controls
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-t5lp
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T20:58:08Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Make the 3D model preview on /models/<slug> interactive: drag to rotate, right-drag or shift-drag to pan, scroll to zoom. Currently the viewer is a static render — the StlViewer.tsx comment even anticipates this as \"Phase 3\" work.

**Scope:**
- Wire up three.js OrbitControls (three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls) on the existing StlViewer canvas.
- Keep the current auto-fit-to-bbox as the initial camera pose — controls start from that framed view.
- Render-on-demand: the current code renders once per STL update; OrbitControls fires 'change' events, so switch to either (a) a requestAnimationFrame loop gated on a dirty flag or (b) a 'change' listener that calls renderer.render. Pick whichever keeps the CPU cost low — don't run a 60fps loop when the user isn't interacting.
- Keep touch support working (OrbitControls handles this by default; just don't disable it).
- Reset/home button is NOT in scope for this bead — just the core orbit/pan/zoom. File a follow-up if you think it's valuable.

**Acceptance:**
- Visit a model page; left-drag rotates, right-drag (or shift-drag) pans, scroll-wheel zooms.
- The retina regression test (tests/e2e/retina-preview.spec.ts) still passes — it asserts canvas CSS size fits the parent. OrbitControls shouldn't change that, but verify.
- No console errors on model pages.
- Add at least one e2e smoke test that loads a model page, dispatches a wheel event on the canvas, and confirms the camera moved (evaluate three.js camera.position before/after). Skip the test gracefully if headless wheel events are flaky in CI — but try first.
- No noticeable CPU churn when idle (don't run a rAF loop unconditionally).

**Out of scope:** any UI chrome around the viewer (control hints, reset button, fullscreen). Keep the viewer's styling as-is.

**Test plan:**
- `npm run test` green (vitest)
- `npm run test:e2e` green (playwright)
- Manual: open dev server, verify each interaction on at least one cylindrical holder model.

Open a PR with this bead ID in the body; wait for CI green before requesting merge.

title: Consolidate cylinder/spraycan holder variants into one parametric model
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-aigp
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T21:10:18Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: unknown
formula_vars: base_branch=main

We now have five near-identical _slot variants in stuff/models/:
- cylinder_holder_42mm_slot.scad
- cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad
- spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad
- spraycan_holder_77mm_slot.scad
(The snap-backer variant spraycan_holder_70mm.scad uses a different backer and is out of scope for this bead. smoke_motor_mount.scad is unrelated.)

The four slot variants share an identical backer+cradle+gusset body. Only user-tunable numbers differ:
- can_diameter: 42 / 46 / 70 / 77
- clearance: 0.25 for small, 0.75 for spraycan
- ring_height: 50 for small, 35 for spraycan
- gusset_back_w / gusset_front_w / gusset_depth vary (20/8/6, 46/30/10, 28/10/6, 32/10/6)
- 46mm drops the bottom chamfer on the gusset (bed-adhesion tweak from st-skn)

**Goal:** one model file (suggest `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad`) with the full tunable range exposed as @param-commented vars, replacing the four variants. UI's slug/param editor should still let a user dial in any of the four existing configurations.

**Design decisions (polecat: use judgment, document in bead notes before coding):**
1. Should the 46mm's wider gusset + missing bottom chamfer become exposed params (gusset_back_w/front_w/depth already numeric; bottom chamfer becomes a boolean like `gusset_bottom_chamfer`)? Yes — all four configs should be reachable without editing the .scad.
2. Clearance and ring_height defaults differ between cylinder (small) and spraycan (large). Pick one reasonable default (suggest clearance=0.5, ring_height=40) — users will tune.
3. Name: suggest `cylindrical_holder_slot.scad` or `holder_slot.scad`. Make the bet.

**Scope:**
- Add one new consolidated .scad with all params exposed and default PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX computed from the defaults.
- Remove the four existing _slot .scad files.
- Update any discover.ts / gallery / smoke test fixtures that reference the old slugs.
- Run vitest + playwright e2e locally; both must pass.
- Verify via the dev server that the new model page renders and the param controls produce a sensible STL at each of the four historic (diameter, clearance, ring_height, gusset) configurations.
- Spot-check one of the four historic STL outputs against its old byte size — they won't be byte-identical (param defaults changed), but 5-10% byte delta is fine; 50%+ means geometry differs.

**Out of scope:** touching spraycan_holder_70mm.scad (snap backer) or smoke_motor_mount.scad. File a follow-up bead if the snap variant should also collapse into the same model later.

**Done when:**
- One consolidated .scad file replaces the four _slot variants
- Discovery/gallery/tests updated
- `npm run test` and `npm run test:e2e` are green
- PR open with bead ID in body; CI green; mayor reviews before merge
notes:
Design decisions before coding:

1. Filename: cylindrical_holder_slot.scad (bead's suggested name).
2. Gusset bottom chamfer: new boolean param gusset_bottom_chamfer (default true). 46mm config sets it false for bed adhesion.
3. Defaults — pick balanced values users will tune:
   - can_diameter = 70 (spraycan is the most common use)
   - clearance = 0.5 (midpoint of 0.25/0.75)
   - ring_height = 40 (midpoint of 35/50)
   - gusset_back_w = 28, gusset_front_w = 10, gusset_depth = 6, gusset_bottom_chamfer = true (matches 70mm spraycan — the most common config)
4. PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX with these defaults: X=max(50, 76)=76, Y=6.5+76=82.5, Z=80.
5. Historic configs users can dial in:
   - 42mm: can_diameter=42, clearance=0.25, ring_height=50, gusset_back_w=20, gusset_front_w=8, gusset_depth=6, gusset_bottom_chamfer=true
   - 46mm: can_diameter=46, clearance=0.25, ring_height=50, gusset_back_w=46, gusset_front_w=30, gusset_depth=10, gusset_bottom_chamfer=false
   - 70mm: can_diameter=70, clearance=0.75, ring_height=35, gusset_back_w=28, gusset_front_w=10, gusset_depth=6, gusset_bottom_chamfer=true
   - 77mm: can_diameter=77, clearance=0.75, ring_height=35, gusset_back_w=32, gusset_front_w=10, gusset_depth=6, gusset_bottom_chamfer=true

Out of scope: spraycan_holder_70mm.scad (snap backer variant).

title: Smoke-test /api/export in Vercel production
description:
The /api/export STL-generation endpoint is the heaviest code path in the web app — it runs openscad-wasm in a Vercel Function with maxDuration=120s and the full BOSL2+QuackWorks include closure. It was deferred during the first Vercel deploy because a round-trip STL render takes ~10s and wasn't worth blocking the initial rollout.

## Goal
Confirm /api/export works end-to-end against production (https://stuff-five-brown.vercel.app) for at least one simple model (smoke_motor_mount) and one BOSL2-heavy model (a cylinder_holder or spraycan_holder variant).

## Checks
- POST returns 200 + a non-trivial STL byte stream
- STL byte count is within expected range for the model (not 0, not truncated)
- Response time stays under the 120s maxDuration configured on the route
- Override params (e.g., non-default plate_w on smoke_motor_mount) produce an STL whose X-extent shifts accordingly — same assertion as the Playwright bug-regression test
- Server logs show no ENOENT for include paths (confirms outputFileTracingIncludes covers the closure walker's full reach)

## Out of scope
- Full Playwright e2e run against production (CI already runs it against local `next start`)
- Render-quality regression harness

## Context
Landed via PR #2 (https://github.com/SeanOC/stuff/pull/2). next.config.mjs outputFileTracingIncludes globs libs/**/*.scad + models/**/*.scad, but the actual runtime reach depends on lib/wasm/closure.ts — if the trace globs miss something the closure walks to, export will 500.

## Reproduction
```
curl -X POST https://stuff-five-brown.vercel.app/api/export \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"smoke-motor-mount","params":{}}' --output /tmp/smoke.stl
file /tmp/smoke.stl  # should report 'STL 3D printer data'
```
close_reason: Dropped per user direction 2026-04-23. /api/export not exercised via CI smoke but user will exercise manually against stuff.seanoc.com as needed.

## Goal
Create a root `README.md` for the stuff repo. Currently there's none; `app/README.md` describes the web app but nothing introduces the repo as a whole.

## Scope

### Contents (aim for ~40-60 lines, no more)
- 1-paragraph intro: this is a collection of 3D-printable parametric designs (OpenSCAD + BOSL2 + QuackWorks) with a browser UI for rendering and downloading STLs.
- "Models" section: one-line per .scad in `models/` with filename + short description. Source these descriptions from the first non-blank comment line of each file; if none, derive from filename.
- "Web app" section: 2-3 sentences pointing to `app/` and `app/README.md` for the Next.js + WASM preview + Vercel-deployed parametric UI.
- "Libraries" section: one paragraph explaining `libs/` (BOSL2 + QuackWorks pinned via `libs/README.md`).
- "Development" section: minimal pointers — `cd app && npm run dev` for web app, `openscad --backend Manifold models/<file>.scad` for direct CLI rendering.

### Out of scope
- Screenshots / GIFs (defer, not needed for v1)
- Badges (CI / license / vercel)
- Contributing guide
- Changelog
- Licensing language (repo has no LICENSE; don't invent one)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Model list matches actual files in `models/`
