If `gt done --pre-verified --target main` succeeds cleanly (MR bead created, refinery picks it up, auto-merge path works), that validates the fix in production.

title: Phase 4: End-to-end browser test suite + GitHub Actions CI integration
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-57zo
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T13:25:33Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal
Ship a full Playwright-based e2e browser test suite that exercises every critical user flow, plus a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full test pyramid (unit + e2e) on every PR/push.

## Depends on
Phase 3 (st-tbv). Needs the gallery, annotated model corpus, and Vercel deploy pipeline to be real before e2e is worth writing — otherwise tests lock in decisions Phase 3 hasn't made yet.

## Scope

### 1. Playwright scaffold
- `@playwright/test` at the repo root. Config targets the local `npm run dev` server for local runs, and Vercel preview URLs for CI runs.
- Headed mode for local development, headless in CI.
- Browsers: Chromium at minimum. Firefox + WebKit optional (v1 can skip for speed).
- `playwright.config.ts` sets sensible defaults: retries=1 in CI, trace-on-first-retry, screenshot+video-on-failure.

### 2. Critical e2e flows (must cover)
- **Gallery loads** — `/` renders the model grid; each expected model appears; thumbnails resolve (no broken image requests).
- **Model page loads** — click a gallery card → navigate to model page → param form renders with correct field types + defaults from the .scad source.
- **Live preview updates on param change** — critical given the silent-bug history from Phase 2. Change a known param (e.g. `can_diameter` on the 46mm holder), wait for debounce, assert the rendered viewer actually reflects the change. Options: read STL bbox via a debug hook, or snapshot pixel differences between two param values.
- **STL download works** — click Download STL, intercept the download, assert file size > 0, parse STL header, assert bbox shifted when params changed.
- **Form validation** — reject out-of-range param values; UI shows helpful error.
- **Error paths** — malformed model URL → 404; server export timeout → inline error rendered, not a silent failure.
- **Navigation** — gallery ↔ model page ↔ gallery round-trip preserves state sensibly.

### 3. Test data
- Fixture models: the existing annotated corpus is fine. Do NOT check in generated STLs; tests generate on the fly.
- Add one `tests/fixtures/bug_regression.scad` covering the Phase 1/2 param-override bug specifically — a model where the default render and a param-overridden render produce visibly different geometry. Prevents silent regressions.

### 4. GitHub Actions workflow
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with jobs:
  - `unit`: `npm test` (Vitest on parser + closure walker + applyParamOverrides)
  - `e2e-local`: `npm run build && npm start` in background, run Playwright against `localhost:3000`
  - `e2e-preview` (optional, nice-to-have): pull request deploys a Vercel preview, run Playwright against the preview URL
- Cache npm deps + Playwright browsers between runs.
- Upload Playwright traces + screenshots as workflow artifacts on failure.
- Status checks required on main branch protection (post-merge; don't block the current no-PR flow unless user changes policy).

### 5. Developer ergonomics
- `npm run test:e2e` for headed local development
- `npm run test:e2e:ui` for Playwright's UI mode
- `npm run test:all` runs unit + e2e
- README section covering 'how to add a new e2e test' and 'how to debug a CI failure'

## Validation
- All flows pass locally and in CI against a freshly built app
- Deliberately break a critical path (e.g. disable `applyParamOverrides`) → relevant test fails with a useful diff, not a cryptic timeout
- CI total runtime stays under ~5 min for good feedback loops
- Playwright traces on failure are actually useful (screenshot + network log + console)

## Deliverable
- `playwright.config.ts`
- `tests/e2e/` directory with the flows above
- `tests/fixtures/` for any special test-only .scad files
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- README updates covering local and CI test runs

## Out of scope
- Visual regression testing (image-diff). Not v1; add later if flakiness forces it.
- Load/perf testing of the server export function. Separate bead when we see real usage.
- Cross-device (mobile) e2e. Optional v2.
- Auth test coverage (no auth exists).
- Flake quarantine infrastructure. Start simple; revisit if flakes show up.

## Context
Parent project: parametric OpenSCAD web app. Final phase in the 0→1→2→3→4 chain. Added after witnessing a silent render-bug (Phase 2 fix for Phase 1 param-override issue) — e2e coverage is the reliable guard against that class of failure.

title: Phase 3: Model gallery + filesystem auto-discovery + Vercel deployment
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-7u9z
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T02:00:04Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal
Turn the single-model demo (Phase 1+2) into a browsable gallery of all parametric models in `models/`, annotate the remaining .scad files, and ship to production on Vercel.

## Depends on
Phase 2 (/api/export working end-to-end).

## Scope
### 1. Filesystem auto-discovery
- At build time, scan `models/*.scad` and emit a manifest (title, slug, param schema, thumbnail path)
- Title derivation: first non-blank comment line, or filename-based default
- Route pattern: `/models/<slug>` (slug = filename without extension, dashes)

### 2. Index page
- `/` or `/models` shows a grid: thumbnail + title + param count + link
- Thumbnail: use the top-view render from `renders/<stem>/top.png` if present; otherwise render once at build time
- No search/filter in v1 — linear grid is fine

### 3. Annotation migration
- Add inline `// @param` annotations to all existing parametric .scad files:
  - `cylinder_holder_42mm_slot.scad`, `cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad`
  - `spraycan_holder_70mm.scad`, `spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad`, `spraycan_holder_77mm_slot.scad`
  - `smoke_motor_mount.scad`
- Preserve existing defaults; pick sensible min/max/step for sliders
- Any file without annotations still appears in the gallery but with a 'parameters not yet annotated' placeholder — don't hard-fail

### 4. Vercel deployment
- `vercel.ts` config (not vercel.json) per current Vercel guidance
- Link project: `vercel link`
- Env vars: any required at build time or runtime (openscad binary path, etc.) documented in `app/README.md`
- Preview deploy → smoke test → promote to production
- Custom domain optional (default `<project>.vercel.app` is fine for v1)

### 5. Deprecate or keep existing static scripts
- Decide: does `scripts/serve.py` (the read-only artifact browser) stay as a local dev tool, or get removed once the Next.js app covers the same ground? Default: keep for now, revisit later.

## Validation
- Preview URL renders gallery + at least 3 working model pages
- Each page: form updates live preview, download STL works, STL imports cleanly in a slicer
- Vercel build green; function cold-start for `/api/export` under ~5s on first hit

## Deliverable
- Gallery page + filesystem scanner + manifest build step
- All existing models annotated
- Deployed preview URL + promoted production URL
- `app/README.md` covering local dev, deploy, annotation convention

## Out of scope
- Auth / user accounts
- Saved/shared parameter sets
- Server-side caching of STL exports (future)
- PWA / offline mode
- Analytics beyond what Vercel provides by default

## Context
Parent project: parametric OpenSCAD web app. Final phase in the 0→1→2→3 chain.
notes:
## Phase 2 landed (st-5t0, commit 70003bd on main) — context for Phase 3

Server export is live:
- POST /api/export returns STL stream, runs openscad-wasm server-side (single codepath — no native openscad binary to package; good for Vercel bundle size).
- maxDuration=120 on the route for CSG headroom.
- Path allowlist (libs/ + models/, reject .., absolute, non-.scad).
- Param names + types validated against parsed @param manifest.
- UI has Download STL button with loading/error states.

**Bug caught & fixed that affects prior phases:** openscad-wasm-prebuilt silently ignores -D flags AND OpenSCAD's last-assignment-wins scoping defeats prepended preludes. Phase 1 preview was silently buggy (form changes did not actually reach the render). Fix: `applyParamOverrides()` rewrites each @param's assignment line in-source. Applied to both preview and export paths. Worth flagging: test-pass != UX-correct; validation matrix caught it (default ~780KB, can_diameter=80 → ~787KB, bbox max-x 26.25→43.25).

Validation end-to-end: clean.

## Phase 3 context
- Gallery + filesystem auto-discovery + annotate remaining .scad files + Vercel deploy.
- Reuse everything: parser, closure walker, applyParamOverrides, /api/export, /api/source, ModelStudio component.
- Annotation pass: 5 files to annotate (cylinder_holder_42mm_slot, spraycan_holder_70mm, spraycan_holder_70mm_slot, spraycan_holder_77mm_slot, smoke_motor_mount). The 46mm one is already done from Phase 1.
- Vercel: use vercel.ts (not vercel.json). Check bundle size with openscad-wasm + libs included — may need to mark libs/ as runtime-fetched via /api/source rather than bundled.
- Thumbnails: existing `renders/<stem>/top.png` from scad-render.py skill are useful; can be generated at build time or lazily via /api/render-thumbnail.
- Decision for Phase 3 polecat: build-time manifest (faster cold starts, stale on lib changes) vs runtime fs scan (fresh, small cost per request). Build-time recommended for static-like UX.
close_reason: Phase 3 landed on main as 6acb3a2 (cherry-picked from polecat/obsidian-mo4ir4ch 07ebb45). 6th hq-wld occurrence; manual recovery applied.

title: Phase 2: Server-side STL export via Vercel Function
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-orws
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T01:37:54Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal
Add a Vercel Function that runs native `openscad` to produce an STL with the user's current parameter set, and wire a 'Download STL' button on the model page.

## Depends on
Phase 1 (the Next.js app + param form).

## Scope
### 1. Function definition
- Route: `POST /api/export` (or `app/api/export/route.ts` with App Router)
- Body: `{ model: string, params: Record<string, string|number|boolean> }` — model is a repo-relative path like `models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad`
- Response: `application/sla` (STL) stream + `Content-Disposition: attachment`
- Runtime: Fluid Compute, Node.js 24
- Timeout: bump above default if needed (render may take 10-30s on complex models)

### 2. OpenSCAD binary packaging
- Ship a Linux openscad binary in the function bundle OR via a Vercel-provided path — whichever works within the ~250MB function size ceiling
- Include BOSL2 + QuackWorks lib files as static assets the function can read
- Document packaging approach in `app/api/export/README.md` so future updates know how to bump the openscad version

### 3. Path safety
- Reject any model path that escapes the repo `models/` dir (no `..`, no absolute paths)
- Reject non-.scad extensions
- Validate param names against parsed param manifest for that model (no arbitrary -D injection)

### 4. UI wiring
- 'Download STL' button on the model page (from Phase 1)
- Shows loading state during the function call
- On success, triggers browser download
- On error, shows inline message (timeout, render failure, etc.)

## Validation
- Download STL for `cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad` with default params — verify in a slicer/viewer
- Change `can_diameter` via form, download again, verify bore diameter changed
- Malicious path input (`../../../etc/passwd`) is rejected with 400

## Deliverable
- Working `/api/export` route committed to main
- STL-download button on Phase 1's single-model page
- README updates

## Out of scope
- Gallery / multiple models on the same page (Phase 3)
- Deployment to Vercel prod (Phase 3)
- Caching export results (future optimization — not blocking)
- Progress streaming during long renders (future optimization)
- Auth / rate-limiting (public; may need revisiting if abused)

## Context
Parent project: parametric OpenSCAD web app for Vercel. Phase chain: Phase 0 → Phase 1 → **Phase 2 (this)** → Phase 3.
notes:
## Phase 1 landed (st-1wf, commit 80f9214 on main) — context for Phase 2

What's already in place:
- Next.js App Router app at repo root, TypeScript. `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `next.config.mjs`, `vitest.config.ts`.
- `lib/scad-params/` — @param parser, 16 tests.
- `lib/wasm/closure` — BFS include/use closure walker that mounts only the dep graph (fixes the 60s parse bottleneck from Phase 0).
- `lib/wasm/render` — openscad-wasm-prebuilt@1.2.0 loader with --backend Manifold.
- `app/api/source/` — read-only file server scoped to libs/ + models/.
- `components/ModelStudio`, `components/ParamForm`, `components/StlViewer` — UI coordinator, form, three.js viewer.
- 250ms debounce + monotonic render token (stale renders discarded).
- One working page at `/models/cylinder-holder-46` against `cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad`.

Phase 2 implementation notes:
- Add `app/api/export/route.ts` — POST, body { model, params }, returns STL stream.
- Native `openscad` binary packaging: the existing `scripts/export.py` already shells out to openscad successfully. Either (a) ship a Linux openscad binary in the function, (b) use a Vercel-provided binary if one exists, or (c) reuse openscad-wasm server-side with the server's higher timeout/memory ceiling (simpler, single codepath — explore first).
- Reuse param parser and closure walker from Phase 1 — same `-D` flag generation logic, same virtual fs mount pattern.
- Validation: reject `..`, absolute paths, non-.scad extensions; param names must appear in that model's parsed manifest.
- UI: add Download STL button to `ModelStudio`; loading state during request; inline error on failure.

Open question for Phase 2 polecat: is openscad-wasm acceptable server-side for STL export, or do we need native? Native gives better fidelity + speed for complex models but adds binary-packaging work. openscad-wasm keeps one codepath. Recommend trying WASM-server-side first; fall back to native only if quality/speed insufficient.

title: Phase 1: Next.js scaffold + inline param parser + single-model page with WASM preview
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-rozu
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-21T01:12:52Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal
First user-visible slice. Stand up a Next.js app in the `stuff` repo, parse one .scad file's inline parameters, render a form, render the model live via WASM as the user edits params. **No STL download yet** (that's Phase 2). **No gallery yet** (that's Phase 3). Single hardcoded model URL is fine.

## Depends on
st-556 (Phase 0 verdict). If Phase 0 returns `go-server-only`, this bead needs rescoping — the 'live WASM preview' part becomes a debounced server round-trip instead.

## Scope
### 1. Next.js app scaffold
- App Router (per Vercel guidance), TypeScript
- Lives alongside existing `scripts/`, `models/`, `libs/` — don't nest .scad files inside the Next.js tree; the app reads from the repo root
- Place app in `app/` or a subdir; don't break existing `scripts/serve.py` flow

### 2. Inline param parser
- Convention for inline annotations: `can_diameter = 46; // @param number min=20 max=200 step=1 label=\"Can diameter\"`
- Accept types: `number`, `integer`, `boolean`, `string`, `enum` (with `choices=a|b|c`)
- Parse the `// === User-tunable parameters ===` section only (stop at next `===` comment)
- Module lives under `lib/scad-params/` — pure function, no fs coupling, unit tested

### 3. Form UI
- Auto-generate form controls from parsed params (number → slider + numeric input, bool → checkbox, enum → select)
- Debounce to avoid re-rendering on every keystroke (~250ms)
- Show current param values serialized as `-D` flags in a collapsible panel (useful for debugging + copying)

### 4. WASM live preview
- Use whatever `openscad-wasm` build Phase 0 validated
- Render model on param change, show resulting PNG (top view or iso — pick one)
- Show bbox + render time
- Clear error state if WASM fails

### 5. One working page
- Hardcoded target: `cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad` at `/models/cylinder-holder-46` or similar
- Link from repo README for now; gallery routing is Phase 3

## Deliverable
- Next.js app committed to main, runnable via `npm run dev`
- Param parser + tests
- Working single-model page with live WASM preview
- Brief README note in `app/README.md` (or wherever) on how to run locally

## Out of scope
- STL download / server export function (Phase 2)
- Gallery / model index / routing across all models (Phase 3)
- Vercel deployment config (Phase 3)
- Modifying existing `scripts/` serve/render/export flow
- Auth
- Storage of user-adjusted params server-side

## Validation
- Hand-tune params via form, observe render update within 1s on a warm page
- Parser unit tests cover number/int/bool/enum with and without min/max/step/label
- No regressions: `scripts/serve.py`, `scripts/render.py`, `scripts/export.py` still work

## Annotation migration
The bead does NOT require re-annotating all existing .scad files. Pick ONE file (`cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad`) to annotate, enough to demonstrate the parser. Other files get annotated as part of Phase 3 or on demand.

## Context
Parent project: parametric OpenSCAD web app for Vercel. Phase chain: Phase 0 (spike) → **Phase 1 (this)** → Phase 2 (STL download) → Phase 3 (gallery + deploy).
notes:
## Phase 0 findings (from st-556, landed on main as 676a247) — do not re-derive

- **Pin** openscad-wasm-prebuilt@1.2.0 (from lorenzowritescode/openscad-wasm, packages OpenSCAD 2025.01.19). The official openscad/openscad-wasm is stale (2022) and fails on BOSL2.
- **MANDATORY** --backend Manifold on every WASM invocation. CGAL default blows RSS to 3.3 GB on trivial BOSL2 models and aborts.
- **Library mount:** mount repo libs into Emscripten FS at /libraries/<libname>/... — that is OpenSCAD's built-in search path so existing 'include <BOSL2/std.scad>' resolves with no -I flags. Follow symlinks (libs/BOSL2 etc. are symlinks).
- **Parse is the bottleneck, not CSG.** 60s wall on spraycan model due to include resolver scanning all 576 mounted .scad files. Internal render is sub-second for every probe.
- **Build a closure-only mounter in Phase 1.** Parse the target model's includes, mount only the dep graph. Warm renders should get under 5s.
- **Render policy:** WASM first with 5s budget; if exceeded or error, show fallback state (server fallback wires up in Phase 2).
- **Harness to copy from:** experiments/wasm-spike/harness.mjs demonstrates the mount + probe pattern. Reproduce: cd experiments/wasm-spike && npm install && node harness.mjs
- **Target model note:** cylinder_holder_46mm_slot renders in 9s wall / 0.4s internal — fine for WASM preview after closure-only mounter lands. Spraycan is the slow case we'd hit later.

title: Expand gusset on cylinder_holder_46mm_slot to fix build-plate adhesion
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-6km8
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-20T22:52:08Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Problem
Prints of `models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad` are failing — initial layers curl up off the build plate because the first-layer contact area is insufficient. The flat back face of the Multiconnect backer contributes most of the footprint, but the backer/ring gap is supported only by a small trapezoidal gusset (20→8 mm × 6 mm deep) that adds little first-layer area.

## Scope
**Focus on the 46mm design only.** Once we have a working geometry, we'll backport the same approach to 42mm and other cylindrical holders in follow-up beads. Do NOT touch 42mm, 70mm, 77mm, or smoke_motor_mount in this bead.

## Approach (starting point — open to alternates)
Expand the gusset to fill more of the gap between the backer and ring:
- Widen `gusset_front_w` (currently 8mm) toward the ring OD (~52.5mm) so the gusset meets a larger chord of the ring
- Optionally increase `gusset_depth` (currently 6mm) so the gusset reaches farther into +Y, adding footprint
- Consider a fillet/web tangent to the ring tangent instead of (or in addition to) the straight-trapezoid sweep
- Keep the gusset bottom-aligned with `cup_z0` so first-layer area grows

Preserve: overall layout (backer front-face on build plate, ring axis along Z), clearance, wall thickness, Multiconnect backer dims, drip-cup design.

## Iteration protocol
1. `python3 .claude/skills/scad-render/scripts/render.py --model models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad` — verify renders_ok, check bbox
2. Inspect bottom view (first-layer footprint) and report contact area estimate
3. Propose parameter change + rationale, apply, re-render
4. Stop when first-layer area is meaningfully larger (target: at least 2× current gusset-contributed area) without breaking aesthetics or overhangs

## Deliverable
- Updated `models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad`
- Brief note in the bead describing final gusset params + estimated first-layer area gain
- PR landing the change

## Out of scope (defer to follow-up beads)
- 42mm backport
- 70mm/77mm spraycan holder changes
- Slicer-level mitigations (brim, raft) — we want geometry that prints without those
- H2S vs X1C differences
notes:
Gusset enlarged to fix build-plate adhesion.

Print orientation analysis: Z-axis vertical (PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX=[52.5,59,80]).
Bed-touching faces: backer bottom (50×6.5 strip, ~325mm²), cup floor (240°
wedge of OD 52.5 circle, ~1440mm²), gusset trap (was ~74mm²).

The narrow backer strip + small trapezoidal gusset created two thin contact
patches with poor edge bond - prone to corner curl.

Parameter changes (models/cylinder_holder_46mm_slot.scad):
- gusset_back_w:  20 → 46 (~full backer width, 2mm clear each side)
- gusset_front_w:  8 → 30 (front edge embraces cup tangent zone)
- gusset_depth:    6 → 10 (reaches past ring tangent at Y=3.25, merges
                            into cup wall)
- gusset module: dropped bottom chamfer (faces build plate; chamfer was
  shrinking first-layer area for an invisible edge)

Estimated first-layer area gain:
- Gusset alone: 74 → 325 mm² (~4.4×, exceeds 2× target)
- Total bed contact: ~1839 → ~2090 mm² (~14% overall)
- Critical: turns the narrow backer strip from a separate weak patch
  into a continuous bonded region with the cup, eliminating the
  most-likely curl initiation site

Renders verified: cradle/ring profile unchanged, gusset visible in side
and front views as a wide skirt at base of cradle. Top view bbox unchanged
(52.5×59), so PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX still accurate.

Out of scope per bead: 42mm/70mm/77mm backports (follow-up beads).

title: Build scad-send skill: STL → cloud-printed on Bambu X1C
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-uqaa
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-20T03:36:57Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Implement the cloud-send path validated in st-5zs/st-qxt/st-g7x/st-98m. Goal: take an STL from the existing scad-export output and cause the Bambu X1C to print it — via Bambu Cloud so the user keeps Handy + cloud working (LAN Mode would break Handy per st-98m).

## Approach (composition, not invention)
1. **Slice:** `bambu-studio --slice 0 --export-3mf out.3mf in.stl` with full-config machine/process/filament profiles (per st-5zs notes).
2. **Upload + start print:** `coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API` Python lib, cloud path. Per st-qxt: `start_cloud_print(device_id, filename)` against `api.bambulab.com`.
3. **Auth:** Bambu Cloud email+password + email 2FA OTP. `cli_tools/login.py` handles the flow; result is a bearer token. Token has an expiry — persist to a path outside the repo (e.g. `~/.config/bambu-send/token.json`), refresh when expired.

## Deliverable
New skill at `.claude/skills/scad-send/` following the shape of existing skills (scad-render, scad-export):
- `scripts/send.py` — takes `--model models/foo.scad` (or `--stl exports/foo.stl`) and a `--printer` alias, slices (if needed), uploads, starts print. Emits JSON verdict.
- `SKILL.md` — usage, prerequisites, auth setup steps.
- Config file (location TBD — suggest `~/.config/bambu-send/printers.yaml`) mapping printer alias → device_id + slicer profile refs. Not checked in.
- One-time `scripts/login.py` wrapper to walk the user through Bambu Cloud auth + token persistence.

## Out of scope (defer)
- H2S support — retarget once X1C works (st-5zs noted ~30min smoke test once the send path is solid).
- LAN fallback — we're explicitly choosing cloud per user call.
- Print monitoring / completion callback — just start the print, report success/failure of the START.
- Bambu Studio profile generation — user exports their preferred profile from Studio GUI once; skill references those files.
- Multi-printer dispatch / farm mode.

## Constraints
- **Library is unofficial.** `coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API` README says "Big commit in progress" and the caveat "some regions may not support" (st-g7x confirmed US works). Keep the adapter shim thin so if the lib breaks we can swap implementations without rewriting the skill contract.
- **Account gating:** Printer must NOT be in LAN-Only Mode (enforce/detect + error clearly if so). Post-Jan-2025 Authorization Control requires cloud reachability — fine for our path.
- **Secrets:** Bambu credentials and the bearer token MUST NOT land in the repo or in bead comments. `.gitignore` the config dir if it ends up anywhere near the repo.

## Deliverable phases
**Phase 1 (this bead): scaffold without live test.** Polecat cannot test end-to-end (no creds, no printer). Deliverable is:
1. Skill layout + scripts that *would* work.
2. A runbook section in SKILL.md explaining exactly what user needs to do for the first live test (create Bambu Cloud account bindings, run login.py, export a profile from Studio, provide device_id).
3. Dry-run / mock-mode flag so polecat can exercise the non-cloud parts (arg parsing, slice invocation, error shapes) without network.
4. `pip`-installable requirements pinned (lib version + any transitive deps that matter).

**Phase 2 (follow-up bead after user runs first test):** iterate based on what breaks in live run.

## Success criteria for Phase 1
- `python3 .claude/skills/scad-send/scripts/send.py --help` works
- Dry-run mode completes without network
- SKILL.md has a runbook a reasonably-technical user can follow end-to-end
- One-liner in bead notes showing the exact commands user will run for first live test

## References
- st-5zs: baseline research
- st-qxt: CLI/Connect coverage (both blocked for cloud-send — confirms we need the unofficial lib)
- st-g7x: US region supported
- st-98m: LAN Mode blocks Handy (reason we picked cloud)
- Library: https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API
- API_AUTHENTICATION.md: https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API/blob/main/API_AUTHENTICATION.md
- API_FILES_PRINTING.md: https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API/blob/main/API_FILES_PRINTING.md
- Bambu Studio CLI wiki: https://github.com/bambulab/BambuStudio/wiki/Command-Line-Usage
notes:
Phase 1 (scaffold) complete.

Deliverables:
- .claude/skills/scad-send/SKILL.md — runbook + verdict enum + config schema
- scripts/send.py — argparse CLI, --model/--stl/--3mf, --dry-run, JSON-to-stdout
- scripts/login.py — interactive Bambu Cloud login → ~/.config/bambu-send/token.json (chmod 600)
- scripts/_cloud.py — single binding line to bambulabs_cloud_api (isolates upstream churn)
- requirements.txt — git-pinned coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API + PyYAML
- printers.example.yaml — sample config (default_printer, printers, profile_sets)
- _tests/test_send.py — 7 dry-run tests, all pass; no network, slicer shimmed on PATH

Phase 1 success criteria met:
- python3 .claude/skills/scad-send/scripts/send.py --help works
- Dry-run completes without network
- Tests: 7 passed in 0.10s

Phase 2 (live verification, requires hardware + creds):
  pip install -r .claude/skills/scad-send/requirements.txt
  python3 .claude/skills/scad-send/scripts/login.py
  cp .claude/skills/scad-send/printers.example.yaml ~/.config/bambu-send/printers.yaml
  # edit yaml: device_id from printer touchscreen, profile_set paths to FULL exported JSONs
  python3 .claude/skills/scad-send/scripts/send.py --3mf <file>.3mf --dry-run
  python3 .claude/skills/scad-send/scripts/send.py --3mf <file>.3mf  # live send

title: Research follow-up: Bambu Handy app on LAN when printer is in LAN Mode?
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-n6jr
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-20T03:25:49Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Follow-up to st-5zs/st-qxt/st-g7x. Context: we're weighing LAN-send path (requires Developer Mode + LAN Mode, which disables cloud). User wants to keep using Bambu Handy app — does Handy work against a printer in LAN Mode if the phone is on the same WiFi?

## Question
When a Bambu X1C (or H2S) is in **LAN Mode** (cloud disabled), can the **Bambu Handy mobile app** still:
1. Connect to the printer on the local network?
2. View live status / camera?
3. Start a print from the app?
4. Manage filaments / send commands?

Some or all of these may work via LAN Mode's local MQTT, OR Handy may be strictly cloud-dependent.

## What to check
- Bambu Lab official docs / wiki on Handy + LAN Mode compatibility
- Reddit / forum threads — empirical reports: "I put my printer in LAN mode, can I still use Handy?"
- Does Handy have a LAN/offline mode? Developer Mode requirements?
- Is there any known difference between X1C Handy support and H2-series Handy support in LAN Mode?

## Out of scope
- LAN send from scripts (already answered)
- Desktop Studio behavior (we've covered that separately)

## Deliverable
Bullet answer:
- Handy on LAN Mode: full / partial / blocked
- Specifically what works and what doesn't
- Any setup steps required (e.g. "Handy must be logged in once to the cloud before LAN use")
- Sources + dates. Under 200 words.
notes:
---
## Addendum (st-98m, 2026-04-20)

### Verdict: Handy on LAN Mode
**Blocked entirely.** The official Bambu Handy app requires a Bambu Cloud connection and does not work against a printer in LAN Mode — even on the same WiFi. This applies to X1-series and H2-series alike.

### What works / doesn't
- ✗ Handy cannot connect to a LAN-Mode printer at all (no status, no camera, no print start, no commands)
- ✗ No "LAN mode" / "offline mode" toggle exists inside Handy
- ✓ Bambu Studio / Orca on a desktop on the same LAN works (status, camera with LAN Liveview, send prints)
- ✓ Third-party mobile / web alternatives fill the gap: SimplyPrint, Bambuddy, Home Assistant `ha-bambulab`

### Setup gotchas
- LAN Mode toggle is on the printer touchscreen (Settings → General → LAN Only Mode); grab the 8-char Access Code from the same screen — Studio needs it.
- For camera over LAN, also enable "LAN Only Liveview" (separate toggle on H2 series; X1 exposed it from firmware 01.06+).
- If you ever want Handy back, you must disable LAN Mode and re-bind the printer to your Bambu Cloud account.

### X1C vs H2 series
No documented difference for Handy — Handy is cloud-only on both. H2D/H2S expose "LAN Only Liveview" as its own toggle and require Developer Mode (firmware 01.01.00.01+) for some third-party tooling; neither changes Handy's behavior.

### Implication for our workflow choice
LAN Mode is a hard tradeoff: we get the headless `bambulabs-api` send path, but the user loses Bambu Handy. Mitigation: Bambuddy (mobile-friendly self-hosted web UI) or SimplyPrint (hosted) cover the Handy use cases on LAN.

### Sources
- Bambu Lab Wiki — How to enable LAN Mode: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/knowledge-sharing/enable-lan-mode
- Bambu Lab H2 forum — How does "LAN only" mode work? (2025): https://forum.bambulab.com/t/how-does-the-lan-only-mode-works/179605
- Bambu Lab forum — LAN only and Bambu mobile: https://forum.bambulab.com/t/lan-only-and-bambu-mobile/24697
- Bambu Lab forum — Understanding limitations of LAN Mode: https://forum.bambulab.com/t/understanding-limitations-of-lan-mode-getting-away-from-bbl-cloud/140005
- SimplyPrint Helpdesk — Bambu LAN-only + Developer Mode (2025): https://help.simplyprint.io/en/article/bambu-lab-lan-only-mode-and-developer-mode-how-to-enable-xa0hch/
- bababuilds.com — Bambu Farm Manager: Cloud-Free Print-Farm Control: https://bababuilds.com/blog/bambu-farm-manager-cloud-free-print/
- XDA — I finally have full control of my Bambu Lab printer (2025): https://www.xda-developers.com/finally-have-full-control-bambu-lab-printer-ditched-bambu-cloud/

**Research caveat:** WebFetch was blocked (402/403) on wiki/forum pages, so exact dates couldn't be verified per-source. Findings synthesized from search-result excerpts across 3 independent queries; no conflicting reports surfaced.
close_reason: no-changes: research-only addendum appended; Handy is cloud-only on both X1 and H2 series. LAN Mode = no Handy. Mitigation: Bambuddy or SimplyPrint cover the mobile use case.

title: Research follow-up: Bambu Cloud remote-print region support?
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-nf80
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-20T02:37:24Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Follow-up to st-qxt. Prior finding noted `coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API`'s `start_cloud_print` has caveat "some regions may not support remote printing."

## Question
Which regions specifically support remote printing via Bambu Cloud, and which don't? Primary interest: **US** (that's where we are).

## What to check
- coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API repo docs/source — does it enumerate supported regions or just parrot Bambu's own restriction?
- Bambu Lab official docs / wiki / forum: is there a region matrix for remote printing (print start via cloud, vs. monitoring-only)?
- Community threads (Reddit r/BambuLab, Bambu forum): empirical reports — who's successfully started cloud prints from which region?
- Is there a distinction between "cloud storage enabled" vs "remote print enabled"? User's account may have one but not the other.

## Out of scope
- Whether LAN path is better (already answered — yes for reliability)
- Implementation
- Other cloud features (slicing, monitoring, etc.)

## Deliverable
Short bullet answer:
- Regions confirmed supporting cloud remote-print (start/trigger, not just status)
- Regions confirmed NOT supporting it
- US specifically: yes/no/unknown, with evidence
- Any account-level gating (e.g. must enable cloud storage first)
- Cited sources + dates. Keep under 200 words.
notes:
---
## Addendum (st-g7x, 2026-04-20)

### Regions: cloud remote-print
- **Supported (global cloud, `api.bambulab.com`):** US, EU, UK, AU, CA, JP, SEA, etc. — anywhere served by global Bambu Cloud / Bambu Handy. The Bambu Studio wiki documents Auto/Cloud send as a default feature with no country exclusion list.
- **Separate (China cloud, `api.bambulab.cn`):** Mainland-China "domestic" accounts/printers — different endpoint, requires a Chinese mobile OTP. Cross-region (CN account ↔ global printer or vice versa) does NOT work; this is the actual region lock people hit.
- **Not supported:** No region is documented as cloud-blocked on the global endpoint. coelacant1's "some regions may not support" caveat is a generic hedge — neither the repo nor Bambu publishes a denylist.

### US specifically
**Verdict: yes.** US accounts on `api.bambulab.com` routinely start cloud prints via Studio / Handy / the unofficial API. No Bambu doc, forum thread, or repo issue surfaces a US-side block.

### Account-level gating
- Account region is chosen at signup (Global vs China) and is effectively locked; printer must be bound to a same-region account.
- Printer must NOT be in LAN-Only Mode (LAN-Only disables cloud send by design).
- Post-Jan-2025 firmware "Authorization Control" requires cloud reachability for network-initiated prints even in LAN — independent of geography.
- Token from `cli_tools/login.py` requires email 2FA; no extra "cloud storage" toggle beyond having a normal global account.

### Sources
- coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API — API_FILES_PRINTING.md (2025-10-25): https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API/blob/main/API_FILES_PRINTING.md
- coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API — API_AUTHENTICATION.md (region: global|china only): https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API/blob/main/API_AUTHENTICATION.md
- Bambu Lab Wiki — Remote Control & Monitoring: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/software/bambu-studio/remote-control
- Bambu Lab Forum — Region Lock (China): https://forum.bambulab.com/t/bambu-lab-region-lock-china/6602
- Bambu Lab Forum — China-Locked H2D vs Global: https://forum.bambulab.com/t/verdict-china-locked-h2d-vs-global/191660
- Slashdot — Bambu Authorization Control beta concerns (2025-01-25): https://hardware.slashdot.org/story/25/01/25/031258/bambu-labs-3d-printer-authorization-update-beta-sparks-concerns
close_reason: no-changes: research-only addendum appended; US works (global cloud at api.bambulab.com). The only real region lock is global vs china (mutually exclusive accounts/endpoints). coelacant1's 'some regions' caveat is a hedge, not a documented matrix.

title: Research follow-up: Bambu Studio CLI cloud-send? Bambu Connect useful?
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-xb6u
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-20T01:55:21Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Follow-up to st-5zs (Bambu direct-send research). Two narrow questions:

## Q1: Can Bambu Studio CLI send prints through the cloud?
Prior finding was that `bambu-studio --slice` exports 3MF headlessly, and the LAN send path uses `bambulabs-api` (FTPS + MQTT signed). But the CLI also has GUI-mode "Print Plate" in the app. Can the CLI itself push a print via Bambu Cloud (auth'd against the user's Bambu account) so we don't need Developer Mode / LAN Mode? Check:
- Bambu Studio CLI flags/subcommands for cloud send (e.g. `--upload`, `--print`, `--send-cloud`)
- Whether there's a `bambu-studio --send` or similar
- Whether `bambu-connect://` URL scheme counts (probably not — it's GUI-mediated per prior research)
- Any community tool that does cloud-side print dispatch (vs LAN)

## Q2: Does Bambu Connect buy us anything?
Bambu Connect was flagged in prior research as "GUI-only, still requires human Send click". Double-check:
- Is there any headless Bambu Connect invocation (CLI flags, config-file mode, URL scheme that auto-confirms)?
- Does Bambu Connect enable any send scenarios that `bambulabs-api` can't (e.g. cloud send, firmware-signed paths, H2S coverage)?
- If strictly worse than the direct-LAN path, document that and we move on.

## Scope
Research only — same as st-5zs. No code. Append findings to this bead's notes.

## Deliverable
Concise addendum: yes/no verdicts with cited sources + dates. Keep under 300 words.
notes:
---
## Addendum (st-qxt, 2026-04-20)

### Q1: Bambu Studio CLI cloud-send?
**Verdict: no** — the official `bambu-studio` CLI is slicing-only (load/export/arrange/orient/scale/log). No `--upload`, `--print`, `--send`, or `--cloud-print` flag exists in the documented options as of the wiki's 2026-03-31 revision.
- Community workaround for cloud dispatch: `coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API` documents `start_cloud_print(device_id, filename)` against the Bambu Cloud REST/MQTT layer (doc dated 2025-10-25), but with hard caveats: "not all accounts have cloud storage enabled" and "some regions may not support remote printing." Not a reliable production path.
- `maziggy/bambuddy` and `davglass/bambu-cli` dispatch via FTP+MQTT (LAN), same class as `bambulabs-api`, not cloud.

### Q2: Bambu Connect useful for headless?
**Verdict: no** — Bambu Connect is GUI-only. The `bambu-connect://import-file?path=…&name=…&version=1.0.0` URL scheme launches the GUI with the file pre-loaded; the user still clicks Send. No documented CLI flag, config-file mode, or auto-confirm parameter.
- Bambu Connect is the *required* shim for third-party slicers post-auth-control (Jan 2025), but only for interactive workflows. It does not enable any path `bambulabs-api` can't already hit on LAN + Developer Mode.
- For headless dispatch, `bambulabs-api` (FTPS + signed MQTT, LAN + Developer Mode) remains the only reliable option. Cloud dispatch via the unofficial Cloud API is possible but fragile / region-gated.

### Sources
- Bambu Studio CLI Wiki (last edited 2026-03-31): https://github.com/bambulab/BambuStudio/wiki/Command-Line-Usage
- Bambu Connect (beta) Wiki: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/software/bambu-connect
- Bambu Lab Third-party Integration Wiki (URL scheme spec): https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/software/third-party-integration
- coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API — API_FILES_PRINTING.md (2025-10-25): https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API/blob/main/API_FILES_PRINTING.md
- davglass/bambu-cli (LAN-only FTP+MQTT): https://github.com/davglass/bambu-cli
- maziggy/bambuddy (LAN FTP+MQTT dispatch): https://github.com/maziggy/bambuddy
- All About Bambu — auth-control breaking 3rd-party integration (2025-01-17): https://www.allaboutbambu.com/2025/01/17/bambu-lab-set-to-introduce-some-authorization-control-mostly-breaking-third-party-integration/
close_reason: no-changes: research-only addendum appended; Q1=no (CLI is slice-only, no cloud-send flag), Q2=no (Bambu Connect strictly GUI). bambulabs-api remains the only reliable headless path.

title: Research: direct print-send from workflow to Bambu X1C + H2S
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-m0p9
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-19T23:57:28Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Today the end of the SCAD workflow is: export STL → open Bambu Studio GUI → import → slice → send. We want to automate the send (ideally end-to-end: STL → slice → print over LAN) so the polecat can close the loop without manual GUI steps.

## Scope
**Research only.** No code, no skill changes. Deliver a written recommendation bead (or update this bead's description) with a verdict plus a concrete proposed path forward.

## Hardware
- Bambu Lab X1C (mature, well-documented)
- Bambu Lab H2S (newer — released late 2025 or early 2026; community tool support may lag)

## Questions to answer
1. Can a sliced 3MF/gcode be pushed to an X1C over LAN? Which protocol (MQTT, FTP, HTTP)? Auth story (access code, LAN-only mode, cloud mode)?
2. Does the H2S speak the same protocol? Any community tools confirmed working against H2S yet?
3. Official Bambu CLI or API? Leading community tools (e.g. `bambulabs-api` Python, `bambu-farm`, OpenBambuAPI, etc.) — names, URLs, maintenance status, last-commit dates.
4. Can Bambu Studio or OrcaSlicer be driven headlessly from CLI to slice STL → 3MF with a profile? Exact invocation.
5. Is there a published end-to-end recipe: STL → slice via CLI → upload over LAN → print starts?
6. Gotchas: LAN-only mode requirement, firmware version constraints, cloud-vs-LAN differences.

## Out of scope
- General Bambu Studio usage tutorials
- Printer reviews / material recommendations
- Actually implementing the send step (that's a follow-up bead once we know the path)

## Deliverable
A recommendation comment on this bead with:
- Verdict: feasible today / partially feasible / blocked
- Concrete proposed next step (e.g. "add a `scad-send` skill wrapping `bambulabs-api` vX.Y targeting X1C LAN mode; H2S deferred until tool Z lands support")
- Cited URLs + source dates (research cutoff, repo last-commit dates)

Distinguish clearly what's confirmed for X1C vs. speculative for H2S.
notes:
---
## Research findings (obsidian, 2026-04-19)

### Verdict
**Partially feasible — green for X1C, amber for H2S.**

End-to-end STL → slice → LAN upload → print start is solved for the **X1C**: Bambu Studio has a documented headless slice CLI, and several maintained community libraries wrap the MQTT+FTPS protocol for upload + start. The one hard gotcha (Jan-2025 firmware requires signed MQTT print commands) has been solved in the community — the signing key leaked and active tools (`bambulabs-api`, `bambu-mcp`) sign transparently.

For **H2S** (released late 2025 / early 2026): same MQTT-on-8883 + `bblp`/access-code auth almost certainly applies, but tool coverage is uneven. `bambulabs-api` 2.6.6 doesn't list H2S (calls H2D "untested"). `bambu-farm` and `bambu-mcp` don't list H2S. The leading HA integration has open issue #1506 "Feature: H2S Support" (Sep 2025) — broken sensor entities suggest the *status* payload differs even if the *command* channel is compatible. **Bambuddy** (web app, v0.2.3, 2026-04-19) is the only tool explicitly claiming H2S support.

### Proposed next step
Add a `scad-send` skill that shells out to `bambu-studio` CLI for STL→3MF, then uses `bambulabs-api` 2.6.6 (`upload_file` + `start_print`) to push over LAN to the X1C. Defer H2S until either `bambulabs-api` adds an explicit H2S target OR we confirm empirically (~30 min smoke test against the H2S IP+access code) that the same path works. Send-path is almost certainly identical bytes-on-wire; only monitoring fields likely differ.

### X1C: confirmed path
- **Transport:** MQTT/TLS on **port 8883** for commands+status; **FTPS port 990** for 3MF upload to printer SD.
- **Auth (LAN):** MQTT user `bblp`, password = LAN access code from printer display. Requires **LAN Mode** + **Developer Mode** (developer mode is needed when triggering print of a `.3mf` via `project_file` MQTT command on current firmware — and it disables cloud connectivity).
- **Signing:** Post-Jan-2025 firmware requires print-start MQTT commands signed (RSA-SHA256). `bambulabs-api` and `bambu-mcp` handle automatically using the leaked app cert. Any tool last-committed before ~Feb 2025 without signing support is broken.
- **Slicer CLI (Bambu Studio, official):**
  ```
  bambu-studio --orient --arrange 1 \
    --load-settings "machine.json;process.json" \
    --load-filaments "filament.json" \
    --slice 0 --export-3mf out.3mf in.stl
  ```
  Profile JSONs must be **full** configs (export from a saved user preset), not the partials under `resources/profiles/BBL/…`.
- **Sender:** `bambulabs-api` (PyPI v2.6.6, MIT, Python ≥3.10). Workflow: zip artifact so gcode is at `Metadata/plate_1.gcode` → `printer.upload_file(io, name)` → check FTP `"226"` → `printer.start_print(name, 1)`.
- **End-to-end recipe:** No single "STL in, printing out" blog post, but composing the two halves above is straightforward. The archived `darkorb/bambu-ftp-and-print` (2023) documents the older unsigned MQTT payload (`"command": "project_file"`, `"url": "ftp:///myfile.3mf"`) — useful as protocol reference, **do not use as-is** (predates signing).

### H2S: status as of April 2026
**Confirmed:** same MQTT-on-8883 endpoint, `bblp` + access-code auth. LAN Mode + Developer Mode exist on H2 series. **Bambuddy** (2026-04-19) explicitly lists H2S — strongest data point that the protocol is compatible.

**Speculative / not in tool support:** `bambulabs-api` 2.6.6 README enumerates X1C/P1S/A1 only ("H2D devices remain untested"), no H2S mention. `bambu-farm` and `bambu-mcp` printer matrices: no H2S. `ha-bambulab` issue #1506 (open Sep 2025) lists non-functioning entities (camera feed, heatbed type, nozzle type) — affects monitoring, not print triggering.

**Bottom line:** send path is almost certainly identical bytes-on-wire to X1C. Pilot against X1C first; treat H2S as a 30-minute smoke test.

### Tool landscape

| Tool | URL | Lang | Last commit | X1C | H2S | Notes |
|------|-----|------|-------------|-----|-----|-------|
| bambulabs-api | github.com/BambuTools/bambulabs_api | Python | 2026-01-31 (v2.6.6) | Yes | Not listed; H2D "untested" | MIT. MQTT+FTPS, signs commands. **Best headless fit.** |
| pybambu | github.com/greghesp/pybambu | Python | active (HA-focused) | Yes | HA #1506 open | Underlies ha-bambulab. Monitoring-first. |
| bambu-mcp | github.com/schwarztim/bambu-mcp | Python | active 2026 | Yes | No | MCP server; signs MQTT. |
| TFyre/bambu-farm | github.com/TFyre/bambu-farm | Java | active | Yes | No | Web UI multi-printer; no documented headless API. |
| maziggy/bambuddy | github.com/maziggy/bambuddy | TS+Py | 2026-04-19 | Yes | **Yes (explicit)** | AGPL. Web app/FastAPI. Only tool naming H2S. |
| davglass/bambu-cli | github.com/davglass/bambu-cli | JS | **archived 2025-01** | Yes | No | Author quit; broken on current firmware. |
| darkorb/bambu-ftp-and-print | github.com/darkorb/bambu-ftp-and-print | Python | **archived 2023-10** | X1 | No | Reference only — predates signing. |
| coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API | github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API | Python | "Big commit in progress" | Yes | Not listed | Cloud+MQTT+FTP; protocol-reversing reference. |
| OpenBambuAPI (Doridian) | github.com/Doridian/OpenBambuAPI | docs | active | P1/X1 | Not documented | **Canonical protocol reference** — not a library. |
| Bambu Studio CLI | github.com/bambulab/BambuStudio | C++ | active (official) | Yes | Yes (BBL ships H2S profiles) | **Official slicer** with headless `--slice`/`--export-3mf`. |
| OrcaSlicer CLI | github.com/OrcaSlicer/OrcaSlicer | C++ | active | Yes | Profile TBD | CLI multi-object/arrange crashes. Prefer Bambu Studio CLI. |
| Bambu Connect | wiki.bambulab.com/en/software/bambu-connect | (closed, official) | active | Yes | Yes | GUI-only; `bambu-connect://import-file?…` URL scheme still requires human "Send" click. **Not headless.** |

### Gotchas
- **Firmware authorization control (Jan 2025):** print-start MQTT must be signed RSA-SHA256. Tools without signing support are broken on current firmware.
- **LAN Mode + Developer Mode required** for `.3mf` `project_file` commands. Developer Mode disables cloud connectivity — decide up-front.
- **Access code rotates** when LAN Mode is toggled off/on. Store per-printer; plan for rotation.
- **MQTT TLS cert** is self-signed; every community lib disables verification. Fine on trusted LAN.
- **3MF packaging:** `start_print` expects ZIP with gcode at `Metadata/plate_1.gcode`. A Bambu-Studio-exported `.gcode.3mf` already matches.
- **Bambu Studio CLI profile JSONs** must be **full** configs (export from saved user preset), not the `resources/profiles/BBL/…` partials.
- **OrcaSlicer CLI instability:** `--load-assemble-list` and multi-plate flows segfault. Use Bambu Studio CLI.
- **H2S status payload likely differs** from X1C (per ha-bambulab #1506). Affects monitoring, not send.
- **bambulabs-api X1 camera not implemented** (<2.7.0) — X1 cam uses TUTK-like tunnel, not RTSP.
- **`bambu-connect://` URL scheme is not headless** — still requires GUI click on Send.

### Sources
- bambulabs-api PyPI v2.6.6 (2026-01-31): https://pypi.org/project/bambulabs-api/
- BambuTools/bambulabs_api: https://github.com/BambuTools/bambulabs_api
- bambulabs_api examples: https://bambutools.github.io/bambulabs_api/examples.html
- Doridian/OpenBambuAPI mqtt.md: https://github.com/Doridian/OpenBambuAPI/blob/main/mqtt.md
- schwarztim/bambu-mcp: https://github.com/schwarztim/bambu-mcp
- TFyre/bambu-farm: https://github.com/TFyre/bambu-farm
- maziggy/bambuddy: https://github.com/maziggy/bambuddy
- davglass/bambu-cli (archived): https://github.com/davglass/bambu-cli
- darkorb/bambu-ftp-and-print (archived 2023): https://github.com/darkorb/bambu-ftp-and-print
- coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API: https://github.com/coelacant1/Bambu-Lab-Cloud-API
- Bambu Studio CLI wiki: https://github.com/bambulab/BambuStudio/wiki/Command-Line-Usage
- OrcaSlicer CLI discussion #8593: https://github.com/OrcaSlicer/OrcaSlicer/discussions/8593
- Bambu blog — Authorization Control (Jan 2025): https://blog.bambulab.com/firmware-update-introducing-new-authorization-control-system-2/
- Consumer Rights Wiki — Reverse Engineering Bambu Connect: https://consumerrights.wiki/w/Reverse_Engineering_Bambu_Connect
- Bambu Wiki — Bambu Connect: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/software/bambu-connect
- Bambu Wiki — LAN Mode: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/knowledge-sharing/enable-lan-mode
- Bambu Wiki — Security: https://wiki.bambulab.com/en/general/bbl-security

- SimplyPrint H2S guide: https://simplyprint.io/setup-guide/bambu-lab/h2s
- ha-bambulab #1506 (H2S support, open): https://github.com/greghesp/ha-bambulab/issues/1506
- Bambu Forum — LAN-mode send tool: https://forum.bambulab.com/t/tool-to-send-files-to-bambu-connect-using-lan-mode/138714
- XDA — Bambu LAN-only workflow: https://www.xda-developers.com/finally-have-full-control-bambu-lab-printer-ditched-bambu-cloud/

title: v2 holder: increase back panel height so multiconnect slot tops don't break the top edge
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-uy16
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T14:54:53Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

User feedback after printing v2 (spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad, st-ljl):

The print came out great functionally, but aesthetically the tops of the multiconnect slots sit too close to the top edge of the backer panel. The slot openings break through the top edge, leaving small holes/cutouts where the panel terminates. No functional impact (the slots still engage the multiboard correctly) — just looks unfinished.

## Fix
Increase `backer_height` in models/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad so there's a clean continuous top edge above the highest slot opening.

Currently: `backer_height = 75`. Multiconnect slots are vertical, generated by multiconnectGenerator, and apparently extend close enough to the top to reach the chamfered edge.

## Suggested approach
1. Determine the actual slot top position from QuackWorks multiconnect geometry (libs/QuackWorks/Modules/multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad). Slots may have a known top inset, or may extend to the full height with the opening at the top.
2. Add 5-10mm to backer_height (or whatever ensures a continuous solid band of at least ~3mm above the topmost slot mouth).
3. Update PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX Z dimension accordingly (currently 75).
4. Re-render to renders/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot/ and verify the top edge is clean.
5. Re-export STL to exports/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.stl.

## Acceptance
- Top-down render shows continuous solid material above all multiconnect slot mouths
- Backer height increase is the minimum needed (don't waste filament)
- All other dimensions/tolerances unchanged
- PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX updated
- No regression in slot engagement (slots should still slide onto multiboard the same way)

Blocks: st-XXX (77mm variant — to be derived from this corrected v2 once it lands)

title: Read-only artifact browser web server for remote viewing
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-slwh
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-17T21:35:51Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Tiny Python http.server-based web UI to browse models, render PNGs, and download STLs from a remote rig host. Accessed via SSH port-forward. Scope 1 (pure static browser): no auth, no regen triggers, no dashboards. See notes.
notes:
## Intent

A small read-only web server that lets a remote operator browse and
download the artifacts the OpenSCAD workflow produces: renders (PNG),
models (SCAD source), and exports (STL). Accessed from the operator's
laptop via mosh + SSH port-forward to the remote host that runs the
rigs.

Scope is deliberately minimum-viable: **view renders, download STLs**.
Regeneration triggers and bead/convoy dashboards are explicitly
out-of-scope — file follow-up beads if we miss them.

## Behaviour

Landing page: list of models in `models/` by stem (e.g.
`spraycan_holder_70mm`, `spraycan_holder_70mm_slot`). Each entry shows:

- Render thumbnails (top/front/side/iso PNGs from `renders/<stem>/`)
  inline — clickable to full-size.
- Links to download `models/<stem>.scad` and
  `exports/<stem>.stl` (if present).
- File size + mtime next to each downloadable.
- A terse 'missing' indicator if the STL or renders don't exist yet.

That's the whole UI. No auth, no forms, no JS framework.

## Constraints

- Python stdlib only (`http.server` + some glue). No Flask, no
  FastAPI, no npm. Keep it sub-300 lines.
- Bind to `127.0.0.1` by default. Configurable via `--host` /
  `--port`. The operator is expected to SSH-port-forward.
- Read-only. Every response is either a file or directory index;
  no state mutation.
- Works from any CWD — take the repo root as `--root` (default: the
  directory the script is in, walking up to the repo root like the
  other skills do).

## Deliverables

- `scripts/serve.py` — the server.
- A short entry in the top-level `CLAUDE.md` or `AGENTS.md` (one
  paragraph) documenting the one-liner to run it + the port-forward
  snippet.
- Smoke test that imports the module and starts the server on an
  ephemeral port, requests `/`, asserts the response contains the
  name of an expected model in `models/`. Part of `scripts/smoke.sh`
  if that's the existing pattern, else a sibling test script the
  smoke wrapper can invoke.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `python3 scripts/serve.py` starts a server on `127.0.0.1:8765`
   (or similar) and prints the exact URL.
2. Visiting `/` in a browser shows the list of models with render
   thumbnails, when renders exist.
3. Clicking a thumbnail opens the full-size PNG.
4. STL and SCAD download links work and serve the right
   Content-Type (`model/stl`, `text/plain`).
5. A model that has `.scad` but no `.stl` / no renders shows as
   present-but-incomplete rather than 404'ing the whole page.
6. The smoke test passes.
7. No new runtime dependencies beyond Python stdlib.

## Out of scope (do NOT build)

- Auth (we're behind SSH).
- Writes / uploads / regen buttons — separate bead if wanted.
- Live reload / websockets.
- STL viewer / 3D preview in-browser — heavy; user has a desktop
  slicer for that.
- Bead/convoy dashboard — separate concern.

title: Multiconnect-slot variant of spraycan holder (v2, retry)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-9dxo
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-17T19:39:48Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Retry of st-5a2 now that st-kls is resolved and BOSL2 is pinned to 456fcd8. Use the SCAD draft from 'bd show st-5a2 --design' as the starting body. See notes for full spec.
notes:
## Intent

Re-attempt v2 (Multiconnect slot variant) of the 70mm spraycan holder
now that st-kls is resolved and BOSL2 is pinned to 456fcd8. The slot
backer should now render cleanly without any local library patches.

## Starting point

- **Read `bd show st-5a2 --design` first.** It contains the
  structural draft (parameters, module layout) authored before st-kls
  resolved. Use it as the starting body; you don't need to re-derive
  the design.
- **Read st-kls resolution notes.** They confirm that at pinned BOSL2
  456fcd8, `multiconnectGenerator(width=50, height=75, ...)` renders
  cleanly with both `onRampEnabled=true` and `slotDimple=true`. No
  workarounds needed.

## Design (from st-5a2, unchanged)

- Open-front C-ring cradle (front_opening_deg = 120°).
- Drip-cup floor ~5mm deep.
- Ring height ~35mm.
- Inner clearance 0.75mm radial (can ⌀70 → ring inner ⌀71.5).
- Wall thickness ~3mm.
- Multiconnect slot backer: `multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad`,
  2 slots × 25mm pitch, 75mm backer height, top-down insertion.

## Output

- `models/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad` (new file; do not touch
  the v1 `spraycan_holder_70mm.scad`).
- Rendered PNGs via `scad-render`.
- STL via `scad-export` at `exports/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.stl`.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Model compiles and renders with **zero local patches** to any lib.
   If any lib assertion fires, stop and reopen st-kls — do not ship
   patched builds.
2. `PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX` declared and matches measured render bbox.
3. Renders show the C-ring, drip cup, and slot geometry on the back
   face with dimples visible.
4. `scripts/smoke.sh` still passes.
5. STL watertight, <2MB.

## Post-completion — important

After `gt done`, before considering the work landed, the caller
(mayor) will verify the MR actually reached the merge queue. Tracked
as `hq-8kk`. Polecat side: you don't have to do anything extra, just
run `gt done` as usual.

title: Multiconnect-slot variant of spraycan holder (v2)
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-7wy8
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-17T16:04:50Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Second variant of the 70mm spraycan holder using Multiconnect slot backer instead of snap connector. Sidesteps the QuackWorks/BOSL2 snap incompatibility (st-kls). See notes for full spec.
notes:
## Intent

Second variant of the 70mm spraycan holder — swap the Multiboard snap
backer for a **Multiconnect slot** backer (slide-on dovetail). Avoids
the QuackWorks/BOSL2 snap incompatibility tracked in `st-kls` and
produces a cleaner build that doesn't depend on a local library patch.

## Reference

- v1 bead: `st-ivh` (closed). Model: `models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad`.
- Bug parking the snap path: `st-kls`.

## What stays the same from v1

- Open-front C-ring cradle.
- Shallow drip-cup floor (~5mm).
- Ring height ~35mm.
- Inner clearance 0.75mm radial (can ⌀70 → ring inner ⌀71.5).
- Wall thickness ~3mm.
- All invariants from `scad-new` (PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX, exposed params,
  `use <>` vs `include <>` rules).

## What changes

- Drop `QuackWorks/Modules/snapConnector.scad`.
- Use `QuackWorks/Modules/multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad` for the
  backer / mount geometry (slide-on slot).
- Backer sizing: match one Multiconnect slot width; stack
  vertically as needed for pullout resistance. Default to two
  slots spaced to spread load.
- Orient the slot for **top-down insertion** (standard Multiconnect).
- `snap_type` parameter is no longer meaningful — remove it. Replace
  with `slot_count` (default 2) and `slot_spacing_mm` (default sensible
  per library).

## Output

- `models/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad` (new file; do not overwrite v1).
- Rendered PNGs via `scad-render`.
- STL via `scad-export` at `exports/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.stl`.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Model compiles and renders without any local patches to QuackWorks
   or BOSL2. If it won't, stop and comment on `st-kls` — do not ship a
   second patched build.
2. PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX declared and matches measured render bbox.
3. Renders show the C-ring, drip cup, and the slot geometry on the
   back face.
4. STL is watertight and within sensible size (say < 2MB).
5. Any deviations from this spec are justified in bead notes before
   closing.

## Out of scope

- Physical print (human handles).
- Changing v1 artifacts — this is a parallel variant, not a
  replacement.
close_reason: no-changes: blocked on st-kls. Verified the Multiconnect slot path hits the same pinned-BOSL2 assertion as the snap path (two call sites: on-ramp line 211, dimple line 177 in multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad). On-ramp is parameter-disabled; dimples are not (disabling them removes the retention mechanism). Per spec criterion #1, stopped rather than shipping a patched build. Findings expanded on st-kls; full analysis + draft SCAD preserved in st-5a2 --design field for reuse once st-kls resolves.

title: QuackWorks snapConnector spin=[0,270,0] incompatible with pinned BOSL2
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-exku
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-17T16:20:41Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

QuackWorks/Modules/snapConnector.scad line 59 passes spin=[0,270,0] (a vector) to offset_sweep. Pinned BOSL2 663cd7c asserts is_finite(spin) in attachable(), so any use of snapConnectBacker() fails with 'Invalid spin: [0, 270, 0]'.

Repro: clone both libs at pinned SHAs, use <QuackWorks/Modules/snapConnector.scad>, call snapConnectBacker(). Asserts.

Durable fix options:
1. Add a patchset in libs/patches/ plus a post-clone script that applies it.
2. Pin QuackWorks to a newer SHA (if upstream has fixed) or accept HEAD.
3. Contribute an upstream PR.

Temporary workaround (applied locally by st-ivh polecat, not committed because libs/ is gitignored): replace `spin=[0,270,0]` with `yrot(270) offset_sweep(...)` on line 59.

Blocks: any future bead using Multiboard snaps via QuackWorks. st-ivh worked around this locally.
notes:
## Resolution (2026-04-17)

**Root cause:** BOSL2 PR #1475 (ae73c6d, 2024-09-27) tightened `attachable()` to
assert `is_finite(spin)`, rejecting the `spin=[x,y,z]` vector syntax. QuackWorks
uses vector spin in two call sites (snapConnector.scad:59, multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad:211)
and in additional attach() sites inside the slot dimple path.

**Upstream check:** QuackWorks pinned SHA 6123129 == upstream HEAD (AndyLevesque/QuackWorks).
No fix available upstream; the vector-spin syntax is still present. Priority 1 (bump
QuackWorks) is a dead end.

**Fix:** Priority 2 — pin BOSL2 back from 663cd7c (2026-04-16) to 456fcd8
(2024-09-22, last commit before PR #1475). This is the newest BOSL2 compatible
with every pinned QuackWorks backer. No local patches in libs/.

**Verified:**
- models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad (v1 snap backer) renders cleanly against
  pinned libs (no local patches). 207-element CSG tree, no errors.
- `multiconnectGenerator(width=50, height=75, multiconnectPartType="Backer",
  slotOrientation="Vertical")` from multiconnectSlotDesignBOSL.scad (the st-5a2
  slot path) renders cleanly — 288-element CSG tree, no errors. Both the
  on-ramp (line 211) and dimple (line 177) paths work at 456fcd8.
- scripts/smoke.sh passes (motor mount regression).

**Committed in e5982c5** on branch polecat/obsidian-mo3478y1.

**Follow-up:** st-nuu filed — scad-lib add currently clones HEAD rather than
the pinned SHA from libs/README.md. Today the pin is documentation-only, which
matters more now that the pin points to a non-HEAD commit.

**st-5a2 status:** The slot backer renders against 456fcd8 without any parameter
workarounds (onRampEnabled=true, slotDimple=true both work). The v2 spraycan
holder model from st-5a2's --design field can be re-authored without the
patches noted there. Leaving st-5a2 closed; whoever picks up v2 next should
read its --design field for the structural draft.

First real exercise of the OpenSCAD AI workflow. Authoring a parameterized Multiboard-mounted holder for a 70mm-diameter spray can. See notes for full spec.
notes:
## Completed workflow
- Authored models/spraycan_holder_70mm.scad with PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX = [77.5, 86.7, 75]
- scad-render: 4 PNGs, no warnings, measured bbox [77.5, 86.79] (matches anchor)
- scad-export: exports/spraycan_holder_70mm.stl, 2554 triangles, watertight, bbox [77.5, 86.675, 75]

## Deviations from spec
1. "Moderate WB" (unidirectional, won't-pop-up) snap geometry is NOT present in
   vendored QuackWorks at pinned SHA 6123129 — snapConnector.scad only ships the
   default bidirectional snap. Used snapConnectBacker() with holdingTolerance=1.0.
   snap_type parameter retained as informational label for future enhancement.
2. Backer thickness 3mm (spec said ~3mm default — matches).

## Library compatibility patch (local, not committed — libs/ is gitignored)
- libs/QuackWorks/Modules/snapConnector.scad line 59 patched to replace
  spin=[0,270,0] (legacy BOSL2 vector-spin syntax) with `yrot(270) offset_sweep(...)`
  because pinned BOSL2 663cd7c asserts is_finite(spin). Without the patch,
  snapConnectBacker() asserts before rendering. Filing follow-up bead to track
  a durable fix (patchset in repo, or pin QuackWorks to newer SHA with scalar spin).
