title: Blu flow meter v1 mount — docstring lists M3×10 but bolt path needs M3×20+
description:
v1 mount (models/blu_flow_meter_mount_80mm.scad pre-v2, st-jtn 60d9356) docstring + the st-jtn bead spec both call for M3×10 SHCS, but the actual bolt path from the cap-top countersink down to the insert at the saddle_bottom top spans ~16.7 mm of cap material — M3×10 falls ~3 mm short of even touching the insert.

Required bolt length for v1: ≥M3×20 (full insert engagement with M3×25).

Discovered during st-246 (v2 rework) — the v2 model now corrects the recommendation to M3×30 (which needs to bridge base 8 mm + saddle_bottom 16.7 mm + insert 5 mm). v2 supersedes v1, so this is a low-priority docstring fix:
- Edit v1's Hardware section to list M3×20 (minimum) with note pointing to v2 model as authoritative.
- No geometry change needed for v1; just the comment.

The operator presumably already discovered this at v1 install (had to source longer bolts than docs said) — but the docstring still lies, so worth a one-line fix to avoid future confusion if someone reprints v1.

title: [P3][fix] StlViewer: attach directional light to camera so detail stays visible across orbit angles
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-m0st
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-28T01:05:43Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Goal

Make the live STL preview's directional light follow the camera ("headlight" mode) instead of being fixed in world space, so faces visible to the user are always lit regardless of orbit angle. Cosmetic but quality-of-life — currently the back side of a model can be visually unreadable just because of where you orbited to.

## Current state

`components/StlViewer.tsx` currently does:

```typescript
scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.6));
const key = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.9);
key.position.set(1, 1, 1);
scene.add(key);
```

The directional light's world position is `(1, 1, 1)` — fixed. As `OrbitControls` rotates the camera around the model, the model rotates relative to the camera but the light stays put. Surfaces facing away from `(1, 1, 1)` are dim regardless of where the camera ended up.

## What to change

Move the directional light to be a child of the camera so its position is camera-local. As the camera orbits, the light orbits with it.

Three.js note: lights that are children of objects only render correctly if that object is part of the scene graph (so the matrix updates traverse). The camera typically isn't `scene.add`-ed in this codebase; verify and add `scene.add(camera);` if it isn't already there. Keep the ambient light unchanged (it's already direction-independent).

Suggested setup:

```typescript
scene.add(camera); // safe to call even if already attached; required for camera-attached light updates

scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.6));

const key = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.9);
// Camera-local offset: slightly up and right of the camera, slightly forward.
// Pure (0, 0, 0) makes the light effectively a perfectly-flat front-light;
// a small offset gives a touch of form-defining shading.
key.position.set(0.5, 1, 0.5);
camera.add(key);
```

The directional light's `target` defaults to world origin; with the light's *world* position now driven by the camera's transform, the resulting light direction rotates with the camera. Effective behavior: a slightly-up-and-right headlight.

If the polecat finds that the resulting render is too flat (no shading variation = pancake-looking surfaces), bump the local offset toward more `y` (up in camera space) before falling back to a more elaborate setup. **Don't introduce additional lights as a first move** — single key + ambient is the established pattern; preserve it.

## Tests

`components/StlViewer.test.tsx` already exists. Add (or extend) a test asserting:

- After scene initialization, the directional light is a child of the camera (`camera.children.some(c => c instanceof THREE.DirectionalLight)`).
- Ambient light is still a child of the scene.

This pins the structural choice so a future refactor doesn't silently revert it.

## Out of scope

- **No UI toggle** between "headlight" and "world-fixed" lighting modes. If the new default feels wrong in any case the user can call out, we add a toggle then.
- **No additional lights** (rim light, fill light, etc.). Keep the existing single-key + ambient setup; just change the parent.
- **No shadow / specular / material changes.** Lighting parent only.
- **No camera-position changes.** Default starting view stays the same.
- **No documentation work** — the change is small enough that it doesn't need a docs entry.

## Acceptance criteria

- Directional light is a child of the camera, not the scene.
- Camera is `.add`-ed to the scene if not already (verify whether this is needed in the existing flow).
- Local light position chosen to keep some form-defining shading (not perfectly flat front-light).
- StlViewer test extended to assert the light is camera-attached.
- Verify manually by rotating any model in the live preview: the lit side of the model stays facing the camera regardless of orbit angle.
- `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test` all green.

## Completion

Open MR titled `fix(viewer): camera-attached headlight so detail stays visible across orbit angles`. Refinery reviews. After merge, the live preview no longer hides detail behind orbit-angle-dependent shadow.
acceptance_criteria: directional light is camera child (not scene); ambient stays scene-level; local light position offset for form-defining shading; StlViewer test asserts the parenting; rotating preview keeps lit side facing camera; lint/typecheck/build/test green

title: [chore] Gitignore next-env.d.ts and tsconfig.tsbuildinfo to silence gt-pvx noise commits
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-1z2i
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-25T16:05:20Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

## Why

Two recent commits on stuff main are gt-pvx safety-net auto-saves containing only auto-generated build artifacts:

- \`43c0874\` (st-97h auto-save): \`next-env.d.ts\` + \`tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\` (1 line each)
- \`aa41f9d\` (st-qt4 auto-save): \`tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\` (1 line)

These files regenerate every \`pnpm dev\` / \`pnpm build\`, so polecats keep capturing them in safety nets. They're already tracked, so just adding the lines to \`.gitignore\` won't fully fix it.

## Scope

In the Caliper Next.js app's gitignore (the file at root \`.gitignore\` only has \`.next/\` per line 35; the relevant gitignore is likely under the Caliper subdirectory — check both):

1. Add to \`.gitignore\`:
   \`\`\`
   # Next.js auto-generated; regenerated on every dev/build
   next-env.d.ts
   *.tsbuildinfo
   \`\`\`
2. \`git rm --cached next-env.d.ts tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\` to untrack the existing tracked copies. Repo will keep the local files (they regenerate); just stops them from being committed.
3. Verify: \`git check-ignore next-env.d.ts tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\` should report both as ignored after the change.

Locate the right gitignore — there's a root \`.gitignore\` (35 lines) and likely an app-specific one under the Caliper directory. The TS files live in the app subdir, so probably both files exist or the app one needs the entries.

## Don't

- Don't add a sweeping \`*.d.ts\` ignore — only \`next-env.d.ts\` (others may be legitimate).
- Don't ignore \`.next/\` — already handled.
- Don't touch any source code.

## Verify

- [ ] Both files no longer show up in \`git status\` after a \`pnpm dev\` run.
- [ ] \`git check-ignore\` reports both as ignored.
- [ ] No future gt-pvx safety net captures them.
- [ ] Brief commit msg: \`chore: gitignore next-env.d.ts and tsbuildinfo (<bd-id>)\`.

## References

- \`43c0874\`, \`aa41f9d\` — the noise commits motivating this
- Existing \`.gitignore\` line 35: \`.next/\`

title: Chamfers + structural gusset for spraycan_holder_70mm_slot
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-uc7v
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T15:24:33Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Mayor had in-progress design work on models/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad that conflicts with st-6zw's top_band() change. Pick it up and land it on current main.

## Intent
Two related quality-of-life improvements:
1. **Outer/inner chamfers** — hand-feel on touchable edges, first-layer release on the drip-cup bottom, and a lead-in chamfer at the can-bore mouth.
2. **Structural gusset** — shallow trapezoidal fillet that widens the backer-to-ring bonded region. Must not intrude into the can bore (ring_id subtraction clears it).

## Key parameters (original values — adjust if obsidian's cradle_h math differs)
```
outer_chamfer      = 1.0;    // outer/touchable edges
inner_chamfer      = 0.5;    // can-entry lead-in (preserves ring wall)
gusset_back_w      = 28;     // width where gusset meets backer (mm)
gusset_front_w     = 10;     // width where gusset meets ring wall (mm)
gusset_depth       = 6;      // reach from backer into +Y (mm)
```

## Deliverables
- New `gusset()` module using `offset_sweep` from BOSL2/rounding.scad with a trapezoid (back_y = backer_thickness/2 - 0.05, front_y = backer_thickness/2 + gusset_depth) and top/bottom `os_chamfer`s. Height = cradle_h (cup_depth + ring_height); centered at cup_z0 + cradle_h/2.
- Ring and drip-cup using `cyl(...)` with `chamfer2` (top of ring) and `chamfer1` (bottom of cup) instead of plain `cylinder()`.
- Lead-in chamfer at the top of the ring's can bore (subtract a conical frustum, d1=ring_id, d2=ring_id + 2*inner_chamfer, height=inner_chamfer).
- Preserve obsidian's `top_band()` module from st-6zw unchanged — the features coexist.

## Conflict guidance
When I tried to 3-way apply the original WIP patch, the conflict was between obsidian's `top_band()` module (st-6zw, backer_height = slot_region_height + top_band_height) and the new `gusset()` module. Both are separate modules and should both live in the file.

## Verification
- Render the model (openscad -o /tmp/out.stl models/spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad) and confirm no errors.
- Visually check the gusset sits between backer and ring, doesn't cross ring_id.
- Confirm top_band solid cap from st-6zw still covers the slot-mouth domes.

## Original patch (for reference, may not apply cleanly)
Full diff from mayor's WIP: 55 insertions, 3 deletions. If needed, recreate from the specs above rather than forcing the patch.

title: Larger spraycan holder variant: 77mm diameter
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-q06p
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T15:55:49Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Once st-6zw lands (v2 backer-height fix), derive a 77mm-diameter variant of the multiconnect-slot holder for larger bottles.

## Source
Copy spraycan_holder_70mm_slot.scad → spraycan_holder_77mm_slot.scad (or refactor into a shared module parameterized on can_diameter — see Out of scope below).

## Required changes
- can_diameter = 77 (was 70)
- ring_id = can_diameter + 2*clearance = 78.5
- ring_od = ring_id + 2*wall = 84.5
- PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX X dimension: now max(backer_w=50, ring_od=84.5) = 84.5
- PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX Y dimension: backer_thickness(6.5) + ring_od(84.5) = 91
- PRINT_ANCHOR_BBOX Z: same as updated v2 (post-st-6zw)

## Things to verify
- Gusset geometry (gusset_back_w=28, gusset_front_w=10, gusset_depth=6) — may need to scale up so the larger ring is properly braced. The ring is heavier and extends further from the backer (~3.5mm more in +Y), so the cantilever moment is bigger. Suggest checking print-in-place stiffness of the gusset and potentially widening gusset_back_w to ~32.
- Drip cup volume scales naturally with ring_od; no explicit change needed unless the user wants more depth for larger drips (default cup_depth=5 stays fine).
- Slot count probably stays at 2 (spacing 25mm × 2 slots = 50mm backer width, narrower than the ring — that's fine and looks consistent with v2). Confirm by render.

## Out of scope
- Refactor into a single parameterized module: tempting (DRY), but risky if it breaks BOSL2 chamfer/anchor anchoring or changes existing v2 STL output. Better as a separate follow-up bead AFTER both 70mm and 77mm prints have shipped successfully. Just copy the file for now.

## Acceptance
- New file: models/spraycan_holder_77mm_slot.scad
- New render dir: renders/spraycan_holder_77mm_slot/
- New export: exports/spraycan_holder_77mm_slot.stl
- 77mm bottle slips into the ring with appropriate clearance (clearance=0.75 same as v2, validate this works for the larger diameter — may need 1.0 if the larger circumference makes the fit too tight)
- Multiconnect engagement unchanged (slot geometry inherited from v2 backer)

title: scad-lib add should check out pinned SHA from libs/README.md
description:
attached_molecule: st-wisp-hjhc
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T19:41:15Z
attached_args: Edit scripts/lib.py so that after cloning, if libs/README.md has an existing pinned SHA for the library name, run: git -C libs/<name> fetch --depth=1 origin <sha> && git -C libs/<name> checkout <sha>. Verify by removing libs/BOSL2 and re-running scad-lib add — confirm BOSL2 ends up at pinned SHA 456fcd8. Commit on stuff main directly.
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

scripts/lib.py add does a shallow clone of HEAD and records the resulting SHA. If libs/README.md already lists a pinned SHA for a library, the clone should check out that SHA instead. Relevant because st-kls discovered the BOSL2 pin must stay at 456fcd8 (2024-09-22) — any fresh clone of BOSL2 HEAD breaks QuackWorks' vector-spin usage. Today the pin is documentation-only. Proposed: after clone, if README has an existing pin for <name>, git -C libs/<name> fetch --depth=1 origin <sha> && git -C libs/<name> checkout <sha>. Verify by removing libs/BOSL2 and re-running scad-lib add with the README in place.
notes: --- 2026-04-18 MAYOR: Completed via mayor-intervention. Obsidian slung st-nuu at 17:25 UTC, started edit in scripts/lib.py at 19:42 UTC, then stalled for 1h+. Mayor implemented fix in stuff/mayor/rig, committed + pushed as e7742a6 on stuff main. Added _find_pinned_sha(name) helper and modified _cmd_add() to clone full history and checkout pinned SHA when one exists in libs/README.md. Unit tested BOSL2/NopSCADlib/threads-scad/unknown — all resolve correctly. Classic option-3 mayor-intervention case.
close_reason: Fixed via mayor-intervention (commit e7742a6 on stuff main); obsidian stalled after 1h on hook
